Every section: value vs cost

LifecycleRefineDiscoveryCritical-reviewTransitive

Each dot is one section of one file (sections under 8 weighted tokens omitted). Down and to the right = low value, high cost = trim first. Weighted tokens = raw tokens × load-frequency weight × dispatch multiplier, i.e. what actually lands in context windows, not bytes on disk. Hover any dot for detail.

Ranked trim candidates (260)

Ranked by weighted cost × (10 − value). Status meaning: VERIFIED survived adversarial refutation (pins, git provenance, behavioral walk) · PROVISIONAL · no pins found unverified by an agent but a mechanical repo-wide grep found zero anchors · PROVISIONAL · pin hits unverified and the grep found potential anchors — verify at execution · REFUTED · KEEP a verifier killed the claim (kept in table for transparency, excluded from savings) · NEEDS YOUR CALL hinges on a user preference. Anything you execute still passes your lifecycle critical-review gates.

skills/commit/SKILL.md
## Release-type markers (L58–78)
 LAZY_REF 4063,6544~3,288 VERIFIED
Move the release-type mechanics (marker regex, precedence, BREAKING: fallback, pre-merge --dry-run procedure) to skills/commit/references/release-type.md behind…
Move the release-type mechanics (marker regex, precedence, BREAKING: fallback, pre-merge --dry-run procedure) to skills/commit/references/release-type.md behind a 2-line pointer keeping the marker token + own-line rule, safe because the mechanics duplicate contracts owned by cortex_command/auto_bump_version.py:33-48, no test pins this prose, and only rare release-cut commits — not the ~9 builder loads per feature run — need the details.
Scorer pins: cortex_command/auto_bump_version.py:33-48 owns MARKER/BREAKING regexes; tests/test_cortex_auto_bump_version.py pins the helper not the prose; docs/release-process.md:124 references the marker independently
SURVIVES (high · batch) Section added with the auto-bump feature itself (f79089b2, 2026-05-14) and tightened alongside the helper regex (544bbab9, 2026-05-18 'Drop release-type: skip from auto-bump and tighten BREAKING regex') — provenance is feature-documentation, not an observed model failure, so no live-failure anchor blocks the move. Regexes at cortex_command/auto_bump_version.py:37-56. Grandfathered MUST ('marker MUST appear as the entire content of its own line', SKILL.md:65) moves with the section; no new escalation introduced. Weighted saving ~3.3k of 3654 tokens across ~9 loads/run for detail only needed at rare release cuts.
revised: Move release-type mechanics (regex display, precedence, BREAKING: fallback detail, --dry-run procedure) to skills/commit/references/release-type.md behind a pointer that keeps three things in the hot path: the two marker tokens, the own-line rule, and one clause that a column-0 BREAKING:/BREAKING CHANGE: line forces a major bump. That last clause is the one authoring-time hazard a commit author can trigger silently (auto-bump misfires are not hook-recoverable — wrong semver ships quietly), so it must not move out of context. Pointer must use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/references/release-type.md per ADR-0009.

## Architectural Constraints — backlog grep-c + bare-Python L201 + skill-dir SP001/SP002 (L44–46)
 COMPRESS 4371,7484~699 VERIFIED
Compress three lint-description bullets to gate-name + suppression-token + pointer lines because each violation is structurally caught at pre-commit (cortex-che…
Compress three lint-description bullets to gate-name + suppression-token + pointer lines because each violation is structurally caught at pre-commit (cortex-check-bare-python-import, cortex-check-skill-path, test_backlog_grep_targets_resolve.py) with self-explanatory failures, and line 46 duplicates CLAUDE.md's always-loaded skill-dir paragraph plus ADR-0009.
Scorer pins: tests/test_backlog_grep_targets_resolve.py; cortex_command/lint/bare_python_import.py + tests/test_bare_python_import_lint.py; cortex_command/lint/skill_path.py + tests/test_check_skill_path.py; cortex/adr/0009; CLAUDE.md skill-dir path-resolution paragraph (duplicate)
SURVIVES (high · batch) Line 46 duplicates the always-loaded CLAUDE.md skill-dir design-principle paragraph (same lint name, same ADR-0009 cite, same propagate-the-resolved-path rule). skill_path.py:1-46 and bare_python_import.py:21 self-document detectors and suppression sentinels; violations fail loudly at pre-commit, so a capable model recovers without the prose. tests/test_backlog_grep_targets_resolve.py structurally enforces line 44. No test greps project.md for any token in lines 44-46 (only Conditional Loading format is content-pinned, tests/test_load_requirements_cli.py:314-337).
revised: Compress lines 44-46 to gate-name + suppression-token + one-line intent + pointer each. Preconditions: (1) keep the literal tokens `cortex-check-bare-python-import`, `cortex-check-skill-path`, `<!-- bare-python-lint:ignore-next -->`, `<!-- skill-path-lint:ignore-next -->`, `tests/test_backlog_grep_targets_resolve.py`, and the ADR-0009 pointer so parity wiring and discoverability survive; (2) project.md is in cortex-check-contract scan scope (tests/test_check_contract.py:162), so the rewrite must not introduce new bare inline-code `cortex-<verb>` mentions that trip E101/E103; (3) keep the one-clause WHY for the backlog grep-c rule (prevents acceptance criteria passing against hallucinated event names) since no other prose home states it.

## Architectural Constraints — SKILL.md L1 surface ratchet + distributed-CLI dependency bounds (L47–48)
 COMPRESS 5712,2846~913 VERIFIED
Cut the 1.6KB L1-ratchet bullet to ~1/3 — keeping the heading verbatim (CLAUDE.md and test docstrings cite it by name) plus the two prose-only policies (re-cap-…
Cut the 1.6KB L1-ratchet bullet to ~1/3 — keeping the heading verbatim (CLAUDE.md and test docstrings cite it by name) plus the two prose-only policies (re-cap-with-rationale, cluster-promotion-by-reviewed-edit) — because budget rows, the non-cluster ≤400 assertion, cluster membership, and the completeness gate are all structurally enforced by tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py.
Scorer pins: CLAUDE.md pointer to 'SKILL.md L1 surface ratchet' by name; tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py:48,141,169; tests/test_skill_routing_disambiguation.py:ROUTING_PRESSURE_CLUSTER; cortex_command/dashboard/tests/test_routes_smoke.py (line 47 anti-revert guard)
SURVIVES (high · batch) CLAUDE.md names this constraint and delegates exactly two policies to it (cluster exemption, re-cap rule). tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py:53-56 restates the re-cap rule, :130-160 restates the 400B default + subtractive-set guard + promotion rule, :169 restates the re-cap rule again — the enforcement site is self-documenting. Cluster membership lives once in ROUTING_PRESSURE_CLUSTER. pyproject.toml:18-19 owns the starlette rationale. No test asserts project.md's body text beyond the heading token.
revised: Cut the line-48 L1-ratchet bullet to ~1/3, keeping (a) the heading token 'SKILL.md L1 surface ratchet' verbatim, (b) the cluster-exemption concept + promotion-by-reviewed-allowlist-edit rule, and (c) the re-cap-with-rationale-plus-lifecycle-id rule — CLAUDE.md points here BY NAME specifically 'for the cluster exemption and re-cap rule', so those two policies are the load-bearing prose; budget rows, the <=400 non-cluster default, the six cluster member names, and the completeness gate are all enforced and re-documented inside tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py (docstrings at lines 130-169 restate the policy). Line 47 (dependency bounds): compress the starlette example to a pointer — pyproject.toml:18-19 carries the FastAPI/starlette rationale inline — but keep the two prose-only policy clauses (uv-tool-install-ignores-uv.lock => requires-dist is the only universal governance; promote-transitive-to-direct-capped rule), which are not derivable from the test.

skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md
§1a Step v — Auto-enter sequence (+ fallback) (L123–152)
 COMPRESS 8734,1908~1,676 VERIFIED
The narration between test-pinned anchors (cache-clear enumeration, example diagnostic strings, the fallback paragraph's re-enumeration of failure modes) can sh…
The narration between test-pinned anchors (cache-clear enumeration, example diagnostic strings, the fallback paragraph's re-enumeration of failure modes) can shrink ~20% safely because tests/test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py and tests/test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py pin operation order and specific tokens ('EnterWorktree skipped', cortex-worktree-precondition, interactive_worktree_entered), not prose bulk.
Scorer pins: tests/test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py; tests/test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py; cortex/adr/0008-picker-selection-authorizes-enterworktree.md
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Verified by execution walk + boundary probe. (a) Block-boundary probe (python re on the test's own _BLOCK_PATTERN): pinned step-v block spans lines 123-148 only; the fallback paragraph (line 149) and scope note (line 151) are OUTSIDE it — 'Fallback in block: False', 'example diagnostics in block: False'. So the fallback prose is pinned only by test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py's ±60-line proximity window, which is redundantly satisfied (create_worktree at line 117, 'EnterWorktree skipped' at line 129 inside op 2, show-toplevel at line 153). (b) grep across cortex_command/, bin/, hooks/
revised: ~20% compression of §1a Step v narration is safe, with three named preconditions: (1) the fallback paragraph's silent-non-invocation clause ('the skill judges the gate unmet and declines to invoke the tool') must survive in meaning — it is spec-mandated (R10 of lifecycle-implement-auto-enter-worktree-via/spec.md), not test-pinned, and not derivable from ops 3/4, and its loss lets a silent skip emit interactive_worktree_entered from main-repo CWD and dispatch §2 from the wrong root; (2) do not introduce any new line-start '**'-bold text between the '**Step v — Auto-enter sequence**' heading and operation 5, since the ordering test's block regex terminates at the next line-start '**' (currently the '**Fallback' heading at line 149) — early truncation drops pinned tokens from the extracted block; (3) keep the full CLI command blocks (cortex-lifecycle-event log --event ... --set ..., cortex-worktree-resolve, cortex-worktree-precondition) intact to avoid contract-checker E101/E103 on inline cortex-* mentions. Given those, the compressible material (duplicate events-log rationale in intro vs op 5, cache-clear enumeration parenthetical, EnterWorktree error-mode examples, fallback diagnostic example strings, fallback condition re-enumeration of ops-3/4 triggers) totals ~80-90 of the section's 469 words with zero pin churn.
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) git log -S 'EnterWorktree skipped' -- skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md → d5578443 (2026-05-25, T10 of cortex/lifecycle/lifecycle-implement-auto-enter-worktree-via/: spec.md R9-R12 designed the five-operation order, the fallback marker, and the silent-non-invocation coverage from research.md F6/F8/F9); a79f9a34 (2026-06-03, ADR-0008: dropped the verify-worktree-auth probe — 'Option B writes no fence to verify' — making the fallback clause the only silent-non-invocation coverage); 333cd43b (2026-06-10 trim, ledger shows this section already adjudicated: order-rationale condensed, ADR-000
revised: COMPRESS stands but at ~10-15% (not ~20%) and only under two provenance preconditions: (1) the fallback paragraph's 'silent non-invocation' trigger (the skill judges the gate unmet and declines to invoke) must survive any rewrite verbatim in intent — it is the SOLE remaining runtime coverage of research finding F6 (model may not treat picker selection as 'explicit instruction') after ADR-0008/commit a79f9a34 deleted the upstream --verify-worktree-auth probe that originally detected that case; ADR-0008's rejected-alternative-2 text confirms the runtime fallback is the deliberate deterministic floor. (2) The cache-clear enumeration '(system prompt sections, memory files, plans directory)' was explicitly adjudicated 'applied-per-downgrade — kept as canonical statement; savings taken only in §vi' in trim commit 333cd43b (2026-06-10); §vi (line 153) now references 'EnterWorktree's cache-clear' without enumerating and leans on op 4 being canonical — cutting it re-litigates a decided trade for ~10 tokens and un-grounds §vi. Safe cuts with no failure-history value: the two example diagnostic strings (spec R10 acceptance itself declared 'specific failure-mode strings ... not required in the structural marker'), the fallback paragraph's re-statement of triggers 1-2 (ops 3 and 4 each already say 'route to the fallback'), the duplicated _resolve_user_project_root_from_cwd/events.log clause (line 125 intro vs line 147 op-5 note — accretion from d5578443 + f3248e04 #330 migration, not design), and op-4 trailing narration. Note the section is already once-trimmed residue: 333cd43b took 5 verified proposals in this exact region three weeks before this audit.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Verified extraction live: block = 2628 chars ending at '**Fallback' line-start boundary, token indices [323, 558, 1556, 2332] ordered, suppressed marker present. Anchors enumerated: tests/test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py (heading anchor, 4-token presence+order, suppressed-skip literal, verify-worktree-auth absence — all token-level, none pins narration bulk); tests/test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py (create_worktree + one of show-toplevel/git-common-dir/'EnterWorktree skipped' within ±60 lines of 'EnterWorktree(' — currently 19 lines apart, shrinking only reduces distances); tests/test
revised: COMPRESS ~20% is safe given these preconditions: (1) keep the line-start heading literal '**Step v — Auto-enter sequence**' and a subsequent line-start '**' boundary (block-extraction regex in tests/test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py); (2) keep first-occurrence order _origin_pwd → cortex-worktree-precondition → 'EnterWorktree(' → interactive_worktree_entered inside that block, and never introduce the open-paren form 'EnterWorktree(' before operation 2's precondition mention; (3) keep the exact literal 'EnterWorktree skipped: suppressed-picker' and do not reintroduce 'verify-worktree-auth'; (4) keep the fenced cortex-lifecycle-event invocation with --event/--feature/--set flags verbatim (contract lint E101/E103 + events-registry scanner both scan skills/**/*.md); (5) regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (dual-source drift pre-commit hook). The specific narration named by the scorer — cache-clear enumeration '(system prompt sections, memory files, plans directory)', the two example diagnostic strings in the fallback, the duplicated _resolve_user_project_root_from_cwd() rationale (lines 125 and 147), and the fallback trigger re-enumeration — is pinned by nothing; the fallback paragraph sits OUTSIDE the ordering test's extracted block (regex stops at the line-start '**Fallback' boundary), making it the least-pinned prose in the section. Condense the three fallback triggers, don't delete them: 'silent non-invocation' is the soft-gate coverage clause and its removal would weaken the event-emission guarantee without any test catching it.

cortex/lifecycle.config.md
### Normalization rules (L47–54)
 DELETE 2309201~828 VERIFIED
Delete the parser-mechanics narration (case-sensitivity, whitespace strip, dup-key last-wins, commented/invalid fall-through) because every rule is enforced by …
Delete the parser-mechanics narration (case-sensitivity, whitespace strip, dup-key last-wins, commented/invalid fall-through) because every rule is enforced by cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py and individually tested in tests/test_lifecycle_config.py, with typos degrading fail-safe to the picker plus a stderr warning — no consumer needs the prose to act correctly and no test pins this file's body.
Scorer pins: tests/test_lifecycle_config.py:11-13,119-172 (rules tested against code, not this file)
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Behavioral walk WITHOUT the section: (1) lifecycle start — SKILL.md:32 reads lifecycle.config.md for overrides; no decision consumes normalization semantics, frontmatter values still parse. (2) implement §1 preflight — skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:28-44 routes on `cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode .` + `cortex-lifecycle-picker-decision . {slug} {branch_mode}` JSON output; the model never validates the value itself. (3) operator typo (TRUNK/wurktree-interactive) — read_branch_mode returns raw string (lifecycle_config.py:94), should_fire_picker returns (True, 'branch_mode_unset_or_invalid
revised: Delete lines 47-54 (### Normalization rules) only. Every rule is structurally enforced and individually tested: whitespace-strip/dup-key-last-wins/commented-value-null live in cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py::read_branch_mode (tests/test_lifecycle_config.py cases 5-8, incl. explicit TRUNK case-sensitivity test at test_out_of_set_value_returns_raw_string); closed-set rejection lives in cortex_command/lifecycle_implement.py::_should_present_picker (_VALID_BRANCH_MODES -> branch_mode_unset_or_invalid -> picker). The executing model never parses this prose: implement.md routes via two CLI verbs (cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode + cortex-lifecycle-picker-decision) that return the decision as JSON, and implement.md:30 already narrates the unset/malformed->picker fallback inline. No test pins this repo-instance file body: the parity gate (tests/test_lifecycle_config_parity.py) compares asset<->init-template only, and the asset template does not contain this section at all. Bonus refutation-of-KEEP: the section is partially STALE — lines 49 and 53 claim 'a stderr warning names the rejected value', but no such warning exists anywhere in the runtime chain (read_branch_mode warns only on malformed YAML; picker_decision_cli.py and lifecycle_implement.py emit no stderr) — so keeping it preserves inaccurate docs. Preconditions: scope the delete to lines 47-54 only (Carve-outs section is conceptually cross-referenced by skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:19 and stays); operator fail-safe holds without prose (typo -> picker fires, never misroutes).
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) git log -L 47,54:cortex/lifecycle.config.md -> single commit d012511e ('Phase 1 of #249's schema + parser foundation; runtime short-circuit and parser primitive land in subsequent phases' — prose predates code). Spec provenance: cortex/lifecycle/lifecycle-implement-auto-enter-worktree-drop/spec.md R1 mandates documenting normalization semantics; its acceptance greps pin only '^## Branch Mode$' and 'branch-mode:', not the H3 subsection; spec line 5 shows #249's motivating failure was picker fatigue (#246 trunk sweep), unrelated to parsing. Feature events.log: 0 F-rows mentioning normalization.
revised: Delete the '### Normalization rules' section (lines 47-54). Provenance confirms it was design-first schema documentation, not a failure fix: it was written in its birth commit d012511e (2026-05-19, 'Phase 1 of #249's schema + parser foundation; the runtime short-circuit and parser primitive land in subsequent phases') BEFORE the parser existed, mandated by spec R1 of cortex/lifecycle/lifecycle-implement-auto-enter-worktree-drop/spec.md ('gains a ## Branch Mode section documenting ... the normalization semantics'). The motivating failure for #249 was picker fatigue (feature #246's 25-commit trunk sweep), not parsing confusion. No F-rows in the feature's events.log, no retro, no ADR ties this prose to an observed failure, and git log -L 47,54 shows no touch since birth. Runtime consumers route through the cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode verb (implement.md:28-33), never the body prose; malformed/typo values degrade fail-safe to the picker with a stderr warning naming the rejected value, so the system self-documents at the point of failure. Neither the plugin asset nor the init template carries this section, so every downstream repo has shipped without it since birth with zero recorded failures — direct field evidence the prose prevents nothing.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Anchor hunt found zero pins on the section body: (1) tests/test_lifecycle_config.py:18-19 builds tmp_path/cortex/lifecycle.config.md fixtures — all 8 normalization cases (dup last-wins :119-124, whitespace strip :137, commented->null :143-148, case/out-of-set fall-through :165-172) test the parser code, never this file; scorer pins confirmed accurate. (2) tests/test_lifecycle_config_parity.py:35-36 gates ASSET<->TEMPLATE frontmatter byte-slices only — the repo instance cortex/lifecycle.config.md is not a parity source, and grep shows 'Normalization' absent from both asset and template, so the
revised: Delete '### Normalization rules' (lines 47-54) from cortex/lifecycle.config.md only — the section is unique to this repo's instance (neither skills/lifecycle/assets/lifecycle.config.md nor cortex_command/init/templates/cortex/lifecycle.config.md contains it), so the ADR-0017 frontmatter parity gate and shipped consumer copies are untouched; every rule is enforced in cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py and tested against tmp_path fixtures, and no test, lint, hook, ADR citation, or skill cross-reference anchors to this section's prose.

cortex/requirements/project.md
## Architectural Constraints — historical compat shim + wheel-binstub invocation (L36–38)
 COMPRESS 4861,9446~777 VERIFIED
Compress the wheel-vs-working-tree bullet to the gotcha + CORTEX_COMMAND_FORCE_SOURCE remedy (~3 lines) and the compat-shim pattern to one line + metrics.py poi…
Compress the wheel-vs-working-tree bullet to the gotcha + CORTEX_COMMAND_FORCE_SOURCE remedy (~3 lines) and the compat-shim pattern to one line + metrics.py pointer, because the shim pattern including the exact docstring retitle is discoverable in cortex_command/pipeline/metrics.py and the template routes such operational detail to CLAUDE.md, keeping the dual-location TERMINAL_STATUSES contract intact.
Scorer pins: cortex_command/common.py:TERMINAL_STATUSES + cortex_command/overnight/plan.py:_TERMINAL; cortex_command/pipeline/metrics.py compat docstrings; CORTEX_COMMAND_FORCE_SOURCE in bin/cortex-* wrappers + cortex_command/lint/contract.py
SURVIVES (medium · batch) metrics.py:367/396 carry the retitled compat docstring verbatim, so line 37's format detail is code-discoverable; but no test enforces shim retention — the policy sentence is the sole guard and must survive compression. grep shows CORTEX_COMMAND_FORCE_SOURCE appears only in bin/ wrapper comments and code, nowhere in docs/ or CLAUDE.md, so line 38's remedy clause is the primary prose home. Both lines 37-38 landed via #246 review drift (commit a1b25507) for an observed failure class that is still live (module deletions and wheel staleness both recurred since).
revised: Line 36 (TERMINAL_STATUSES dual-location contract) stays intact per the claim. Line 37: compress to the policy sentence ('deleted pipeline modules keep their read-side schema filters as historical compat shims; retitle docstrings — pattern at pipeline/metrics.py:_DAYTIME_DISPATCH_FIELDS') — the exact docstring wording is discoverable at metrics.py:367/396 and the pattern is prose-only policy, so the one-line form must survive (it is the ONLY enforcement; there is no test pinning shim retention). Line 38: compress to gotcha + remedy, keeping three facts verbatim: binstubs read the installed wheel not the working tree; python3 -m runs the working tree; CORTEX_COMMAND_FORCE_SOURCE=1 forces working-tree execution in the bin/cortex-* wrappers. This bullet is operationally load-bearing during lifecycle implement phases (the wheel-vs-tree staleness gotcha recurs in session history), and project.md is its main prose home — the mechanism is otherwise documented only in bin wrapper header comments.
8

Tighten the trunk/feature-branch bullets (lines 85-86) but do NOT delete them outright: line 19's recorded-choice path routes by reference to 'the identical post-selection routing', so the Dispatch-by-selection mapping must remain present in compressed form; keep the worktree bullet with Steps A/B intact including the 'record entry mode `selected`' marker (S1a step v branches on it), both 'bash -s --' sidecar lines (pinned at exactly 2 by test_sidecar_invocation_form_bash_s_count), and the 'cortex-interactive-lock acquire' token (pinned by test_interactive_lock_acquire_in_worktree_dispatch_block).

The five-case merge-back procedure is explicitly skipped entirely for sequential dispatch and the dispatch mode is decided in §1 before any batch runs, so it can move to a worktree-dispatch-gated reference read only on that branch — no test pins its heading or body, and a lazy ref (unlike a verb) does not re-enter the killed #336 offload hunting ground.

No F-row, retro, debug note, or ADR records a merge-back failure: grep of cortex/lifecycle/*/events.log for worktree+fail/conflict/error and cortex/debug/ for mer
revised: The five-case merge-back body (lines 196-205) can move to a worktree-dispatch-gated sibling reference, PROVIDED the extraction follows the #341 competing-plans playbook: keep the '**e. Worktree Integration**: Skip this step entirely for sequential dispatch' stub line inline at §2e (implement.md:151 cross-references '§2(e) merge-back applies unchanged' and depends on the step existing), add the new target to lifecycle SKILL.md's Reference-path propagation manifest with a condition+read-verb+target trigger line (satisfies the cortex-check-skill-path lint, mitigates the Anthropic 'one level deep' nesting caution the same way plan.md->competing-plans.md does), ship a small wiring test mirroring tests/test_competing_plans_wired.py, and commit the new tracked .md with explicit git add plus regenerated plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py crashes on uncommitted moved/new refs).
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Simulated both arms. Sequential arm (dominant: ~15 recent lifecycles in memory all ran sequential/trunk; archived lifecycle 'remove-single-agent-worktree-dispatch-and-flip-recommended-default-to-current-branch' flipped the default away from worktree dispatch): stub's skip line makes behavior byte-identical, tokens saved. Worktree arm (rare but live — parallel-execution.md still documents Agent(isolation:worktree); §2d keeps the worktree checkpoint arm): trigger sits at the exact branch point where the orchestrator just ran the mode-specific §2d checkpoint, so a frontier model reads the target
revised: LAZY_REF is behaviorally safe given three named preconditions: (1) the stub keeps §2e's identity and inlines the 'skip entirely for sequential (non-worktree) dispatch' arm, so the dominant path needs no read and §1a's '§2(e) merge-back applies unchanged' cross-ref (implement.md:151) stays valid; (2) the extracted file is registered as a named target in lifecycle SKILL.md's Reference-path propagation manifest (competing-plans precedent, ADR-0009 / cortex-check-skill-path lint) and the worktree arm's trigger reads the body-resolved target; (3) the new file is git-added in the same commit as the edit plus mirror regen (test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py FileNotFoundError gotcha; drift hook forces canonical+mirror together). One claim correction: per-task isolation is not literally decided in §1 (§1 decides branch mode; the orchestrator picks isolation at §2b dispatch), but by §2e the mode is a known, already-acted-on fact — §2d's checkpoint is already mode-specific — so the read trigger is deterministic regardless.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Exhaustive anchor hunt found NO enforcement pin on the section body. (1) Tests: grep of tests/ for 'Worktree Integration', 'merge worktree', 'worktree/{task-name}', 'git branch -d', 'cortex-worktree-resolve' hits only PATH-bootstrap/hook tests (tests/test_hooks.sh, tests/test_session_start_path_bootstrap.sh) that shim the resolver binary — none read implement.md. All implement.md-pinning tests (tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py, tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py, tests/test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py, tests/test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py) pin §1/§1a
revised: The five-case merge-back body (implement.md:196-205) can move to a worktree-dispatch-gated lazy ref, PROVIDED: (1) the '**e. Worktree Integration**' heading and the 'Skip this step entirely for sequential (non-worktree) dispatch' gate line stay inline at the same position so the in-file cross-ref at implement.md:151 ('§2(e) merge-back applies unchanged') keeps a live target; (2) the pointer uses the SP002-exempt form ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/references/<new>.md (precedent: implement.md:68,106 sidecar reads + SKILL.md Reference-path propagation manifest); (3) the new file is never composed into a subagent prompt (the orchestrator performs merges itself, so SP001 fence rules don't apply); (4) canonical + regenerated plugins/cortex-core mirror commit together per the drift hook. One scorer inaccuracy corrected: §1 decides BRANCH mode, not per-task dispatch mode — worktree-vs-sequential dispatch surfaces at §2b/§2d and is not structurally recorded in §1; the gate condition for the lazy read is 'this batch was dispatched via Agent(isolation: worktree)', which the orchestrator trivially knows at §2e.

SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Zero live enforcement pins: no tests/ hit (test greps for 'uncommitted' all target complete-route/gc-worktrees, not this prose), no ADR citation, no hook reader, no overnight-pipeline reader (cortex/research/vertical-planning/audit.md:199 confirms the guard is harness-level, absent from the pipeline module), no cortex-* inline command for cortex-check-contract, no ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} for cortex-check-skill-path. The only greppable pins are one-time acceptance criteria in COMPLETED lifecycle features (both feature_complete per events.log): cortex/lifecycle/archive/add-uncommitted-changes-guard-
revised: COMPRESS is safe with named preconditions: keep both exact literals verbatim exactly once each (`Warning: uncommitted changes in working tree — this will mix them into the commit on main.` and `uncommitted-changes guard skipped: git status failed`), keep the demote-in-place semantics as an explicit instruction (prefix warning to the current-branch option's description, drop `(recommended)`, option stays selectable — no removal/blocking), keep the trigger (`git status --porcelain` immediately before AskUserQuestion) and the fail-open policy, keep the bolded run-in heading `**Uncommitted-changes guard**` (lines 28/44/48 of the same file reference it by name), and regenerate the plugin mirror (`just build-plugin`) in the same commit per the drift hook. What is droppable: the (a)/(b) labels, '(no path filter, no additional flags)', 'stays at its existing position — no removal, no gating pre-question' (compress to 'stays selectable in place'), the parenthetical git-failure examples, and the redundant 'neither the demotion nor the warning prefix are applied' restatement. Note the prose is genuinely load-bearing, not redundant with the module: should_fire_picker (cortex_command/lifecycle_implement.py) is first-match-wins, so on the common branch_mode-unset path its dirty_tree check never runs and this paragraph is the ONLY dirty-tree warning — semantics must survive compression intact.
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) Provenance refutes nothing: (1) Origin commit 8af5a14b (2026-04-21, lifecycle #096, epic #093) was a PROACTIVE guard ahead of #097's default-flip — not a fix for an observed failure; the clarify_critic row in cortex/lifecycle/archive/add-uncommitted-changes-guard-to-lifecycle-implement-phase-pre-flight/events.log explicitly records 'zero trunk-safety incidents in the retro corpus'. No F-row, no retro mention (grep of cortex/retros + all events.log = 0 hits). (2) The (a)/(b) choreography exists because spec #096 R2 used grep-witness acceptance criteria ('prefix', '(recommended)', 'If non-empty'
revised: COMPRESS is safe given three named preconditions: keep BOTH literals verbatim (the warning `Warning: uncommitted changes in working tree — this will mix them into the commit on main.` and the skip diagnostic `uncommitted-changes guard skipped: git status failed`), and keep one clause stating the non-blocking disposition (demote in place — still selectable, not removed, no gating pre-question), because the repo's sibling porcelain-check precedents (skills/pr, skills/overnight) are BLOCKING and demote-not-block is this guard's one deliberate divergence a pattern-matching model could get wrong. The (a)/(b) prepend/strip/position choreography can go.
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Simulated execution without the choreography: (1) dirty-tree-on-main path still demotes+warns because 'demote in place' + the warning literal carry the semantics; (2) fail-open path kept per claim; (3) ordering preserved by the runtime probe's own 'After the uncommitted-changes guard' anchor at implement.md:48; (4) tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py's block regex terminates at '**Branch-mode dispatch preflight**' (implement.md:28), before the guard, so no live test breaks. Refutation attempts that failed: cortex/lifecycle/consolidate-implementmd-branch-worktree-dispatch-and/spe
revised: COMPRESS is safe given named preconditions: the two-sentence rewrite keeps (1) the '**Uncommitted-changes guard**:' heading token (referenced by implement.md:44 fall-through routing and :48 runtime-probe ordering anchor), (2) the 'git status --porcelain' trigger (should_fire_picker is first-match-wins, so on the common branch_mode_unset_or_invalid path this prose run is the ONLY dirty check), (3) 'demote in place' plus a brief strip-'(recommended)' mention (classified a preserved control mechanic by cortex/lifecycle/consolidate-implementmd-branch-worktree-dispatch-and/spec.md Req 8, APPROVED 2026-06-29; the code's dirty_tree fire reason at cortex_command/lifecycle_implement.py:135 exists solely to route into this prose), and (4) both fixed literals. What can go: the (a)/(b) list formatting, 'no path filter, no additional flags', the git-failure examples parenthetical, 'no removal, no gating pre-question' (derivable from 'in place'), and 'continues normally as a fallback'. Scorer's 'unpinned' is wrong in the soft sense: no LIVE CI test greps this paragraph, but the literals are pinned by archived #096 acceptance greps and cited verbatim by two other completed lifecycle specs, so keep them byte-identical.

revised: Delete the two worked examples from the hot path; optionally fold them into the references/release-type.md file created by s7 (they cost nothing there). Only the option-list re-enumerations in the exit-0/exit-1 bullets and the illustrative Bash-failure examples are cut. Realistic saving is ~40% of the section, not quite half, because the routing policy and literal diagnostic are non-derivable and incompressible. Mirror at plugins/cortex-core must regenerate via build-plugin in the same commit (drift hook).
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) Origin: commit 6cdb5706 (2026-04-24) added the probe for feature lifecycle-skill-gracefully-degrades-autonomous-worktree-option-when-runner-absent — graceful degradation for consumer installs missing the console-script, an architecture-driven concern still live under uv-tool distribution (refutes DELETE, not COMPRESS). The origin spec's Requirements 5/6 hardened the exit-0/1/other routing after critical review; those grep-based acceptance pins targeted a python3-importlib probe form that commit a9b7a21a (2026-05-26) wholly replaced with `command -v`, rewriting the disposition prose — the secti
revised: COMPRESS is safe with four named preconditions: (1) keep the fenced `command -v cortex-worktree-create >/dev/null 2>&1` block in §1 (test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py extracts the binary via regex `command -v (\S+)` and asserts parity with the §1a-iii invocation); (2) preserve all three disposition SEMANTICS in one line each — exit 0 → options unchanged; exit 1 → silently hide the worktree option, no diagnostic; Bash failure or exit ≠ 0/1 → fail open with the literal diagnostic `runtime probe skipped: console-script probe failed` — because these semantics were critical-review-hardened in the origin spec (exit-1 collision ambiguity was one of six fix-invalidating objections) and plan.md:182 lazy-refs §1 as their canonical statement; (3) compress IN PLACE — do not extract to a shared reference (commit 47269601 tried exactly that and 30905d17 reverted it because content-pinning tests require command -v self-contained in §1); (4) any surviving prose mention of the binary stays in the fenced block, not bare inline code (contract-checker E101/E103). What compresses: the option-label re-enumerations in the exit-0 and exit-1 bullets (labels are pinned only inside the **Branch selection** block at lines 22-27, which test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py bounds at the next `**` heading — the probe's copies are unpinned) and the parenthetical enumeration of Bash-failure kinds.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Every enforcement anchor either targets content the compression preserves or lies outside lines 48-61. (2) tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_w
revised: Collapse each exit-code disposition to one line, preserving (a) the fenced `command -v cortex-worktree-create >/dev/null 2>&1` block verbatim, (b) the exit-1 hide-worktree-option semantic, and (c) the literal fail-open diagnostic string `runtime probe skipped: console-script probe failed`. Safe given three preconditions: do not convert the fenced probe into a bare inline-code invocation-shaped mention (contract lint E101/E103 exempts only probe-context per _PROBE_HEAD_RE); do not touch the binary name (parity with the §1a-iii invocation is test-pinned); regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (drift hook).

SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) (1) Provenance of closing paragraph (line 121): commit 1e9c01f6 (2026-06-29, 'Remove implement.md worktree pre-flight heredoc, dash re-resolve step v') deleted the 33-line python3 containment heredoc (old step iv) and added this paragraph as its successor note. The failure mode it guarded (worktree escaping repo root via stale CORTEX_WORKTREE_ROOT) is now structurally enforced inside create_worktree at cortex_command/pipeline/worktree.py:238 (worktree_escapes_repo), gating every return path per R2 of cortex/lifecycle/consolidate-implementmd-branch-worktree-dispatch-and/spec.md, with discrimina
revised: COMPRESS survives, but the scorer's safety reasoning is wrong on a load-bearing point: the pin inventory missed tests/test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py, which requires the literal `create_worktree` token within ±60 lines of the `EnterWorktree(` call site (implement.md:136). Lines 117 and 121 are the ONLY carriers of that token in the whole file (the code block's `cortex-worktree-create` does not contain the substring). Cutting both prose blocks as claimed fails test_callsite_has_create_worktree_within_proximity. Safe compression precondition: the compressed text must retain (a) one prose occurrence of the literal `create_worktree` token, and preferably (b) a one-clause containment guarantee (exits 1 with `worktree_escapes_repo`; no pre-flight needed) to block a helpful re-addition of the deleted heredoc, and (c) the stdout=path / stderr=informational contract since the invocation captures via $(...). Everything else — the settings-copy/.venv-symlink description (enforced by the tool's own integration test) and the historical 'no longer a separate pre-flight step' framing (successor note for the heredoc deleted in 1e9c01f6, failure mode now enforced at worktree.py:238 with discriminating tests) — cuts safely. Example compressed form (~45 tokens): '`create_worktree` resolves the branch as `interactive/{slug}`, materializes `<repo>/.claude/worktrees/interactive-{slug}/`, and enforces inside-repo containment (exit 1, `worktree_escapes_repo`) — no separate pre-flight needed. Stdout = absolute worktree path; stderr = informational. On failure surface stderr and exit §1a.'
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Anchor hunt results: (a) tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py:112-138 — the only prose pin on §1a step iii; needs the '**iii.' marker and the '--feature interactive-' invocation line, both retained under COMPRESS; lines 117/119/121 are not matched by any assertion. (b) tests/test_implement_option2_worktree_creation.py:101-152 — pins settings.local.json copy and .venv symlink against create_worktree's runtime behavior in a tmp repo; it never reads implement.md, so cutting the prose leaves the behavior fully tested. (c) Containment: worktree.py:238 raises 'worktree_escapes_repo'
revised: COMPRESS is safe with three preconditions: (1) keep the '**iii.' bold marker and the fenced 'worktree_path=$(cortex-worktree-create --feature interactive-{slug} --base-branch main)' line verbatim — the binary-parity contract test (tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py:112-138) slices §1a at the '**iii.' marker and regex-matches '^(?:\w+=\$\()?(\S+?)\s+--feature\s+interactive-'; (2) keep line 119's failure-handling instruction (exit-1 => surface stderr, exit §1a, no handoff) — it is the agent's only branch-on-failure directive; (3) regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror via build-plugin and commit canonical+mirror together (drift pre-commit hook). Line 117's facts are redundant (interactive/{slug} branch + worktree path already stated at implement.md:25; stdout/stderr contract documented in worktree_create_cli.py docstring and enforced by test_implement_option2_worktree_creation.py against the implementation, not the prose). Line 121's containment paragraph is enforced at worktree.py:238, pinned by the test_containment_* block in tests/test_worktree.py, and recorded as an Architectural Constraint at cortex/requirements/project.md:50 — no test greps implement.md for containment tokens, and the 'why no pre-flight here' rationale survives in project.md, satisfying the CLAUDE.md document-the-removed-boundary guideline.

### 4. Transition (L259–279)
 COMPRESS 3081,4787~591 VERIFIED

The 8-cell review-gating matrix is a drift-prone duplicate of cortex_command/common.py:requires_review (all cells tested in tests/test_common_utils.py) and can be replaced by a one-line rule plus code citation, and the proceed-automatically paragraph tightens around its kept-pauses pointer; the roundtrip-pinned phase_transition line with its tier/from/to keys must stay verbatim.
Scorer pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py (phase_transition count + tier/from/to key set); cortex_command/common.py:requires_review; tests/test_common_utils.py; skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) git log -L 259,279 shows the matrix unchanged since the initial commit 428e54ea (git log -S 'Review gating matrix' hits only that commit) — no failure artifact behind the table form. Prior trim 333cd43b (harness-token-efficiency-trim, 2026-06-10) removed the matrix's prose restatement while keeping the table, establishing the pair as duplicative encodings. criticality-matrix.md:17-22 encodes the identical rule ('Tier-based (skip for simple)' / 'Forced regardless of tier'); common.py:846 requires_review is one line ('tier == "complex" or criticality in ("high", "critical")') with all 8 cells pi
revised: Matrix compression is provenance-safe: the 8-cell table dates to the initial commit (design, not failure-fix), the rule is triply encoded (implement.md table; criticality-matrix.md §Criticality Behavior Matrix 'Review phase' column already cited in the same sentence; common.py:846 requires_review with all 8 cells tested), and a prior trim (333cd43b) already treated the table+prose pair as duplicative. Replace with a one-line rule ('to=review iff tier==complex OR criticality in {high,critical}, else complete') citing cortex_command/common.py:requires_review as the drift anchor. PRECONDITION on the proceed-automatically paragraph: f7a52cc6 (2026-05-20, current-model era) built it as the named 'runtime backstop' for an OBSERVED boundary-ask failure that no structural gate prevents; tightening must retain (a) the no-ask directive, (b) the 'fires on gate conditions, not user input' diagnosis, and (c) the kept-pauses/SKILL.md §Phase Transition pointer — only redundancy with SKILL.md:164-187 may be cut, so savings there are small. The cortex-lifecycle-event verb line (tier/from/to) and the cortex-lifecycle-state criticality-read line stay verbatim.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Anchors hunted, none refute: (a) tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:100-141 — CANONICAL allows phase_transition key set {tier,from,to} 'the implement.md §4 row carries a preserved tier' and FILE_EVENTS pins implement.md to phase_transition:2 + batch_dispatch:1; claim already keeps the bash line verbatim, test unaffected (verified 35 passed baseline). (b) cortex_command/common.py:846 requires_review encodes the identical matrix ('return tier == "complex" or criticality in ("high", "critical")'); tests/test_common_utils.py:219-240 tests all 8 cells; archived spec wire-review-phase-into-over
revised: COMPRESS is safe given four named preconditions: (1) the §4 `cortex-lifecycle-event log --event phase_transition --set tier=... --set from=implement --set to=...` line stays verbatim — tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py pins implement.md to exactly 2 phase_transition invocations and the {tier,from,to} key set; (2) the `cortex-lifecycle-state --feature {feature} --field criticality` invocation keeps its flag shape (cortex-check-contract E101/E103 scans inline cortex-* invocations); (3) the requires_review citation is a prose path cite (cortex_command/common.py:requires_review), NOT a bare `python -c` import — tests/test_bare_python_import_lint.py L201 lint scans implement.md specifically; (4) the SKILL.md §Phase Transition pointer in the proceed-automatically sentence is kept. The 8-cell matrix itself has zero structural pins: no test greps the table rows, the code (requires_review: tier=='complex' or criticality in ('high','critical')) is the cross-surface canonical already cited by morning-review walkthrough.md:243, and all 8 cells are tested only in tests/test_common_utils.py. Replacing the table with the equivalent 2-clause rule + code citation reduces silent-drift surface (no test cross-validates the prose table against the code today). Mirror regen (plugins/cortex-core) in the same commit is the only combined cost, ~0.
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Behavioral walk with the compressed section: the matrix is a pure 2-input boolean and cortex_command/common.py:846-862 requires_review encodes it exactly (tier == 'complex' or criticality in ('high','critical')); every cell of the table maps to that one line, so a frontier model applying the inline boolean reaches the identical to=review|complete decision on all 8 branches — the bolded surprising cells (simple+high/critical -> Review) are carried more explicitly by the boolean than by the table. The gating is unified-by-design, not coincidentally duplicated: skills/morning-review/references/wa
revised: COMPRESS is safe given four preconditions: (1) keep both fenced cortex-lifecycle-event bash blocks verbatim — tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py pins implement.md at exactly 2 phase_transition invocations and the §4 row's {tier,from,to} key set (lines 104-106, 139-141); (2) replace the 8-cell table with the explicit boolean, not just a citation: 'Review if tier is complex OR criticality is high/critical; else Complete — encoded in cortex_command/common.py:requires_review, all 8 cells tested in tests/test_common_utils.py'; (3) keep the cortex-lifecycle-state --feature --field criticality read line with its flags (contract checker scopes implement.md, test_check_contract.py:146); (4) the tightened proceed-automatically sentence must retain 'do not ask the user' plus the SKILL.md §Phase Transition pointer, since CLAUDE.md requires removed-boundary reasoning to stay documented.

skills/commit/SKILL.md
## Commit Message Format (L19–40)
 COMPRESS 1201,0806~432 VERIFIED
Drop the derivable format template block and halve the bullet rules, safe because hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh structurally enforces capital/no-period/impera…
Drop the derivable format template block and halve the bullet rules, safe because hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh structurally enforces capital/no-period/imperative/blank-line and denies with the reason on violation — keep only the non-enforced judgment lines (summarize the why, skip body when self-evident, 72-char wrap).
Scorer pins: hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh:57-88 enforces the same four rules structurally
SURVIVES (high · batch) Section is initial-commit provenance (428e54ea, 2026-04-01) — pre-hook-maturity boilerplate, no observed-failure anchor. Behavioral walk-through without the section: a frontier model produces conventional imperative git messages by default; any structural miss is denied by the PreToolUse hook with the exact reason, and the retained judgment lines cover everything the hook is silent on (why-not-what, wrap, body-skip). Saving is partial (~60-70 of 120 tokens x9 loads) but the change is safe with the imperative-mood precondition named above.
revised: Drop the derivable template block and the hook-duplicated structural bullets (capital opener, no trailing period, blank line before body), keeping the judgment lines the hook cannot check: summarize the why not the what, 72-char wrap, skip body for self-evident changes — PLUS keep 'imperative mood' in the retained line, because the hook's check is only a past-tense-opener blacklist (added|fixed|removed|...) and does not catch third-person forms like 'Adds foo', so imperative is partially enforced, not fully.

§1 Branch selection — fallback picker options (L22–27)
 LAZY_REF 2971,4267~1,283 VERIFIEDre-proposes killed idea
Since ADR-0012 made the plan-time recorded choice the primary path, the picker option prose fires only on the no-recorded-choice fallback and can move to a gate…
Since ADR-0012 made the plan-time recorded choice the primary path, the picker option prose fires only on the no-recorded-choice fallback and can move to a gated reference — countering the kill: the 30905d17 revert happened because the extraction moved prose while leaving the three content-pinning tests (picker labels, should_fire_picker, command -v) anchored to §1, so a re-extraction must relocate those test anchors in the same commit, which this audit's rules explicitly permit.
Scorer pins: tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py; tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py
SURVIVES (medium · enforcement-and-pins) Baseline: all 23 tests in test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py, test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py, test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py, test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py, test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py pass. Pin-by-pin: (a) test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py hard-anchors '**Branch selection**'→next '**' block in implement.md and requires a '- **label**' item containing 'worktree' — breaks on extraction, protection (ADR-0008: live EnterWorktree schema requires user-direct 'worktree' mention; the label IS the authorizing instruction) is location-indep
revised: LAZY_REF of implement.md lines 22-27 survives the enforcement lens, but only with a wider same-commit precondition set than the claim names, and one premise correction. Premise fix: the option prose is NOT fallback-only — plan.md §4 (the ADR-0012 primary path, plan.md:175/182/186) treats Implement §1 as the authoritative option-set source when assembling the merged approval surface for every main/master feature, so the extraction must retarget that authority pointer to the new reference. Scope note in the claim is over-cautious the other way: the 30905d17 revert's two heaviest pins (should_fire_picker wiring in test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py, command -v gate in test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py) anchor to lines 28-58 which STAY under the 22-27 scope — they need no relocation. Required same-commit set: (1) repoint tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py IMPLEMENT_MD + block anchor to the new reference (its ADR-0008 EnterWorktree 'worktree'-token protection is location-independent and survives repointing intact); (2) kept-pauses parity direction 2 (test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py scans ALL .md under skills/lifecycle/ for AskUserQuestion tokens) — either keep the 'prompt the user via AskUserQuestion' sentence in implement.md and move only the three bullets, or add a kept-pauses.md inventory entry for the new file; (3) HIDDEN ANCHOR the scorer missed: test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py fails on bare-prose stale citations — u2-decisions.md:64-65 cite implement.md:282/:283 bare (square brackets, not backticks) against the current 283-line file, so any net shrink >1 line flips them to 'stale'; backtick or renumber those two lines same-commit; (4) plan.md:175/182 pointer retarget; (5) plugins/cortex-core mirror regen same-commit per drift hook; (6) new reference must use the body-resolved propagated-path pattern (ADR-0009 / cortex-check-skill-path) — implement.md line 68/71 sidecar is the in-file template. Verified non-anchors: test_menu_label_present_in_section_1 still passes post-move (label remains in §1 at probe dispositions lines 56-57 and dispatch-by-selection line 63); test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py requires open-paren EnterWorktree( so line 25's prose mention doesn't register; kept-pauses implement.md:37 anchor absorbs the -6 shift within ±35 tolerance; ADR-0008/0012 cite implement.md prose-only without line pins; overnight pipeline has its own separate cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/implement.md with zero picker content. Value caveat for the synthesizer (not a refutation on this lens): all three labels remain in implement.md regardless (runtime-probe disposition list + dispatch-by-selection), so the extraction moves only ~5 lines of description/when-to-pick prose (~300 tokens) at the cost of a ~7-file commit.
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) Provenance refutes the kill, not the claim. (1) The revert was expedient, not doctrinal: extraction commit 47269601 ('No behavior change') touched zero tests; revert 30905d17's message says the full suite 'revealed it broke implement.md content-pinning tests' — a mechanical anchor failure, exactly as the claim states. (2) Decisive: ADR-0012 (accepted, committed IN the revert commit 30905d17 itself) still records the extraction as the design — lines 46-50: 'The branch-option decision logic is extracted into a shared reference (skills/lifecycle/references/branch-picker.md) consulted by both phas
revised: Extract lines 22-27 into a shared branch-picker-options reference — but as the two-phase shared reference ADR-0012 already records as the accepted design, NOT as a fallback-only gated ref: plan.md sec 4 (lines 175-186) consumes the option set and description-demotion rule on every main-branch plan approval (the PRIMARY path), so the reference loads at Plan approval too and the 'fires only on the no-recorded-choice fallback' savings premise overstates the win. Preconditions in the same commit: (1) re-anchor tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py's **Branch selection** block pattern to the new file; (2) update plan.md:175/182's 'see Implement sec 1' pointer to the new reference; (3) regen the plugins/cortex-core mirror (drift hook); (4) verify kept-pauses.md:19's implement.md:37 anchor stays within the parity test's +/-20-line tolerance after the ~4-line shift. tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py and tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py need NO changes — their anchors (label at implement.md:56/57/63, should_fire_picker at 28-44, command -v at 48-58) are outside the candidate range.
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Behavioral walk without the section inline: (a) primary path — implement.md:19 fully specifies post-selection routing for a recorded choice ('Treat the value exactly as if the operator had selected that option') without needing option descriptions; (b) branch-mode suppressed path (lines 37-43) never assembles the picker; (c) picker-fires path has a crisp structural read-trigger (explicit fall-through at lines 20/42/44), and all three labels plus per-selection routing are duplicated in the surviving prose (lines 56-58, 62-88), so only the When-to-pick descriptions, '(recommended)' marker, and t
revised: Lines 22-27 (picker option labels + When-to-pick descriptions) can move to a gated sibling reference, safe under four named preconditions: (1) the implement.md stub keeps the AskUserQuestion token and an explicit read-trigger at the fall-through point BEFORE the uncommitted-changes guard (the guard mutates option descriptions and strips '(recommended)', so options must be loaded first), and the new ref must NOT contain the literal 'AskUserQuestion' or kept-pauses parity rule 2 (which rglobs all skills/lifecycle/**/*.md) demands a new inventory entry; (2) tests/test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py is re-anchored to the new ref in the same commit — it is the ONLY pin inside the block, and its protection (EnterWorktree authorization requires 'worktree' in the user-facing label, ADR-0008) is location-independent so relocation preserves it fully; (3) the ref read uses the established sidecar-style body-resolved path propagation already present in implement.md; (4) mirror regen in the same commit per the drift hook. Notably cheaper than the scorer feared: test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py passes UNCHANGED because the label literal 'Implement on feature branch with worktree' remains in section 1 at the runtime-probe dispositions (lines 56-57) and dispatch-by-selection (line 63), and the command -v / should_fire_picker / sidecar content all live outside lines 22-27.

revised: Move the four-value descriptions (lines 33-39) to the lazily-read companion doc, with one precondition: the remaining Branch Mode intro (lines 29-31) currently says 'closed-set values below' and must be rewritten to name the doc path, otherwise it dangles. Step 2 is orchestrator-side and never rides into searcher prompts.
revised: Reduce Step 2 to the pointer sentence plus ONE retained clause: keep a one-line 'upper bound on investigation breadth, not a quota — dispatch fewer if fewer distinct angles' rider (matching discovery's deliberately-condensed sibling at skills/discovery/references/research.md:43, which kept exactly that clause when it went pointer-only), and delete the floor/corner/monotonic restatement. Keep the ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/references/fanout.md link (skill-path lint).

No test pins lines 153-156: the step-v ordering regex (test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py) ends at the ^** boundary '**Fallback' at line 149, and the callsites proximity test (test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py) is satisfied by tokens at lines 117/149 independent of §vi.

revised: COMPRESS survives only at sharply reduced scope (~20-25 tokens, not ~60), under a hard precondition: preserve (1) the suppressed-path delta — no EnterWorktree cache-clear, NO session-exit keep/remove prompt, cd-only restoration — because the opening sentence is NOT restatement of step-v operation 2 (op-2 carries routing only, never restoration/prompt semantics; the sentence was added by a79f9a34 as the operational surface of ADR-0008's accepted degradation, current architecture); and (2) both mid-session restoration paths (ExitWorktree action="keep" vs cd), which are ADR-0004 Decision-1/R13 load-bearing (tool schema forbids proactive ExitWorktree; complete.md's twin of this teaching is snapshot-pinned by tests/test_complete_md_hard_guard_snapshot.py). Safe cuts are connective tissue only: tighten 'On entry mode suppressed the operation-2 cd-shim roots the session without...' phrasing, drop the '(dismisses the exit prompt)' parenthetical that duplicates the 'deferring the prompt' contrast. Note this paragraph already survived a verified trim (333cd43b, harness-token-efficiency-trim Task 6) that removed the genuine EnterWorktree-state restatement — what remains is the verified keep. No test anchors step-vi literals (step_v_ordering pins the suppressed-picker marker in step v; enterworktree_callsites' ±60 radius is satisfied by step-v tokens at lines 114-149), so the small compression carries zero pin churn. Do not use 'EnterWorktree(' with parens in any rewording (8bffd7c8 deliberately de-parenthesized step vi to dodge the call-site regex).
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py block regex '^\*\*Step v — Auto-enter sequence\*\*.*?(?=^\*\*)' terminates at '**Fallback' (line 149) — empirically verified the extracted block ends with the operation-5 events.log sentence, so lines 153-156 are outside every pinned assertion. test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py needs create_worktree + one of {show-toplevel, git-common-dir, 'EnterWorktree skipped'} within ±60 lines of 'EnterWorktree(' (line 136); the Fallback's 'EnterWorktree skipped' literal at line 149 satisfies it independently of vi (vi's show-toplevel is redundant coverage; window
revised: COMPRESS lines 153-156 is safe from the enforcement lens: no test, lint, hook, or pipeline reader pins any token unique to vi/vii. Preconditions: (1) keep the keep/remove data-loss warning and the ADR-0004 pointer (as proposed); (2) preserve the path-conditional in the opening sentence — the suppressed path has NO session-exit keep/remove prompt (cd-shim, no EnterWorktree session state), so the warning applies to the selected path; collapsing that distinction makes the warning factually wrong; (3) do not delete the line-start '**' boundary structure (the untouched '**Fallback' heading at line 149 already bounds the step-v ordering test's block, so vi's own heading is not load-bearing for extraction); (4) regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (dual-source drift pre-commit hook).

Highest leverage per token in the file (embedded verbatim in ~8 builder prompts per run): instructions 2 and 5 each state their rule twice and compress by ~60 tokens without touching the self-sealing guard (provenance commit c2daec5b), the anti-raw-git rule, or the scope fences — the template is not test-pinned so only behavioral contracts constrain the edit.

SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) No test/lint pins the template text: repo-wide grep for 'silently deviating' and 'task name, status' hits only the canonical file and its auto-generated mirror (plugins/cortex-core/skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:230,233); grep of tests/, cortex_command/, hooks/, bin/ for distinctive fragments ('Do not implement other tasks', 'self-certifying', 'flag it as an issue', 'Verification field') returns no template assertions — the many implement.md tests (branch-mode, step-v ordering, EnterWorktree callsites, daytime-free, lint globs) all target sections 1/1a, not the template. Commit c2dae
revised: Compress instructions 2 and 5 of the Builder Prompt Template (lines 230, 233): merge instruction 2's two sentences into one that keeps BOTH contracts (exact-match paths + flag-don't-silently-deviate), and drop instruction 5's first sentence ('Report what you did and any issues encountered'), which is fully subsumed by the field enumeration. Preconditions: (1) retain the 'For each task completed' distributive scoping in instruction 5 — combined-builder dispatches observably put multiple task blocks in one verbatim prompt (lifecycles #331, #339), and that phrase is what yields per-task report rows there; (2) leave instruction 6 (self-sealing guard, provenance c2daec5b), instruction 4 (anti-raw-git, echoed by the Constraints section line 283), and the trailing scope fences untouched; (3) regenerate plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (drift hook). Realistic savings ~25-40 tokens per prompt (not ~60 as claimed; the two instructions total only ~70 tokens), multiplied by ~8 builders per run.
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) Greps for every distinctive template phrase ('task name, status', 'verification outcome', 'silently deviating', 'artifact locations must match', 'self-certifying', 'Do not implement other tasks') across tests/, cortex_command/, hooks/, claude/, bin/, cortex/adr/ returned zero hits -- the template is not machine-pinned. The implement.md tests that do exist pin other sections: test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py and test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py pin section 1/1a markers and helper names; test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py extracts the step-v block by markers not line numbers
revised: COMPRESS survives the enforcement lens: zero tests, lints, hooks, ADRs, or pipeline readers pin the template's text, and all line-anchored tests (kept-pauses parity anchor at implement.md:37, AskUserQuestion mentions at lines 22-60) sit above line 217, so line-count changes in the template shift nothing pinned. Preconditions: (1) instruction 5's five enumerated report fields (task name, status, files modified, verification outcome, issues/deviations) are a shipped contract from the archived add-subagent-output-formats feature -- drop only the redundant lead sentence, keep all five fields; (2) instruction 2's two sentences are complementary (exact-match rule + flag-don't-silently-deviate escape hatch), not duplicates -- reword into one sentence but keep both halves, so realistic savings are ~25-35 tokens per prompt, not ~60; (3) keep instruction 6 verbatim (commit c2daec5b), the anti-raw-git line, and the three scope-fence lines; (4) do not introduce bare cortex_command python (test_bare_python_import_lint.py scans implement.md); (5) regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (drift pre-commit hook). Caveat: the prior token-efficiency pass (cortex/lifecycle/harness-token-efficiency-trim/evidence.json keep_rationale item 6) classified the template as verbatim-keep, but that is a prior audit's conservative scope line, not an enforcement mechanism protecting anything beyond the behavioral contracts enumerated above.
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) git log -L 217,240:skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md shows only 4 commits: 428e54ea (initial, 2026-04-01 — instructions 1-4, both sentences of instruction 2, scope fences), c2daec5b (2026-04-04 — instruction 6 self-sealing guard; motivated by observed failure recorded in cortex/retros/archive/2026-04-02-1629.md where a plan task wrote its own req1_verified evidence; failure mode recurred in retros 2026-04-18-2014, 2026-04-21-0853, 2026-04-22-1204 — still live), 6fb6657f (2026-04-10 — diff shows instruction 5's structured spec appended after the retained generic sentence; governing requi
revised: COMPRESS survives the provenance lens. Instruction 5's doubling is provably additive vestige: commit 6fb6657f (2026-04-10) appended the structured five-field report spec to the pre-existing generic sentence 'Report what you did and any issues encountered' without removing it; the governing contract (archived spec A3 of add-subagent-output-formats-compress-synthesis) requires only the five named fields (task name, status, files modified, verification outcome, issues/deviations), so dropping the generic lead-in loses nothing. Instruction 2 dates entirely to the initial commit (428e54ea) with no failure record behind either sentence; it is constraint + escape hatch rather than a literal double-statement, so the merge must preserve BOTH the exact-path constraint and the flag-don't-silently-deviate escape hatch in one sentence. Preconditions: (a) keep the five report fields verbatim-in-substance, (b) keep instruction 6 (self-sealing guard) untouched — it is failure-proven (c2daec5b, retro 2026-04-02-1629.md) and the failure mode recurred in at least three later retros (084, 085, 127 plan retros) and remains a live 'verification vacuity' through-line in 2026-06 critical reviews, (c) keep instruction 4's anti-raw-git/Skill-tool rule and the closing scope fences, (d) commit canonical + regenerated plugins/cortex-core mirror together per the drift hook. The ~60-token estimate is optimistic (~25-35 tokens is realistic from instructions 2+5 alone) but the direction is sound and the per-run multiplier (~8 builder prompts) makes even the smaller saving worthwhile.

## Instructions (+ literal prose findings format) (L44–61)
 COMPRESS 1415646~225 PROVISIONAL · no pins found

The sidecar exit-code semantics (0=proceed / 1=reject / 2=warn-and-continue) are stated verbatim twice in this file — here and §1 Step A line 73 — and can be stated once with a cross-reference, safely, because the pin counts invocation lines ('bash -s --' == 2, sidecar name >= 2) which both remain.
Scorer pins: tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py (bash -s -- count == 2); tests/test_bidirectional_concurrency_contract.py
SURVIVES (high · enforcement-and-pins) No enforcement anchors the exit-code prose itself. (a) tests/test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py pins only invocation-level facts: menu label, lock-acquire presence, sidecar filename >= 2, 'bash -s --' == 2 (exact), and gate/gated binary agreement — all survive a semantics-sentence dedup; the bash-s pin's own docstring (lines 71-77) says it was written 'to guard the §1/§1a consolidation trim', i.e. the pin authors anticipated this dedup and fenced only the invocation form. The originating spec (cortex/lifecycle/consolidate-implementmd-branch-worktree-dispatch-and/spec.md line 22) c
revised: The exit-code semantics can be stated once, but they are NOT verbatim duplicates: line 73 routes 'proceed to Step B / exit §1' while line 109 routes 'proceed normally / exit §1a' plus a '(runner.pid absent or process dead)' gloss. Safe merge = keep §1 Step A (line 73) as the full statement and reduce §1a.ii (line 109) to a cross-ref that preserves site-specific routing, e.g. 'Exit codes as in §1 Step A: on 1 exit §1a; on 0/2 proceed.' Preconditions: (1) keep the §1a.ii heading and its full invocation line untouched — test_sidecar_invocation_form_bash_s_count pins 'bash -s --' count at EXACTLY 2 and test_overnight_guard_sidecar_called_at_least_twice pins the sidecar filename >= 2, so the merged cross-ref must not add an example invocation; (2) trim at line 109, not line 73, so cortex_command/lifecycle_implement.py:75's line-cite 'implement.md:78–82' (Step B block) does not shift; (3) SKILL.md:151's target-map row citing '§1 Step A and §1a.ii' stays valid because both sites and both invocations remain. Realistic savings are only the ~50-token semantics sentence, far below the 715-token weighted_cost which prices the whole pinned section.
SURVIVES (high · failure-history-and-provenance) No failure provenance protects the duplicated prose. (1) Origin: both statements landed in two same-day design commits on 2026-05-18 — 62e298c4 'Extract overnight-active probe into sidecar bash' (§1a.ii; commit body says the sidecar is the 'single source for the daytime mirror and the upcoming interactive mirror') and cfc784da 'Wire interactive preflight guards into implement.md §1' (Step A mirror). Neither cites an F-row, transcript, ADR, or observed failure; the duplication is design residue of a mirror commit, and the exit-code contract's canonical home is the sidecar script header itself (
revised: Dedup survives with one correction and one precondition. Correction: the two exit-code statements are NOT verbatim — they share the semantic core (0=proceed / 1=reject / 2=warn-and-continue) but differ in site-specific routing targets (Step A: 'proceed to Step B' / 'exit §1'; §1a.ii: 'proceed normally' / 'exit §1a') and §1a.ii carries the stale-runner definition '(runner.pid absent or process dead)' (which also lives canonically in the sidecar script header). Precondition: keep both invocation code blocks (with their distinct wording args and root args) and keep the §1a.ii-specific routing targets in compressed form, e.g. 'Sidecar exit codes as in §1 Step A; routing here: 0 → proceed to iii, 1 → exit §1a, 2 → warn-and-continue and proceed.' Realizable savings are therefore smaller than a full state-once claim implies (~1 sentence core, not the whole section).
SURVIVES (high · behavioral-necessity) Execution-path walk: (a) selected mode — §1 Step A (line 73) executes the identical sidecar with full semantics 35 lines earlier in the same file, so a back-reference at §1a ii resolves trivially; (b) suppressed mode (branch-mode: worktree-interactive) — §1a ii is the ONLY overnight guard on the path, but the whole reference file is in context, so line 73's table is still readable even though Step A never executed. Semantics cannot be fully DELETED at §1a ii (sidecar exit 2 is silent — skills/lifecycle/references/_interactive_overnight_check.sh exits 2 with no output, so warn-and-continue vs r
revised: Line 109's exit-code MEANINGS (0=no overnight, 1=live, 2=stale) can be cross-referenced to §1 Step A line 73, PROVIDED the per-site route targets stay explicit at §1a ii ('1 -> surface wording and exit §1a; 0/2 -> proceed to step iii, warn-and-continue diagnostic on 2'). The two lines are not verbatim identical — Step A routes 0/2 to Step B (lock acquisition) which never runs on the suppressed branch-mode path, so a bare 'same as Step A' pointer would be the one unsafe form. Scope note: only the semantics sentence (~60 tokens, ~25-35 net savings) is dedupable; the invocation block lines 105-107 is pin-protected (bash -s -- count == 2 exact) and must remain.

revised: Compress only the two optional-field description strings inside the schema exemplar; keep delimiter, field names, structure, and enum byte-exact. Asymmetric precondition the scorer missed: line 72 (fix_invalidation_argument) is safely pointer-able because line 38 (outside this candidate, retained) carries the full instruction verbatim; line 73 (straddle_rationale) is NOT purely pointer-able because the Straddle Protocol section (lines 40-42) never names the field or its population condition -- line 73 is the SOLE instruction telling reviewers to populate straddle_rationale when biasing up to A on an unsplittable case, and the a-to-b-downgrade-rubric Trigger-3 Straddle exemption plus the live with_straddle classifier test depend on that field being populated in exactly that case. Safe form: pointer for line 72; for line 73 either keep the 'when biasing up to A on an unsplittable case' trigger clause or move the field-population instruction into the Straddle Protocol section in the same edit. Realistic savings ~25-30 raw tokens (~100-120 weighted), below the scorer's ~40-token estimate. Regenerate plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (drift hook).

revised: Compress the lines 1-12 preamble to ~2 lines keeping the parity-test path (tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py) and one bidirectional-parity sentence that retains the scan-scope clause ('every entry resolves to a real AskUserQuestion site, and every such site under skills/lifecycle/ and skills/refine/ has an entry here') — the two-directory scope is the only preamble fact not resident elsewhere. Safe given three preconditions: (1) the compressed preamble must not introduce any line matching the parity test's bullet regex `^-\s+`?<path>.md`?:<line>` (would parse as a phantom inventory entry); (2) at least one real bullet must remain (test asserts non-empty parse at test:129); (3) regenerate the plugins/cortex-core mirror in the same commit (dual-source drift pre-commit hook).

SURVIVES (medium · general-skeptic) (a) Pins: no standing test, lint, or hook greps the trimmed phrases — tests/test_resolve_model.py pins verb behavior only (golden matrix + exit codes); tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py pins {spec_path}/{requirements} placeholders in review.md, not the model section; grep of hooks/, bin/, cortex_command/ for 'never hardcode'/'halt and escalate' returns nothing. #334 spec (cortex/lifecycle/criticality-dispatch-reference-cleanup-relocate-fanoutmd/spec.md Req 8) required 'an explicit nonzero-exit/halt-and-escalate instruction (grep >=1 per site)' — the trim keeps that instruction, so e
revised: The failure-cause enumeration ('whether the verb rejected the input or the inner cortex-lifecycle-state read returned corrupt/absent criticality') is behaviorally redundant — a failed/corrupt cortex-lifecycle-state read feeds bad criticality into cortex-resolve-model which exits 2 (#334 spec Req 4 fail-loud), so both causes funnel into the one observable the kept sentence already handles ('on nonzero exit ... halt and escalate ... rather than guessing or substituting a model'). Trim is safe given two preconditions: (1) keep the literal 'nonzero exit' + 'halt and escalate' wording — #334 spec Req 8 acceptance was a per-site grep for exactly that instruction; (2) apply the identical trim to the sibling dispatch sites (implement.md:172-178, competing-plans.md:16 and :67, orchestrator-review.md, critical-review/SKILL.md:76) in the same change, since the enumeration was authored as deliberate cross-site parity in commit 1be0a634 one day ago and a review.md-only trim creates drift among intentionally parallel sites. The 'never hardcode a model literal' clause (~7 tokens) is the only standing anti-re-inlining guard (no test or lint bans model literals in skill prose), but it is editorial-audience not runtime-audience; dropping it is acceptable, keeping just those 7 tokens is a defensible smaller trim.

Precondition: edit the canonical skills/ file only and let the pre-commit mirror hook regenerate plugins/cortex-core/.

Per-file scorecards

Grouped by cluster, files sorted by weighted hot-path cost. Bar length = weighted cost (log scale); bar color = value (light = low value, dark = high). Expand a file, then a section, for the scorer rationale and pins.

rationale

rationale

§1 Branch selection — fallback picker optionsL22–27 · 297 tok · 1,426 wtok · v7LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
Fires only when no plan-time dispatch_choice was recorded (ADR-0012 made that the primary path), so option prose is rare-branch content; option labels are test-pinned and a prior branch-picker.md extraction was reverted (30905d17) because pins stayed anchored to implement.md §1 — re-extraction must move the pin tests in the same change.

rationale

## ApplicabilityL5–16 · 184 tok · 221 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Skip rule (low AND simple) is a non-derivable project contract; corrupted-fires-the-gate clause has commit provenance (#287, 4800ed31, spec R8b). Corrupted clause twins criticality-matrix.md:34 but this file is read standalone by refine specify.md, so inline survives the R1 kill-reason logic.
