## Protocol / ### 1. Identify Phase and ChecklistL17–22 · 52 tok · 62 wtok · v3COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Step 1 is procedural narration of the obvious — a frontier model selects Post-Specify vs Post-Plan from the checklist headings alone. Sentence was added in d1ae91d5 only to fill a previously-empty heading; folding it into step 2's opening (with step renumber, or keeping numbering) is safe since the discovery shim's pinned section list does not include step 1.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:9 pins section names Execute Review/Handle Verdict/Fix Dispatch/Escalation/Cycle Cap — step 1 NOT in that list
### 2. Execute ReviewL23–31 · 96 tok · 115 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The main-conversation no-dispatch routing line is a real decision (artifact already in context) — keep. The pass/flag bullet definitions ('The item is satisfied. No action needed.') are padding a frontier model derives from the words pass/flag; ~40 tokens compressible.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:9 pins 'Execute Review' heading
### 3. Handle VerdictL32–39 · 109 tok · 131 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Carries the operative cycle-counter routing (cycle 3+ -> escalate) and the one-line-assessment output shape with example. This is the canonical statement of the cap routing; the Cycle Cap section (s12) is the redundant copy, so dedup targets s12 not here.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:9 pins 'Handle Verdict' heading
### 4. Fix DispatchL40–57 · 383 tok · 460 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Fresh-vs-same-conversation criterion (needs user input or not), the resolve-model contract (verb+flags+halt-on-nonzero, pinned by tests/test_resolve_model.py and migrated in #334), and envelope absorption (provenance 4fc01480) are all load-bearing. The two example-lists of issue types ('research depth issues, plan restructuring, feasibility re-assessment...') are derivable from the criterion; ~80-100 tokens compressible without touching the contract lines.
Pins: tests/test_resolve_model.py (golden-anchor for orchestrator-fix row); docs/internals/sdk.md:142-144 (matrix ownership); cortex_command/lifecycle/resolve_model_cli.py:9; commit 4fc01480 (silent envelope absorption); skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:9 pins 'Fix Dispatch' heading
#### Fix Agent Prompt TemplateL58–85 · 262 tok · 314 wtok · v8LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
The YAML envelope contract (f5ff1ad8) and full-rewrite rule are load-bearing, but the template is only consumed on a flag verdict (0-2 dispatches typical; discovery typical pass = 0), while it is resident on every §3a read (2x per feature). Extracting to a sibling read only at Fix Dispatch saves ~262 tokens per load; requires updating the discovery shim's substitution pointer (line 11) and keeping the reference resolvable for test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:11 (substitutes {feature}/{artifact} paths and swaps envelope format in this template by name); tests/test_plugin_mirror_parity.py:32 (mirror regen on edit); tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py (new ref path must resolve; tracked-file-move gotcha)

rationale

Pins: commits 024cb6fa, c49ccc5f, 9bce456d; spec.md format contract in refine specify.md §3a (mandatory read-and-follow)
### Post-Plan ChecklistL114–130 · 658 tok · 790 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
P1-P10 with observed-failure provenance: P7 self-sealing operational test (c2daec5b) matches the recurring 'verification vacuity' critical-review through-line; P8 (4484e3ae) encodes the closed-set taxonomy and the criticality=critical gating; P9/P10 (c49ccc5f) name exact section headings and per-complexity thresholds. The long P7 prose encodes a benign-vs-harmful discrimination that compression would blunt.
Pins: commits c2daec5b, 4484e3ae, c49ccc5f; P8 references §1b (competing-plans, #341 stub heading)
## Cycle CapL131–134 · 58 tok · 70 wtok · v4MERGE_DEDUPVERIFIED
rationale
Third statement of the 2-cycle cap — step 3 (line 38) routes on it and step 5 (line 88) defines it; the only novel facts here ('a cycle is one complete pass', 'counter resets per phase') fold into step 5 in one clause. The 'Cycle Cap' section name is pinned by the discovery shim's protocol-section list, so either keep a stub heading or update shim line 9 in the same commit.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md:9 (names 'Cycle Cap' as an apply-as-written section); d1ae91d5 already removed one cycle-cap restatement, confirming the dedup direction
## ConstraintsL135–139 · 63 tok · 76 wtok · v2DELETEVERIFIED
rationale
One-row Thought/Reality table restating the Applicability skip rule's AND semantics — pure defensive guard against a weak-model misreading, which the stated trust bar rates LOW; 'Run review for all other combinations' (line 13) already closes the gap. No test/hook/ADR pins the heading; d1ae91d5 already deleted this table's other row.
Pins: none found (grep for '## Constraints' and 'Thought.*Reality' hits only overnight prompt files; discovery sibling has its own independent table)
skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md common · ×1 · 3,759 tok · 2,255 wtok · median v9 · 19 sections
# Plan Phase (title + intro)L1–4 · 42 tok · 25 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Frame-setting one-liner ('plans are prose, not code') that anchors the whole phase; tiny cost.
## Protocol / ### 1. Load ContextL5–13 · 68 tok · 41 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Exact non-derivable artifact paths (research.md required, spec.md required, lifecycle.config.md optional project constraint).
### 1a. Check CriticalityL14–20 · 139 tok · 83 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Test-pinned heading routing the critical-only competing-plans branch; exact verb invocation. Contains 1 of 4 repeated manifest-boilerplate parentheticals (see file-compress).
Pins: tests/test_skill_section_citations.py:41-50; cortex/adr/0009 (manifest-propagation phrasing rationale)
### 1b. Competing Plans (Critical Only) — stubL21–24 · 72 tok · 43 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Deliberate #341 extraction stub kept for two test pins; already minimal — the resident 10.9KB block was already moved to competing-plans.md.
Pins: tests/test_skill_section_citations.py:58-65; tests/test_competing_plans_wired.py:86
### 2. Design the ApproachL25–32 · 58 tok · 35 wtok · v4COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Generic design checklist (architecture, file order, integration, testing) a frontier model performs unprompted; heading must survive because competing-plans.md routes its all-variants-rejected fallback to 'plan.md §2 (Design the Approach)'.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:63,90,108 (heading cited as fallback target)
### 3. Write Plan Artifact (template)L33–87 · 638 tok · 383 wtok · v10KEEP
rationale
The core non-derivable contract: every field name, heading level, and conditional (Outline H3-only, Acceptance complex-only, Verification (a)/(b)/(c)) is parsed by the overnight pipeline or gated by the P-checklist.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/parser.py (## Outline, ### Phase, **Depends on**, **Complexity** grammar); cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_parser.py:398-484,597-756; skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md P4/P8/P9/P10; tests/test_critical_review_classifier.py:39 (## Acceptance)
### Task SizingL88–93 · 80 tok · 48 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Project-specific calibration numbers (5-15 min, 1-5 files, 5-15 tasks) that P1 enforces at review; self-containment rule shapes dispatchability.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md P1 (same bounds)
### Wiring Co-LocationL94–99 · 151 tok · 91 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Observed-failure provenance: commit 83667df2 records ticket-108 Task 5 deploying cortex-backlog-ready before Task 7 wired it, forcing --no-verify at the W003 orphan gate; the reorder escape hatch (wiring-first is safe) is non-derivable W003 asymmetry knowledge.
Pins: commit 83667df2 (ticket 108 failure); cortex_command/parity_check.py:5 (W003); tests/fixtures/cortex-check-parity/orphan_bin.stdout
### Task Complexity ClassificationL100–113 · 276 tok · 166 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Tier semantics and the never-trivial rule are load-bearing (dispatch.py maps tier to model/turn budget; trivial=15 turns causes turn exhaustion on commit-bearing tasks), but the Examples column and some bold narration are derivable filler worth ~60-80 tokens.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/dispatch.py:129-131 (tier→model/max_turns); cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_parser.py:199-242 (**Complexity** field)
### Dependency AnnotationsL114–117 · 90 tok · 54 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Exact field-format contract ([N, M] vs none, position between What and Context) consumed by the parallel-dispatch parser; misformat blocks parallelism.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/parser.py:503-540 (Depends-on grammar); cortex_command/common.py:928; cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_parser.py:696-716 (empty/prose forms raise)
### Sub-task headings (### Task Na)L118–123 · 294 tok · 176 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Every clause mirrors parser behavior (3 < 3a < 3b < 4 ordering, no auto-expansion of bare [3], multi-letter/uppercase fail-loud) and the disjoint-Files rule prevents a real same-batch shared-worktree last-writer-wins race; needed at authoring time, not a rare branch, so LAZY_REF would just force a Read on every plan.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/parser.py:68,80,336-366,503-540; commit 1fb02129 (#297)
### Files/Verification ConsistencyL124–127 · 79 tok · 47 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Names a non-obvious impossible-constraint interaction (builders are file-scope-restricted, so Verification implying unlisted files deadlocks); P6 enforces it at review but authoring-time statement prevents the rework cycle.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md P6
### Caller EnumerationL128–131 · 71 tok · 43 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Partially derivable completeness rule, but it feeds the same builder file-scope restriction as s12 and is cheap; no pin found, kept on value-per-token.
### Code Budget (+ artifact registration line)L132–154 · 284 tok · 170 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The design/implementation line and self-sealing prohibition are gate-backed (P5/P7; self-sealing is the recurring critical-review through-line), but the prose-only-Verification bullet duplicates the line-86 P4 note and several allowed/prohibited bullets restate the template's Context field; line 153's artifact-registration recipe pointer must be preserved verbatim. ~50-60 tokens compressible.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md P5, P7; backlog-writeback.md artifact-registration recipe (line 153 contract)
### 3a. Orchestrator ReviewL155–158 · 88 tok · 53 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Ordering gate (review must pass before user approval) plus the load pointer; contains 1 of 4 manifest-boilerplate parentheticals (see file-compress).
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md (protocol target); cortex/adr/0009
### 3b. Critical ReviewL159–167 · 200 tok · 120 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Exact auto-trigger predicate (tier=complex AND criticality in {medium,high,critical}) matching the CLAUDE.md-documented lifecycle behavior, plus exact state-read verbs; contains 2 of 4 manifest-boilerplate parentheticals (see file-compress).
Pins: CLAUDE.md critical-review auto-trigger clause; skills/lifecycle/references/criticality-matrix.md §Reading lifecycle state; cortex/adr/0009
### 4. User Approval (merged branch/dispatch surface)L168–196 · 861 tok · 517 wtok · v10KEEP
rationale
Protects the plan-approval user affordance (kept-pauses inventory pins the AskUserQuestion at plan.md:184 within ±20 lines) and carries exact event contracts: dispatch_choice value set, plan_approved-before-phase_transition ordering, no-emit-on-revision-rounds, feature_paused + overnight-eligibility warning. The ~60-token recap of Implement §1's preflight rules is mild dedup slack (folded into file-compress); any trim above line 184 must move the kept-pauses anchor with it.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:18 via tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py (±20-line window); tests/test_dispatch_choice_resolver.py; tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:102,130,191-201; skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:28-46 (§1 preflight source of truth)
### 5. TransitionL197–206 · 182 tok · 109 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Test-pinned heading (cited by lifecycle_config.py); exact phase_transition emission, plan_approved-precedes ordering constraint, and the commit-artifacts flag routing including the wait-path durability rationale.
Pins: tests/test_skill_section_citations.py:73-82; cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py:8-9 (cites plan.md §5); tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py (phase_transition)
## Hard GateL207–211 · 93 tok · 56 wtok · v6MERGE_DEDUPVERIFIED
rationale
First paragraph restates s14 Code Budget / P5 (no implementation code in plans) — pure duplication; second paragraph (backlog items suggest approaches, don't prescribe them) is distinct judgment guidance worth keeping. No external pin on this heading (test_refine_skill.py's '## Hard Gate' is refine SKILL.md's own section).
skills/lifecycle/references/complete.md common · ×1 · 3,433 tok · 2,059 wtok · median v8 · 15 sections
# Complete Phase (intro + ## Protocol)L1–6 · 32 tok · 19 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
One-line framing that the phase is multi-step with a merge-wait pause and re-invocation — shapes the whole read. Negligible cost.
### Step 1 — Run TestsL7–16 · 135 tok · 81 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Project-specific contract (cortex/lifecycle.config.md, test-command key, three-branch routing, fail-stop) plus the first-run-only cross-ref to Step 7. Already terse; compression yield is trivial.
Pins: cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py (parses the test-command config this step consumes)
### Step 2 — Commit Lifecycle ArtifactsL17–22 · 194 tok · 116 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
commit-artifacts flag routing (cortex-read-commit-artifacts) plus the on-main short-circuit ordering contract (skip 2-5, jump to 9-12, Step 11a still runs) — non-derivable routing with heading pinned verbatim.
Pins: tests/test_skill_section_citations.py:94 (requires '### Step 2 — Commit Lifecycle Artifacts' verbatim); cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py:8-9 (docstring cites complete.md Step 2 as the prose-resident default)
### Step 3 — Push Branch and Create PRL23–37 · 353 tok · 212 wtok · v5COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The Variant-A two-signal detection is spelled out as a numbered procedure with shell narration (save _origin_pwd, cd in, invoke, cd back) — over-prescribed How for a frontier model. The structural test pins only ~7 token anchors (Step 3 heading, read_lock, git rev-parse --show-toplevel, cd-in-then-out/_origin_pwd, both-signals, advisory, absent-or-contradictory fallback), all preservable in a ~half-size form.
Pins: tests/test_complete_pr_routing.py:450-540 TestCompleteStepThreeStructural (7 substring assertions listed above); tests/test_complete_pr_routing.py:44-116 (Python mirror of the detection logic, docstring-linked); tests/test_interactive_lock.py:406 (comment: exercises complete.md Step 3's read_lock); tests/test_variant_a_writer_sites_baseline.py:5 (names complete.md a Variant-A site NOT modified by #331 Phase 3; does not read its text); cortex/adr/0004-multi-step-complete-and-interactive-worktree-lifecycle.md
### Step 4 — Write pr.json AtomicallyL38–59 · 188 tok · 113 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Closed 5-field schema, tempfile+os.replace same-filesystem atomicity, and the repo-field lock rationale — every element is individually test-pinned and non-derivable.
Pins: tests/test_complete_pr_json_schema.py:301-350 (pins Step 4 heading, os.replace, tempfile, all 5 schema fields, repo locked-at-PR-creation rationale, pipeline.md atomicity cite, opened_at ISO8601); cortex/requirements/pipeline.md:124-130 (cited atomicity invariant)
### Step 5 — Emit pr_opened EventL60–67 · 73 tok · 44 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Exact event schema; a test asserts the raw-JSON literal deliberately stays hand-written here (NOT migrated to the event verb in #331) — the prose is a pinned decision record.
Pins: tests/test_complete_feature_complete_emission.py:278-287 (asserts pr_opened raw-JSON literal remains in complete.md Step 5)
### Step 6 — Phase-Exit Pause (Handoff Message)L68–77 · 71 tok · 43 wtok · v10KEEP
rationale
User affordance: the merge-wait pause is in the kept-pauses inventory and parity-tested; 'do not poll — manual re-invocation is the gate' is the phase's core control point.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:21 (inventory entry anchored at complete.md:73, tagged phase-exit pause); tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py (bidirectional parity enforcement)
## Re-invocation: State-Aware Routing (intro)L78–81 · 47 tok · 28 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Two-line entry framing for the re-invocation half of the file; below any meaningful compression yield.
### Step 7 — State-Aware RoutingL82–101 · 443 tok · 266 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Post-#331 residue after a deliberate prose collapse (bcfba442): the verb invocation plus the non-derivable continue_to→step mapping and the orphan_ambiguous user-disambiguation affordance. The test forbids re-growing verb-owned terminal strings, so the section is structurally held at roughly this size.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_complete_state_routing.py:821-895 (pins Step 7 heading, requires cortex-lifecycle-complete-route token in region, forbids 9 verb-owned terminal-message strings); cortex_command/lifecycle/complete_route.py (the verb the mapping targets); provenance: bcfba442 (#331 extraction), #339 Branch-2 narrowing in the verb
### Step 8 — Worktree CleanupL102–124 · 402 tok · 241 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Guards a destructive operation (worktree removal): hard guard is byte-equality-pinned to a fixture, both cleanup gates and the no-force constraint are test-pinned, and the two-exit-paths sentence is a user affordance. Effectively immutable without coordinated fixture updates.
Pins: tests/test_complete_md_hard_guard_snapshot.py + tests/fixtures/complete_md_hard_guard.txt (byte-equality on the Hard-guard paragraph, lines 104-108); tests/test_complete_cleanup_gates.py:389-401 (pins 'git status --porcelain' dirty check, 'git merge-base --is-ancestor' ancestor check, dirty-skip warning, no force=True); cortex/adr/0004-multi-step-complete-and-interactive-worktree-lifecycle.md
### Step 9 — Backlog Write-BackL125–134 · 102 tok · 61 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Exact verb+flags (cortex-update-item --status complete --session-id null), the exit-2 contract, and a pointer to canonical ambiguous-slug handling in backlog-writeback.md — already the pointer form the killed R1 dedup analysis endorsed.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/backlog-writeback.md (canonical handling this section defers to; killed-idea R1 protects the site-specific arm shape); cortex_command/backlog/update_item.py:439 (--session-id flag contract)
### Step 10 — Backlog Index SyncL135–146 · 374 tok · 224 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The backend gate is pinned and high-value (#321 R12, commit ca28428d), but the section carries an unpinned ~40-token provenance parenthetical explaining why the gate exists, plus three verbatim 'Index regenerated via...'/WARNING emit strings and command -v narration in the fallback chain that no test asserts — the gate test pins only the heading, cortex-read-backlog-backend ordered before cortex-generate-backlog-index, and the 'disabled for this repo' advisory.
Pins: tests/test_complete_index_sync_gate.py:19-70 (pins '### Step 10 — Backlog Index Sync' verbatim for section slicing, BACKEND_READER before REGEN_ANCHOR ordering, 'disabled for this repo' skip-arm witness; negative control forbids the pre-edit unconditional intro); provenance: ca28428d 'Gate complete.md Step 10 index sync on backend (#321 Task 9)'
### Step 11 — Log feature_completeL147–160 · 337 tok · 202 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Exact verb invocation with --set-json/--set type discipline, counters source, no-schema_version note, and the idempotent-skip guard with observed-failure provenance (commit-retry duplicate row, b235b439 R4). The JSON-line-parse-not-substring nuance is a real false-positive correctness detail, cheap to keep.
Pins: tests/test_complete_feature_complete_emission.py:88 (replicates the production Step 11 invocation); tests/test_cortex_lifecycle_counters_parity.py; provenance: b7616647 (#331 verb migration), b235b439 (R4 idempotent-skip guard)
### Step 11a — Commit Finalization ArtifactsL161–197 · 548 tok · 329 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Largest section; densely pinned by the region test, but carries ~110 unpinned tokens re-narrating stage-artifacts verb internals (what it stages, no-glob/no--u discipline the verb itself owns), an example commit subject, and the git-diff-cached restatement of the signal semantics. A ~20% trim retains every pinned token (anchors, both verb invocations, /cortex-core:commit, nothing_staged, halt clause, 'main' advisory).
Pins: tests/test_complete_md_finalization_commit.py (pins <!-- finalization-commit-step --> anchors verbatim, cortex-lifecycle-stage-artifacts invocation, cortex-read-commit-artifacts, /cortex-core:commit, stage-first idempotent guard 'nothing_staged' token, halt-on-failure clause, 'main' in branch advisory R13); cortex_command/lifecycle/stage_artifacts.py + tests/test_stage_artifacts.py (verb contract); provenance: 2da23311 (#331 extraction), e46e51d2 (R7 broadened nothing_staged note — the two-reach-paths parenthetical is deliberate)
### Step 12 — Summarize and Preserve Lifecycle DirectoryL198–209 · 134 tok · 80 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Output shape (4 summary bullets), the preserve-directory rule, and 'proceed automatically — do not ask' which encodes the auto-progression decision to remove a ceremonial pause here. No pins found, but each line changes behavior.
skills/lifecycle/references/backlog-writeback.md always · ×1 · 1,676 tok · 1,676 wtok · median v8 · 6 sections
# Backlog Status Check and Backlog Write-Back (intro)L1–6 · 142 tok · 142 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Canonical-home declaration is the routing anchor that keeps 8 cross-file citers (plan.md:153, review.md:136, complete.md:133/137, refine SKILL.md:83/135/165, clarify.md:112) pointer-thin; the consume-Step-1/never-re-scan contract is a real ordering constraint. Already tight; its 'never re-scan' phrase is restated in s3/s4 and the dedupe is counted there.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:114 (references index entry); tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py (file must exist and resolve)
## Backend routing (resolve once)L7–16 · 341 tok · 341 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The three routing arms + cortex-read-backlog-backend verb name are a non-derivable contract (added ba5bac8e, #317 R2/R7 fail-safe) and must stay, but ~40% is within-file restatement: line 15 repeats line 9's carrier mapping in substance, and 'proceed exactly as today'/'the unchanged behavior' are #317-migration-era narration now vestigial. This is within-file compression, NOT the killed R1 cross-file dedupe.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/complete.md:137 cites the file for backend gating (no heading dependency); no test pins the 'Backend routing' heading (verified grep tests/ hooks/ cortex/adr/ cortex_command/)
## Backlog Status CheckL17–47 · 549 tok · 549 wtok · v9COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Core content is pinned and high-anchor: AskUserQuestion close-vs-continue is a kept user pause (kept-pauses.md:20 -> parity test, +-35-line tolerance on lines 22/26), never-auto-close is a correctness gate, and the feature_complete verb line + omit-tasks_total note are pinned to an exact count-1 field-map by test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:153. Narrow trim only: step 4.2 restates s2's none/external arms (~2 lines) and 'Append the following NDJSON event...(one JSON object per line)' is vestigial pre-#330 framing now that a verb does the append (migrated 1cb699f1).
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:153 (feature_complete count=1, canonical field-map); tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py via kept-pauses.md:20 anchor backlog-writeback.md:11 (AskUserQuestion within +-35 lines)
## Backlog Write-Back (Lifecycle Start)L48–59 · 377 tok · 377 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The start-sync invocation with exact flags, basename semantics, and exit-3 empty-string arg are non-derivable contract (10f62b6d, #326). But line 58's first half re-describes what the verb already does structurally on cortex-backlog/none (ADR-0019 guard: the verb owns those arms) — prose duplicating shipped verb behavior; only the external-tracker best-effort instruction is model-facing. Third 'Do not re-scan' restatement also compressible. Heading must stay verbatim (start_sync.py docstring cites it).
Pins: cortex_command/lifecycle/start_sync.py:5 docstring cites the 'Backlog Write-Back (Lifecycle Start)' heading; tests/test_start_sync.py pins the verb's behavior (not this prose)
## `cortex-update-item` Exit-2 Handling (canonical)L60–63 · 151 tok · 151 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The rule itself is one sentence and heavily cited (5 files point to 'the canonical ambiguous-slug handling'); the second sentence enumerating every covered call site is coverage narration derivable from 'canonical' + the citers themselves pointing here. Keep heading and rule verbatim; drop the enumeration (~40% of section).
Pins: complete.md:133, clarify.md:112, refine SKILL.md:83 cite 'canonical ambiguous-slug handling in backlog-writeback.md'; start_sync.py:28+ re-implements exit-2 passthrough citing this rule
## Registering an Artifact in index.md (canonical)L64–73 · 118 tok · 118 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Already compact (4 bullets); cited by plan.md:153, review.md:136, refine SKILL.md:135/165 as the canonical recipe; idempotent-skip + atomic-rewrite are contract. No existing verb registers artifacts (create_index is creation-only, stage-artifacts is worktree staging — verified), and proposing a new OFFLOAD_CLI verb falls in the killed #336-harvested category with a byte win too small to justify verb+test+mirror burden.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md:153; skills/lifecycle/references/review.md:136; skills/refine/SKILL.md:135 and :165
skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md rare · ×3 dispatch · 2,830 tok · 849 wtok · median v8 · 9 sections
### 1b. Competing Plans (Critical Only) — heading + extraction note + introL1–5 · 168 tok · 50 wtok · v6COMPRESSREFUTED · KEEP
rationale
Heading is the external citation anchor (overnight prompt + test cite 'competing-plans.md §1b') and the 2-3-agent decision criterion is real What+Why; but the line-3 blockquote's a-g contents listing (~360B) duplicates the section structure a frontier model sees two lines later. Keep heading verbatim + provenance sentence; drop the ToC.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:302 (cites canonical §1b); cortex_command/overnight/tests/test_orchestrator_round.py:85 (cites canonical §1b); tests/test_competing_plans_wired.py:31-44 (file + mirror existence)
**a. Prepare shared context**L6–7 · 77 tok · 23 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable contract: absolute-path injection (path+SHA migration Req 6) plus the independence constraint (no draft sharing) that is the entire point of competing plans. Already terse.
Pins: tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py:134-147 ({spec_path}/{research_path} must appear in this file)
**b. Dispatch plan agents** + model-resolution bash blockL8–17 · 253 tok · 76 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The cortex-resolve-model --role competing-plan invocation + halt-on-nonzero rule is a pinned, non-derivable verb contract (9-value). But the surrounding explanation (why read criticality back instead of hardcoding; enumerating the two nonzero-exit causes) is derivable — the verb itself fail-louds on undefined cells — and compresses ~30%.
Pins: tests/test_resolve_model.py:71,118-119,180 (competing-plan critical-only matrix); cortex_command/lifecycle/resolve_model_cli.py:49-62 (verb contract); cortex-check-contract lint requires flags on inline cortex-* mentions (bash block already compliant)
Plan Agent Prompt Template (verbatim-embedded, dispatched 2-3x)L18–62 · 665 tok · 200 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Highest-cost section and it multiplies 3x per critical plan. Load-bearing: placeholders test-pinned, Architectural Pattern closed enum parity with plan.md:42, self-sealing verification guard has observed-failure provenance (c2daec5b). Compressible ~20-25%: the READ_OK paragraph (line 33) states the sentinel format three times and no runtime parser consumes plan-agent READ_OK (unlike critical-review); instructions 3 and 4 both describe populating the Architectural Pattern field; line 60 carries a bare 'plan.md §3' ref a sub-agent cannot resolve alongside the already-inlined required fields. Allowed/Prohibited list duplication with plan.md:136-151 is deliberate self-containment (do not pointer-ify — R1 kill reason applies).
Pins: tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py:134-147 ({spec_path}/{research_path} Req 10b); skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md:42,136-151 (Architectural Pattern field + Allowed/Prohibited parity); commit c2daec5b (self-sealing verification guard provenance); line 40's plan_comparison mention contributes to tests/test_competing_plans_wired.py:97-104 token check (token also present in s9)
**c. Collect results** (partial/single/total failure routing)L63–64 · 96 tok · 29 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable failure-routing contract with exact section targets (skip §1b.d-f → plan.md §3a; all-fail → plan.md §2-§3), parity with orchestrator-round.md's variant-edge-cases block. Already one line.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:296-298 (parity twin single-survivor/all-fail rules)
**d. Synthesizer dispatch**L65–70 · 307 tok · 92 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Verb contract (synthesizer criticality-independent), canonical-fragment load rule (importlib.resources, do-not-paraphrase), and user-prompt content list are pinned contracts. Compressible ~15-20%: the halt-and-escalate sentence repeats s3 verbatim-ish, and the swap-and-require-agreement description restates a protocol already carried verbatim (test-pinned) inside plan-synthesizer.md itself.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py:77-80,172,175 (fragment load + verbatim swap-probe phrase); tests/test_resolve_model.py:33,71 (synthesizer role); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:300 (near-verbatim parity twin, deliberately dual-surface)
**e. Envelope extraction** (LAST-occurrence anchor + schema validation)L71–76 · 223 tok · 67 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
This is the CANONICAL definition the overnight orchestrator prompt explicitly defers to ('the same LAST-occurrence anchor pattern as the canonical skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md §1b'). Exact regex, envelope schema enum, and the degrade-to-low-confidence rule are correctness gates; trimming the citation anchor risks severing the cross-surface parity.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:302 (cites this section as canonical); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/plan-synthesizer.md:74-112 (envelope schema parity); cortex_command/overnight/tests/test_orchestrator_round.py:335-416 (envelope→routing behavior)
**f. Route on verdict + confidence** (rubber-stamp/override/fallback table/cross-graft)L77–93 · 529 tok · 159 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The operator pick (rubber-stamp default, typed override, reject-all) is a user affordance — 9-10 anchor — and the verdict-C and hidden-rationale rules are correctness gates; those stay. Compressible ~20%: 'write the ... variant's content to cortex/lifecycle/{feature}/plan.md' is stated four times (rubber-stamp, override, selection, graft) and the full 4-row markdown table skeleton is renderable by a frontier model from a column list.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_metrics.py:1522 (rubber_stamp/override/deferred disposition values consumed downstream); skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:18 (plan-approval pause pinned at plan.md:184 — adjacent affordance, this section's pick is the critical-arm variant of it)
**g. Log v2 plan_comparison event** + field semantics + closing routeL94–108 · 514 tok · 154 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Event name + full v2 schema are heavily pinned (wired-test token check, metrics round-trip, registry row) and ADR-0020 explicitly exempts plan_comparison from the event verb, so OFFLOAD_CLI is contra-ADR and MERGE_DEDUP with orchestrator-round would sever the deliberately parity-visible dual-producer schema (killed idea R2's argument). Safe compression ~25%: the schema_version/selection_rationale/selector_confidence semantics bullets restate what the inline JSONL example already shows; keep only the non-derivable derivation rules (position_swap mapping, disposition definitions, auto_select reservation, operator_choice null semantics) and the closing route targets.
Pins: tests/test_competing_plans_wired.py:97-104 (plan_comparison token required in this file); cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_metrics.py:1496-1544 (v2 schema round-trip); cortex/adr/0020-lifecycle-event-emission-contract.md:13 (hand-written exemption); bin/.events-registry.md:21 (registry row; emitter cite is stale plan.md, pre-#341); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:296,345-347 (parity-visible sibling producer)
skills/lifecycle/references/review.md conditional · ×1 · 2,763 tok · 829 wtok · median v8 · 11 sections
# Review Phase (title + intro)L1–4 · 34 tok · 10 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
Framing is useful but 'Complex tier only.' is stale/incomplete — the load profile and criticality-matrix.md/implement.md gating also force review for simple+high/critical, and the gate is decided upstream before this file is ever read. Correct or drop that clause; keep 'two-stage' and 'reviewer must NOT modify files'.
## Protocol / ### 1. Gather Review InputsL5–13 · 175 tok · 53 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Bullets 1-3 (read spec.md, identify changed files via git log or plan.md lists, read plan.md) are input-gathering a frontier model performs from the prompt placeholders alone (~40 tokens trimmable). Line 12 is load-bearing and pinned: verb+flag contract, path-list injection, no-match fallback semantics — keep near-verbatim.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py CONSUMER_REFS includes review.md; citation regex 'load-requirements\.md|tag-based.*loading' must match (line 12)
### 2. Launch Review Sub-TaskL14–25 · 180 tok · 54 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The exact cortex-resolve-model + nested cortex-lifecycle-state invocation is a non-derivable verb contract (#334, commit 1be0a634) and the halt-on-nonzero degrade-loud rule is policy — both keep. The failure-cause enumeration ('whether the verb rejected the input or the inner read returned corrupt/absent criticality') and the 'never hardcode a model literal' framing are explanatory hedging (~35 tokens) a frontier model doesn't need beyond 'on nonzero exit, halt and escalate'.
Pins: commit 1be0a634 migrated this dispatch site to the verb (#334); tests/test_resolve_model.py pins the verb behavior (review@high=opus discriminator) but not this prose
### Reviewer Prompt Template — header + input slotsL26–42 · 131 tok · 39 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Prompt-shape contract: {spec_path} substitution is test-pinned (present-required) and the old hedged-contents placeholder is absent-required; the path-list injection slot wires §1's verb output into the drift check. Non-derivable and dispatch-embedded.
Pins: tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py: {spec_path} must be present (Req 7); hedged-contents placeholder must be absent (Req 10a)
Reviewer Prompt Template — Stage 1/2 + Requirements Drift instructionsL43–64 · 298 tok · 89 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
The two-stage gate (any FAIL skips Stage 2), PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL rating vocabulary, and drift state=none/detected semantics are downstream-consumed contracts (§4a auto-apply, morning report drift parse, stage-artifacts fixtures). A near-duplicate exists in cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/review.md, but the two are deliberate separate producers (interactive vs overnight worktree, different placeholders) — deduping across the skill/package boundary echoes the killed R2 parity concern.
Pins: tests/test_stage_artifacts.py and tests/test_requirements_parity_audit.py build review.md fixtures on '## Stage 1: Spec Compliance' + drift headings; cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/review.md carries the parallel overnight copy (parity-visible producer pair)
Reviewer Prompt Template — Write Review, verdict JSON, drift + Suggested Update formatsL65–97 · 470 tok · 141 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Exact machine-parsed contracts: verdict JSON field names/values are parsed by cortex_command/pipeline/review_dispatch.parse_verdict and common.py state detection; the ## Requirements Drift format is parsed by the morning report; the Suggested Update File/Section/Content format feeds §4a auto-apply. The negative-example line (not 'overall'/'result'/'status') has observed-failure provenance — metrics.py _VERDICT_FIELD_ALIASES ('verdict','review_verdict','decision') exists because reviewers emitted alias names.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/review_dispatch.py parse_verdict() (exact JSON fields); cortex_command/common.py verdict regex + cycle counting; tests/test_report.py:791-796 drift-section fixture format; tests/test_drift_enforcement_protocol.py + tests/test_stage_artifacts.py + tests/test_requirements_parity_audit.py (Suggested Requirements Update format); cortex_command/pipeline/metrics.py:151 _VERDICT_FIELD_ALIASES (observed-failure provenance for the negative examples)
### 3. Review Artifact FormatL98–129 · 196 tok · 59 wtok · v5MERGE_DEDUPREFUTED · KEEP
rationale
The verdict JSON and drift formats it scaffolds are already fully specified inside the §2 prompt (line 113's HTML comment already defers to §2), and a complete copy of the same artifact scaffold lives in cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/review.md:67-90. Only the Stage-1 per-requirement Expected/Actual/Verdict/Notes scaffold is unique, and no test or parser pins §3's headings — machine parsing targets the JSON block and drift heading defined in §2. Collapsible to the per-requirement scaffold plus a one-line pointer (~100-120 tokens).
Pins: none on §3 itself — 'Requirement 1: {requirement text}' and '### 3. Review Artifact Format' appear in no test/hook/cortex_command consumer; the parsed contracts are pinned via s6
### 4. Process VerdictL130–154 · 450 tok · 135 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The verdict/cycle action table (user-affordance escalation points) and the review_verdict event block (pinned: exactly 1 invocation with canonical --set/--set-json field kinds) must stay verbatim. Line 145 restates the table rows ('cycle counter prevents infinite rework loops... after cycle 2 always escalate') and line 134's re-dispatch quote can tighten; ~35 tokens trimmable without touching pins. The drift-presence check is a distinct fail-loud gate from §4a (different missing section, 1 retry vs 2) — do not fold them.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py FILE_EVENTS: review.md must contain exactly 1 review_verdict invocation with canonical fields/flag-kinds; backlog-writeback.md canonical artifact-registration pointer (already deduped, matches plan.md/refine pattern)
### 4a. Auto-Apply Requirements DriftL155–184 · 552 tok · 166 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Heavily pinned protocol with observed-need provenance (e7f75540, R7 part 1) — heading, three colocated anchors, and the breach event block must survive verbatim. But the exhaustion fall-through is described twice (step 2's final paragraph and step 5 restate the same skip+notice behavior, ~50 tokens) and step 4's report format block over-specifies a trivial display; ~60-70 tokens trimmable while keeping all anchors colocated in §4a. Not an OFFLOAD_CLI candidate: the retry loop re-dispatches an LLM reviewer, which a verb cannot do, and the lifecycle-mechanics offload category is already harvested (#336).
Pins: tests/test_skill_section_citations.py:121 — '### 4a. Auto-Apply Requirements Drift' heading verbatim; tests/test_drift_enforcement_protocol.py — three anchors (Suggested Requirements Update, max-retry 2 regex, drift_protocol_breach) must be colocated in §4a; protocol semantics simulated behaviorally; tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py FILE_EVENTS: exactly 1 drift_protocol_breach invocation with canonical state/suggestion/retries fields; commit e7f75540 'Enforce Suggested Update section on detected drift (R7 part 1)' — observed-need provenance
### 5. TransitionL185–201 · 218 tok · 65 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Three phase_transition event blocks are pinned (roundtrip test expects exactly 3 in review.md with canonical from/to fields); the 'Proceed automatically' line is the documented affordance-removal record from the auto-advance change (cf20e5e5) per CLAUDE.md's boundary-removal rule; the escalation arm preserves the genuine user pause.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py FILE_EVENTS: exactly 3 phase_transition invocations in review.md; commit cf20e5e5 'Auto-advance lifecycle past phase boundaries with no decisions' — deliberate affordance decision record; skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md resume-detection reads review_verdict/escalated states this section emits
## ConstraintsL202–205 · 63 tok · 19 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Two terse, genuine decision criteria: PARTIAL-bias for minor issues and the drift false-positive/false-negative asymmetry (each with its Why). Already minimal. Note (placement, not tokens): these live outside the fenced prompt, so the dispatched reviewer only sees them if the orchestrator embeds them — arguably they belong inside the §2 template.
skills/lifecycle/references/criticality-matrix.md common · ×1 · 1,179 tok · 707 wtok · median v7 · 5 sections
# Criticality Override and Behavior Matrix (title)L1–2 · 11 tok · 7 wtok · v2KEEP
rationale
File title; negligible cost, structural.
## Criticality OverrideL3–14 · 336 tok · 202 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The verb invocation (test-pinned: FILE_EVENTS in test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py expects exactly 1 criticality_override invocation with from/to field-map) and the user-finality affordance are 9-10 anchor content, but the two dense paragraphs explaining gate:'clarify_reconcile' provenance discrimination, consumer field-key analysis, monotonic-up-only ratcheting, and recency supersession (~900B) are Why-narration duplicated in cortex_command/refine.py docstrings and tests/test_refine_module.py R7; a frontier model needs only 'reconcile-clarify emits a gate-marked, up-only override; an explicit user override (ungated, later) always wins'. Provenance: added by 61576839 as design documentation, not an observed-failure fix.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:147 FILE_EVENTS pins exactly 1 criticality_override invocation in this file with from/to fields; cortex_command/refine.py:160,202,215 implements gate clarify_reconcile; tests/test_refine_module.py:636-660 R7 provenance tests
## Criticality Behavior MatrixL15–27 · 312 tok · 187 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The 4-row table is a non-derivable project contract (which criticality forces review, competing plans critical-only) and the heading is cited by SKILL.md:157 manifest and refine SKILL.md:158; the cortex-resolve-model pointer is load-bearing per ADR-0023 single-matrix-owner. But line 26 restates table cells ('always parallel', 'competing plans critical-only') and three rows carry an identical 'Parallel research (sized by fan-out matrix), single plan' cell — collapsible to a footnote. Modest compress; heading verbatim.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:157,191 cites §Criticality Behavior Matrix; skills/refine/SKILL.md:158 'full behavior matrix'; cortex/adr/0023 names this file in the single-matrix-owner constraint; tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py FILE_EVENTS (file-level count is section-agnostic but must not gain event invocations)
## Reading lifecycle stateL28–35 · 208 tok · 125 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Hottest section — 6 citers say 'rules: criticality-matrix.md §Reading lifecycle state' (SKILL.md:100, plan.md:16,161, implement.md:261, specify.md:166), so heading must stay verbatim. The corrupted:true fail-closed rule is a high-value correctness gate; but the criticality/tier reduction bullets narrate what the verb already deterministically implements (common.py reduce_lifecycle_state) — the model never performs the reduction manually, and the defaults are restated inline at SKILL.md:100. Compress the two reduction bullets to one line; keep invocation forms + corrupted rule. Canonicalized here by 13481ffd, so citers must not re-inline the rules.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:100,157; skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md:16,161; skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:261; skills/refine/references/specify.md:166; cortex_command/common.py:665,819 reduce_lifecycle_state corrupted signal
## Seed → reconcile → gate orderingL36–43 · 313 tok · 188 wtok · v7LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
Evidence-backed ordering constraint (silent gate-skip on non-local backends), but the runtime invariant already lives inline at refine SKILL.md:62, which is the sole citer of this section and links here only for 'full rationale' — maintainer-time content. The section rides along on every hot-path load of this common file (Step 2 state reads, plan/implement phase transitions) whose consumers never need the ordering mechanism. Extract to a colder reference (e.g. references/seed-reconcile-ordering.md) and repoint the one refine SKILL.md link; consistent with the #341 physical-extraction precedent (internal reorder saves nothing, extraction does). The content was relocated INTO this file by f2627424 from refine's hot path — this continues that direction, not a reversal.
Pins: skills/refine/SKILL.md:62 links to this file 'under Seed → reconcile → gate ordering'
skills/lifecycle/references/discovery-bootstrap.md common · ×1 · 1,012 tok · 607 wtok · median v6 · 5 sections
# Discovery Bootstrap (title)L1–2 · 6 tok · 4 wtok · v2KEEP
rationale
File title only; negligible cost, serves as the identity anchor for the SKILL.md pointer and <DISCOVERY_BOOTSTRAP_MD> substitution.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:115 pointer to discovery-bootstrap.md; skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:154 <DISCOVERY_BOOTSTRAP_MD> substitution declaration
## Create index.md (New Lifecycle Only)L3–14 · 224 tok · 134 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Post-#326 verb wrapper carrying real non-derivable contracts (verb name+flags, basename-not-path arg, #193-provenance no-re-scan rule at 7ee2d9c8, skip-if-exists safety, backend-blind no --backend). But the 'owns the byte-faithful 7-field template, the backlog-linked vs ad-hoc shapes, and the wikilink body' clause restates verb internals already documented in cortex_command/lifecycle/create_index.py and pinned by tests/test_create_index.py; ~35% compressible without losing any contract.
Pins: cortex_command/lifecycle/create_index.py:5 docstring cites discovery-bootstrap.md as the prose it replaced; tests/test_create_index.py pins the verb contract (golden-byte), not this prose; skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:115 pointer describes 'Create index.md (new lifecycle only)'; provenance: 7ee2d9c8 (#193 single-resolve) added the do-not-re-scan rule for an observed double-resolution failure; fad82d13 (#326) added the verb invocation
## Epic Research DetectionL15–43 · 429 tok · 257 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The 19-line pseudocode block (lines 19-38) is deterministic field-precedence narration a frontier model executes correctly from 3 lines of What+Why (prefer discovery_source over research; record epic_research_path only if the file exists on disk else warn; record epic_spec_path only alongside a recorded research path). The load-bearing parts are the field precedence contract, the initial-commit-provenance 'Do not copy epic content' epic-bleed guard, and the literal discovery_source token (test-pinned); the verbatim announce string at line 42 is droppable. Deliberately NOT proposing OFFLOAD_CLI to avoid re-proposing the harvested #336 offload category; the win is prose compression (~429 to ~180 tokens).
Pins: tests/test_skill_handoff.py + tests/fixtures/skill_handoff_schema.yaml require the literal token 'discovery_source' somewhere in skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md or references/*.md — this file is currently its ONLY occurrence under skills/lifecycle/, so any rewrite must retain the token; tests/test_superseded_frontmatter_tolerance.py:31,132,139 names the 'discovery-bootstrap loader' but tests bin/cortex-resolve-backlog-item, not this prose; provenance: 'Do not copy epic content' guard dates to initial commit 428e54ea (genuine decision criterion, keep); no-re-scan clause from 7ee2d9c8 (#193)
## Epic Context Injection (during /cortex-core:refine delegation)L44–51 · 196 tok · 118 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Output-shape contracts (scope-to-this-ticket instruction, '## Epic Reference' section in research.md, spec.md preamble note) genuinely shape refine's output and are non-derivable; the heading is a verbatim prose anchor for refine-delegation.md:7 and must survive. Intro narration ('This explains the broader epic scope...') is compressible ~25%; only a light trim available.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/refine-delegation.md:7 points to 'the Epic Context Injection protocol' by heading — heading must stay verbatim; '## Epic Reference' output section name is produced-only (no test or consumer greps it) but is the cross-artifact convention
## Refine Starting-Point RulesL52–57 · 157 tok · 94 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The one non-derivable rule is bullet 2: an epic research path in frontmatter is background context, not a substitute — refine must still produce cortex/lifecycle/{slug}/research.md. Bullet 1 ('if both files exist Step 2 proceeds normally') is near-vacuous, and the 'refine's Step 2 (Check State) checks ... at those exact paths' framing partially duplicates behavior #322 offloaded into the cortex-refine resume-point verb; compressible to ~2 sentences (~157 to ~70 tokens) keeping the heading verbatim.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/refine-delegation.md:9 points to 'the Refine Starting-Point Rules' by heading — heading must stay verbatim; adjacent: #322 (complete) moved refine Step-2 state-checking into the cortex-refine resume-point verb, making the exact-path narration here partially redundant
skills/lifecycle/references/post-refine-commit.md common · ×1 · 735 tok · 441 wtok · median v7 · 6 sections
# Post-Refine Commit (title + trigger intro)L1–4 · 29 tok · 17 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Trigger statement locating the procedure at the Specify->Plan boundary; minimal and load-bearing for a file read out of context by refine-delegation Step 6.
Pins: tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py (file must exist + mirror parity); skills/lifecycle/references/refine-delegation.md:25 (reader)
## Flag CheckL5–11 · 100 tok · 60 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Verb name, config path, and false->skip-silently/return-to-Step-3 routing are non-derivable contract; but the default-true/absent-field semantics and 'prints true or false on stdout' narration duplicate behavior cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py already owns and tests pin.
Pins: tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:150 (cortex-read-commit-artifacts token); tests/test_lifecycle_config_commit_artifacts.py (verb default/absent-field behavior)
## StagingL12–28 · 262 tok · 157 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Invocation line, signal routing (nothing_staged->Step 3 resume, staged->Commit), and non-zero-exit halt are essential contract; but line 20's verb-internals paragraph (auto-detected sub-mode, exact staged file list, explicit-paths-never-glob discipline, Context A, spec.md-absent-on-cancel rationale) and the two 'git diff --cached --quiet' equivalences duplicate stage_artifacts.py's docstring and test_stage_artifacts.py's pinned semantics verbatim — residue narration left behind after the #331 offload, not an offload proposal.
Pins: tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:145 (cortex-lifecycle-stage-artifacts token); tests/test_stage_artifacts.py (signal/staged_paths semantics live in the verb); cortex_command/lifecycle/stage_artifacts.py:7 (docstring names this file as caller)
## Commit SubjectL29–37 · 117 tok · 70 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Exact non-derivable subject strings for approval vs cancel, with the spec.md-staged discriminator; becomes the single carrier of the approval/cancel distinction once s3's duplicate narration is trimmed. Cancel token and /cortex-core:commit token are test-pinned.
Pins: tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:153 (/cortex-core:commit token); tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:163 (cancel token)
## Halt-Before-Plan GateL38–41 · 154 tok · 92 wtok · v7COMPRESSREFUTED · KEEP
rationale
The gate itself is an evidence-backed correctness boundary (prevents re-creating the original missing-commit-boundary problem, per 9557cb67 provenance) and the halt token is test-pinned; but the failure-mode enumeration (index lock, hook rejection, conflict), operator recovery-option narration (resolve/re-run/revert), and resume-routing explanation are all derivable by a frontier model, and the halt clause is already stated in the caller (refine-delegation.md:25). MUST is grandfathered pre-#91 language.
Pins: tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:156 (halt token: halt / do not auto-advance); skills/lifecycle/references/refine-delegation.md:25 (duplicate halt clause at call site)
## ConstraintsL42–46 · 75 tok · 45 wtok · v5COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The no-new-event-types bullet is an editor-facing authoring note (enforced by bin/.events-registry.md and events tests), near-zero value to the executing consumer; the no-user-pause note and hand-edits-committed-as-is directive carry real runtime intent but can be tightened. Any rewrite must keep avoiding the literal AskUserQuestion token — the kept-pauses parity scanner rglobs this file and treats that token as a call site (fixed once in 7716fbba).
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py (rglob scan for literal AskUserQuestion token — absence pin, not presence); 7716fbba (parity-scan fix provenance)
skills/lifecycle/references/critical-review-gate.md conditional · ×1 · 649 tok · 389 wtok · median v7 · 4 sections
# Critical Review Gate (title + intro)L1–4 · 61 tok · 37 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Scoping statement that this ref is consulted only on the skip branch after the run/skip condition was evaluated — this framing is what makes the rest of the file's dead-Run-row compression safe, and it orients both consumers (specify.md, plan.md). Cheap at 61 tokens.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:159 (Reference-path propagation manifest entry); skills/refine/references/specify.md:177 + skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md:166 (skip-branch handoff lines; 'critical-review gate protocol' marker is order-asserted by tests/test_critical_review_gate_nonlocal_failsafe.py::test_specify_3b_reads_backend_before_skip_decision)
## Corrupted State RuleL5–8 · 67 tok · 40 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable project contract: the exact '"corrupted": true' key in cortex-lifecycle-state output (cortex_command/common.py:665, output shape pinned by tests/test_reduce_lifecycle_state.py:146-150 and tests/test_bin_lifecycle_state_parity.py) plus the fail-safe posture (untrustworthy tier -> review, not skip). Already terse at 4 lines; the posture sentence is also the anchor s3 mirrors ('mirroring the existing Corrupted State Rule' in the failsafe test docstring).
Pins: cortex_command/common.py:665 (corrupted property — the output contract this rule keys on); tests/test_reduce_lifecycle_state.py:146,150 (pins the '"corrupted":true' CLI output shape); tests/test_critical_review_gate_nonlocal_failsafe.py:13,30,98 (docstring/assertion messages cite 'Corrupted State Rule' as the mirrored posture — prose citation, not an assertion on this heading)
## Non-Local Seed-Tier RuleL9–14 · 304 tok · 182 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Evidence-backed correctness gate (added da962e27 for #317's A-class critical-review finding, spec R8 + Task 11) — the operative condition in line 13 is 9-10 material. But the ~560B wind-up paragraph (line 11) narrating WHY the hole exists (reconcile-clarify had no in-session value, no --backlog-slug fallback, monotonic reducer, simple/medium seed) duplicates the 'Why this hole exists' block in the pin test's own docstring AND specify.md:175's inline fail-safe copy; and the rule is live only on the Plan path, since Specify applies it inline before the gate-ref handoff. Compress to the operative condition + local exemption, keeping the four test-pinned tokens: cortex-read-backlog-backend, 'non-local', 'simple', 'research.md'. Est. 1216B -> ~550B.
Pins: tests/test_critical_review_gate_nonlocal_failsafe.py::test_gate_ref_documents_nonlocal_seed_tier_rule (asserts this file contains 'cortex-read-backlog-backend', 'non-local', 'simple', 'research.md' — token presence only, not the narrative); skills/refine/references/specify.md:175 (inline duplicate of the fail-safe for the Specify path); provenance: da962e27 'Add non-local seed-tier fail-safe to specify §3b gate (#317)'
## Run/Skip MatrixL15–33 · 219 tok · 131 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The cortex-lifecycle-event command block (lines 28-32) is a pinned non-derivable contract (event name + verb + flag shapes; tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py FILE_EVENTS requires exactly 1 lifecycle_critical_review_skipped invocation here with phase/tier/criticality as --set keys) and the skip-silent vs log+skip distinction is the file's core decision content. But the 2x4 matrix table (lines 17-20) and the **Run** paragraph (line 22) are dead at read time — per the intro, this ref is consulted only AFTER the run/skip condition was evaluated on the skip branch, and the Run condition is already stated inline at plan.md:166 and specify.md:171. Compress to the two skip arms + command + <phase> substitution note. Est. 873B -> ~500B.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:150-152 (FILE_EVENTS pins exactly 1 lifecycle_critical_review_skipped invocation in this file; :116-118 pins the {phase,tier,criticality} set-flag key shape); skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md:166 + skills/refine/references/specify.md:171 (inline Run condition — the matrix's Run row duplicates these); provenance: 2a0d61d5 '#330 event verb migration' rewrote the command block; 1771d014 created the file as the extracted shared gate ref
skills/lifecycle/references/refine-delegation.md common · ×1 · 529 tok · 317 wtok · median v7 · 7 sections
Title + delegation trigger introL1–4 · 39 tok · 23 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale

rationale
The <REFINE_SKILL_MD> pointer and the stays-in-sync Why are load-bearing non-derivable wiring; the 'Do not paraphrase or reconstruct... The file read is mandatory' sentences are weak-model defense dating to the initial commit (git -S: 428e54ea) with no observed-failure provenance, low value under the frontier trust bar.

rationale
Conditional routing (epic_research_path recorded) is non-derivable, but Steps 2 and 3 both point at the same <DISCOVERY_BOOTSTRAP_MD> file and name it twice each (placeholder + literal filename); merging into one step reading the file once for both protocols saves ~25 tokens.

Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:154 (manifest: 'read in refine-delegation.md Steps 2–3')

rationale
The lifecycle_start post-critic/post-Q&A timing constraint and the two exact cortex-lifecycle-event invocations are pinned, non-derivable contracts (already verb-offloaded by #330 — no OFFLOAD_CLI left here). But 'per boundary' is stated twice (lines 17 and 21) and '(one JSON object per boundary)' is derivable from the verb; ~30-40 tokens trimmable while keeping the bash fences byte-identical and one line co-locating phase_transition/specify/plan tokens.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py:156 (CANONICAL map: exactly 1 lifecycle_start + 1 phase_transition invocation with exact field-key sets parsed from this file); tests/test_post_refine_commit_wired.py:87-95 (requires a line containing phase_transition+specify+plan; line 21 and line 25 both satisfy)

Trigger timing (after specify→plan or lifecycle_cancelled row, before auto-advance to Plan) and the halt-on-failure ordering are directly test-pinned; the lifecycle_cancelled mention and the 50-line proximity of 'post-refine-commit' to both anchors are hard assertions. skills/lifecycle/references/load-requirements.md common · ×1 · 383 tok · 230 wtok · median v7 · 3 sections
# Tag-Based Requirements Loading (title + intro paragraph)L1–4 · 148 tok · 89 wtok · v5COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Identity plus Why is genuine, but ~half is compressible: the consumer roster ('lifecycle review, refine clarify, discovery clarify/research') is maintenance-prone metadata already enforced by test_six_consumer_references_cite_shared_protocol; 'single source of selection truth', 'this reference covers only how a consumer drives it', and the --help delegation say the same thing twice; the under/over-loading parentheticals amplify an already-clear point. Must retain a 'tag-based ... loading' phrase for _CITATION_RE and (somewhere in file) the verb name.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py::test_load_requirements_md_drives_the_verb requires _CITATION_RE match (r'load-requirements\.md|tag-based.*loading') — satisfied case-sensitively by 'tag-based loading' in line 3, NOT by the title; same test requires 'cortex-load-requirements' somewhere in file (also present in s2 line 7)
## ProtocolL5–10 · 200 tok · 120 wtok · v7COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The invocation-arm distinction (--feature {slug} vs bare), the '(skipped: file absent)' read filter, 'paths only, never contents', and the verbatim path-list injection + fallback-note relay are non-derivable project contracts (review.md's reviewer-prompt template consumes the injected list). But the stdout ordering narration ('project.md first, then Global Context entries in file order, then tag-matched area docs') is pure description of deterministic verb output the consumer takes no order-dependent action on — it is pinned behaviorally in tests/test_load_requirements_cli.py and documented in the verb's --help/docstring, so ~150-200 bytes of step 1 can be cut. Note steps 1-3 are also restated one-line inline by all four hot-path consumers (review.md:12, refine/clarify.md:30, discovery/clarify.md:15, discovery/research.md:25), but that duplication is deliberate post-#333 structure (canonical here, summaries there); do not dedup by deleting this section.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py::test_load_requirements_md_drives_the_verb requires literal '## Protocol' heading and requires '## Matching Semantics' to remain ABSENT; tests/test_load_requirements_cli.py + cortex_command/lifecycle/load_requirements_cli.py pin the actual ordering/suffix/fallback behavior (making the prose restatement of ordering redundant, not load-bearing); tests/test_cortex_load_requirements_parity.py cites this file as protocol-of-record (docstring only, no content assertion)
Glossary-absence consumer rule (trailing paragraph)L11–11 · 36 tok · 22 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Canonical statement of the undefined-glossary-term consumer rule; this file is a designated RULE_CARRIER and the test requires the exact phrases 'absence as a signal' / 'surface the term'. Other carriers (specify.md, discovery clarify/research) exist only because their read paths can skip this protocol — deleting or rewording here breaks the canonicalization done by harness-token-efficiency-trim.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py::test_rule_carriers_carry_consumer_rule_prose — RULE_CARRIERS includes this file; regex r'absence as a signal|surface the term' must match
skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md conditional · ×1 · 626 tok · 188 wtok · median v10 · 2 sections
# Kept user pauses (preamble, lines 1-12)L1–12 · 153 tok · 46 wtok · v5COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Seven lines of self-description (what the file is, who points here, how the parity test works) plus a filler lead-in line 11 that restates the title. The bidirectional-parity contract and test path are already stated in CLAUDE.md's kept-pauses paragraph (always resident) and in skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:187, which is the only route by which this conditional file gets loaded; the parity test parses bullet lines only, so the preamble is not structurally pinned. Compressible to ~2 lines retaining the test path and the one-sentence bidirectional contract.
Pins: CLAUDE.md:57 names the file path and the parity test (pins file existence/path, not preamble wording); skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:171,185-187 link references/kept-pauses.md and cite tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py; tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py:27 pins the file path; _parse_inventory reads bullets only — heading/preamble text is not parsed (the 'section header may have drifted' string is a failure message, not a check)
Inventory bullets (lines 13-22)L13–22 · 474 tok · 142 wtok · v10KEEP
rationale
The payload: 10 parity-tested bullets, each protecting a user affordance (approval/veto pauses) — the top anchor for value 9-10. Bullet format `- file:line — rationale` is parsed by _INVENTORY_BULLET; the 'phase-exit pause' (complete.md:73) and 'conditional pause' (implement.md:37) tags are test-dispatched validation modes, so rationale wording is partially load-bearing. Long parentheticals (plan.md:184, implement.md:37) are exactly the detail needed at the pause-legitimacy consultation moment this conditional file exists for; relocated here by #327 (8c2ec8ce) with anchors actively maintained (8f7af829, 177436e5).
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py _INVENTORY_BULLET regex parses every bullet's file:line anchor (±35-line tolerance); tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py _PHASE_EXIT_PAUSE_TAG requires literal 'phase-exit pause' in the complete.md:73 rationale; tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py _CONDITIONAL_PAUSE_TAG requires literal 'conditional pause' in the implement.md:37 rationale (plus structural marker in implement.md); cortex/adr/0012 and cortex/adr/0004 cite the kept-pauses inventory as the tracked pause record; skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:278 cites the inventory to justify auto-proceed at Implement→Review

rationale
The intro's framing ('during refine delegation, two gates run at phase transitions') duplicates the caller line refine-delegation.md:23, which already names both gates before pointing here; the second sentence ('route based on exit code and stdout') pre-states what the bullets below state concretely. A one-line title-plus-verb sentence suffices.

## Research → Specify gateL5–12 · 165 tok · 99 wtok · v8COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Core is a non-derivable contract: exact verb `cortex-complexity-escalator <feature> --gate research_open_questions` plus the three-way routing policy (escalate-to-Complex on non-empty stdout, proceed on empty, fail-closed halt on non-zero) — keep verbatim. The parenthetical example lists in bullets 2 and 3 (~45 tokens: non-fire causes, remediation examples like 'restore sandbox write permission') are derivable from the CLI's --help/stderr by a frontier model and are the only trimmable fat.
Pins: Gate name research_open_questions and verb pinned by tests/test_complexity_escalator.py, tests/test_cortex_complexity_escalator_parity.py, tests/test_variant_a_writer_sites_baseline.py — pins are on the CLI surface, not this prose; no test greps this file's headings or bullet text; cortex_command/lifecycle/complexity_escalator.py implements the exit-code/stdout contract this section describes
## Specify → Plan gateL13–17 · 67 tok · 40 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Already minimal: carries the second gate's non-derivable flag (specify_open_decisions) and the ordering constraint 'after spec approval, before Specify → Plan' (positions the gate relative to a kept user pause), and dedupes the branching via 'identical to the gate above'. No meaningful compression available without losing the contract.
Pins: Gate name specify_open_decisions pinned by tests/test_complexity_escalator.py and tests/test_cortex_complexity_escalator_parity.py (CLI surface, not this prose)
skills/lifecycle/references/parallel-execution.md rare · ×1 · 633 tok · 63 wtok · median v8 · 2 sections
# Parallel ExecutionL1–15 · 383 tok · 38 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Core contract (Agent isolation:worktree per feature; cortex-worktree-resolve as the single chokepoint; orphaned-branch cleanup on failed manual checkout) is non-derivable and worth keeping, but roughly half the tokens are ruled-out-hypothesis narration: the .mcp.json filename-scoped-deny explanation, the 'Anthropic-aligned repo-relative default that lives under the project's trust scope' rationale, and resolve_worktree_root() internals that the cortex-worktree-resolve script already encapsulates (and whose chokepoint rationale is restated in the hook's own comments at claude/hooks/cortex-worktree-create.sh:29-33). A frontier model needs the directive and the gotcha, not the defense of why alternatives were rejected.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:202 (lazy-ref pointer that already summarizes the core instruction); claude/hooks/cortex-worktree-create.sh:29-33 (chokepoint rationale duplicated in hook comment); tests/test_hooks.sh + tests/test_hooks_resolver_parity.sh (pin the resolver/hook behavior, not this prose); skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:201-204 (overlapping cortex-worktree-resolve cleanup mechanics)
## Worktree Inspection InvariantL16–28 · 250 tok · 25 wtok · v8COMPRESS
rationale
The cd-and-git prohibition (hardcoded, non-bypassable Claude Code security check) and the git log HEAD..worktree/{name} pattern are an evidence-backed correctness gate with initial-commit provenance (428e54ea) — keep. But line 28's 'updatedPermissions session injection: ruled out' paragraph is design-history narration duplicated verbatim in claude/hooks/cortex-worktree-create.sh:74-77, and the '(from claude/hooks/cortex-worktree-create.sh line 30)' cite is stale — branch naming now lives at hook line 47 — so the line number should be dropped, not fixed.
Pins: claude/hooks/cortex-worktree-create.sh:74-77 (same updatedPermissions ruled-out research recorded as a hook comment); skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md:189 (repeats the git log HEAD..worktree/{task-name} inspection pattern); skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:202 (pointer names 'worktree-inspection invariant')
skills/lifecycle/references/wontfix.md rare · ×1 · 593 tok · 59 wtok · median v6 · 3 sections

rationale

Deliberately kept 2 days ago in the #329 thinning commit (818ab5e9, 'Keep the WHEN/WHY triage'). Rare load makes its 13 weighted tokens negligible.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:203 lazy-ref link (file existence enforced generically by tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py — deleting/moving the file breaks that test)
## HowL5–18 · 316 tok · 32 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The verb invocation, --backlog-slug override, ad-hoc no-op, and exit-2 ambiguity contract are non-derivable and worth keeping, but roughly half the section is transition-era narration ('the three steps that used to be a hand-run bash sequence', 'no longer prose you must follow by hand', 'the rationale lives as comments in wontfix_cli.py and back-points to ADR-0004') that duplicates the verb's own docstring/--help and serves no frontier-model consumer. Provenance: 818ab5e9 (#329, 2026-06-29) authored this section when thinning the old hand-run bash sequence — the narration is a leftover explanation of that migration, not an observed-failure guard. No test greps this section's body text; --reason is optional (default='') so there is no E101 contract-lint pin on the fenced invocation.
Pins: cortex_command/lifecycle/wontfix_cli.py:14 docstring cites skills/lifecycle/references/wontfix.md as the human-readable workflow doc (citation survives compression); cortex/adr/0018-structural-lifecycle-invocation-grammar.md:70 references the cortex-lifecycle-wontfix order-enforcing verb (verb name, not this prose)
## Why it lands as it doesL19–21 · 150 tok · 15 wtok · v4MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
The move-first rationale and detector-belt note are duplicated in cortex_command/lifecycle/wontfix_cli.py's module docstring/comments (which s2 itself points to) and ADR-0004, and the ordering is now a code invariant, so this prose is background a consumer never needs to act on; a one-line pointer to wontfix_cli.py/ADR-0004 suffices. Counter-signal factored in: the #329 thinning commit (818ab5e9) explicitly chose to keep the detector-belt note 2 days ago, and common.py:338 cites this file as the convention source — challengeable pins, but the code-side comments already carry the same content, so the dedup direction is prose->code, not deletion of the convention.
Pins: cortex_command/common.py:338 comment cites skills/lifecycle/references/wontfix.md as the terminal-marker convention source; cortex_command/lifecycle/wontfix_cli.py:11 carries the same scan_lifecycle.py:907 archive-skip citation (the duplicate this section would merge into); commit 818ab5e9 (#329) deliberately kept the detector-belt note during the recent thinning

**Session-feature association**L5–12 · 87 tok · 9 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
The echo-to-.session command block is byte-identical to SKILL.md:105 ('Register session' in Step 2), which every lifecycle session already executes on the linear path; this copy adds only the SessionStart-hook provenance of LIFECYCLE_SESSION_ID, a fact the agent never acts on. Duplicated in: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:103-105.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:105 (identical command — the dedup survivor); tests/test_hooks_scan_lifecycle.py:245-262 pins the SessionStart export mechanism, not this prose; tests/_hook_fixture_helpers.py:106,143 pins .session fixture wiring, not this prose
**.session files are ephemeral**L13–14 · 50 tok · 5 wtok · v4COMPRESS
rationale
All three facts are machine-enforced (gitignore blocks committing, SessionEnd hook cleans up per tests/test_hooks.sh:51-83, overwrite-on-resume is inherent); the only agent-facing payload is 'stale .session may exist and is meaningless', which fits in half a sentence. 'Do not commit them' guards against a weak-model misread of an already-gitignored path.
Pins: tests/test_hooks.sh:51-83 pins the SessionEnd cleanup hook behavior (mechanism pin, does not grep this file)
**Listing incomplete features**L15–15 · 79 tok · 8 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
This is the empty-arguments fallback SKILL.md routes to twice (SKILL.md:52,69) and the file's actual reason to exist: it protects the user affordance of choosing which feature to resume (list-and-ask) and names the completion criteria. The criteria clause (feature_complete event / APPROVED verdict) duplicates SKILL.md's phase-table 'complete' row and cortex-common detect-phase semantics, so a light rewrite to 'skip dirs detect-phase reports complete' would be more durable, but at 8 weighted tokens the trim is below action threshold.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:52 and :69 route the empty-args path to this fallback; cortex-common detect-phase (cortex_command) encodes the same completion criteria in its 'complete' route

Refine 4 files · 10,970 weighted tokens on hot paths

Waste: narrating verb internals ('is_file() over spec.md/research.md and prints {...}' - the JSON is self-describing), and the ADR-0019 guard mechanics paragraph ('you do not branch... structurally drops... stays byte-identical to today') which is post-ship change-review residue re-explained again in Step 5. ~180 tok compressible without touching any pinned literal.

But the 3-numbered expansion plus the Sufficient/Insufficient announce-narration can collapse to ~2 sentences; the S2a bypass paragraph stays. ~80 tok compressible.

rationale
Test pins constrain only the anchor phrase, '(Recommended)' within 35 lines of it, a recommend-trigger, rationale-first ordering, and no 'MUST decide' - not the full 1.8KB of label mechanics. The exact lead-label wording, description-opens-with-rationale mechanics, and three parenthetically-defined downsize alternatives are over-prescription a frontier model executes from 'recommendation-first; (Recommended) suffix on lead option; offer downsize alternatives where they apply'. Compress in place (LAZY_REF fights the 35-line proximity pin). ~200 tok.
