## Step 6: CompletionL189–196 · 80 tok · 80 wtok · v3COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 272 pin hits
rationale
A 5-bullet summary checklist a frontier model produces unaided at end of any skill run; 'Announce completion, summarizing artifacts and backlog fields written' covers it in one line. ~55 tok.
## Constraints (Thought/Reality table)L197–204 · 141 tok · 141 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 31 pin hits
rationale
No test pins; dates to the initial commit. Rows 1, 2, and 4 duplicate the intro/when_to_use (stops at spec), s10d line 163 (status:refined after approval), and s5 line 83 (surface cortex-update-item failures); row 3's slug gotcha is the only unique content and is already encoded by the {backlog-filename-slug} placeholder at every call site - keep at most that one row inline.
Pins: none found (grep 'Thought | Reality' in tests/ empty)
skills/refine/references/specify.md common · ×1 · 4,207 tok · 2,524 wtok · median v8 · 15 sections
# Specify Phase (intro) + ## ProtocolL1–6 · 56 tok · 34 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
One-sentence purpose framing plus the Protocol heading; cheap orientation. File-wide note: the whole file is mirrored (tests/test_plugin_mirror_parity.py), so any edit must land canonical+mirror together.
Pins: tests/test_plugin_mirror_parity.py (file-wide mirror parity)
### 1. Load ContextL7–12 · 202 tok · 121 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Doubly test-pinned: must contain the load-requirements citation phrase AND the glossary-absence consumer rule (specify.md is a deliberate RULE_CARRIER because refine's standalone resume-at-spec path skips requirements loading). Path contracts (research.md, lifecycle.config.md) are non-derivable; residual verbosity is small.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py CONSUMER_REFS citation regex 'load-requirements.md|tag-based.*loading'; tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py RULE_CARRIERS regex 'absence as a signal|surface the term' with in-test rationale naming specify.md as deliberate carrier
### 2. Structured Interview (triage rule + six interview areas)L13–38 · 447 tok · 268 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The already-clear/partial/unclear triage, the six area names, the ADR-posture in-the-moment gate (cortex/adr/README.md three-criteria cross-ref), and the AskUserQuestion pause are load-bearing What+Why. But the probe sub-bullets (must-have vs nice-to-have, measurable criteria, user-facing vs internal) and elaborations like 'Challenge optimistic assumptions' are frontier-derivable examples — roughly 130-170 tokens compressible without touching any pinned anchor.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:15 (structured-interview gap-fill AskUserQuestion pause); cortex/adr/README.md three-criteria gate cross-reference in the ADR-posture block
§2 posture sub-blocks (Interview posture / Cadence / File-path citation / Verification posture / Edge-case invention)L39–49 · 364 tok · 218 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Every block here is commit-backed deliberate calibration: interactive-default posture (474d729c, user-directed refine-default-interactive feature), one-question cadence + grounded file-path citation (a810e905), judgmental edge-case invention (1ca0af67). These are exactly the observed-behavior corrections the audit anchors call high-value; already terse.
Pins: commit 474d729c (interactive-default reframe, review-approved feature); commit a810e905 (cadence + file-path citation); commit 1ca0af67 (edge-case invention); skills/interview/references/loop.md canonical-cadence cross-ref
### 2a. Research Confidence Check — missing research.md guardL50–56 · 160 tok · 96 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 89 pin hits
rationale
Defensive corrupted-state guard on a path that refine's resume-point verb already makes near-unreachable (resume-at-spec implies research.md exists); the two long bullets restate the cycle-1 loop-back procedure that s5c already specifies. Compressible to one sentence ('if research.md is missing, announce and run the cycle-1 loop-back below without evaluating C1-C3') preserving the Sufficiency-Check-bypass coupling.
Pins: skills/refine/SKILL.md:100 §2a bypass-case coupling (Sufficiency Check bypass on loop-back)
§2a C1/C2/C3 signals + calibrations + cycle countL57–69 · 422 tok · 253 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
The three pass/fail signals with explicit anti-over-trigger calibration clauses are decision criteria that genuinely gate an expensive loop-back to Research; the clarify §6 non-re-evaluation note and silent-pass rule prevent observed chattiness. Cycle-count formula is mechanical but 1 line.
Pins: commit 0b58e988 lineage (failure-path structuring of §2a)
§2a flagged branches (cycle-1 forced loop-back, cycle-2+ user choice)L70–82 · 224 tok · 134 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Cycle-2+ AskUserQuestion is an inventoried kept user pause; the cycle-1 Sufficiency-Check bypass is a cross-file contract with refine SKILL.md Step 4 (without it refine re-declares stale research sufficient); terse-bullet format is commit-backed against prose expansion.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:16 (cycle-2 confidence-check pause); skills/refine/SKILL.md:100 (bypass case consumer); commit 0b58e988 (terse bulleted signal list structure)
### 2b. Pre-Write ChecksL83–102 · 621 tok · 373 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 116 pin hits
rationale
The research cross-check (silent-omission rule) and the 3-step Open Decision resolution order are high-value project contracts and should stay. The four verification sub-checks are an example catalog under 'verify claims against actual code' — the git two-dot/three-dot and state-ownership bullets read as incident-derived but a frontier model executes the parent rule without the worked examples; ~120-150 tokens compressible. Caution: 'State ownership' text was touched in d4cd8949, a prior token-efficiency trim this section survived, so trim the examples, not the parent rules or the silent-on-pass output contract.
Pins: commit d4cd8949 (section survived a prior deliberate token-efficiency trim)
### 3. Write Specification Artifact — compile + declined-loop-back advisory calloutL103–115 · 209 tok · 125 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 39 pin hits
rationale
The advisory-callout branch fires only when a cycle-2+ confidence check flagged AND the user declined loop-back — a rare compound branch resident on every load; the verbatim 3-line markdown template is derivable from a one-sentence instruction ('prepend an advisory callout naming each flagged signal before ## Problem Statement'). No test pins the callout text; only the confidence_check event-field coupling (action: declined) must survive.
§3 spec.md template (fenced schema)L116–159 · 534 tok · 320 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable output contract: exact section headings, Phase-tag matching rule, binary-checkable criterion format (a/b/c), Changes-to-Existing-Behavior inclusion rule, and the 'None considered.' Proposed-ADR default are all consumed downstream — orchestrator-review checks S1/S6/S7 grade specs against exactly these shapes, and §4's approval surface keys on the 'None considered.' literal.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md S1 (binary-checkable), S6 (## Changes to Existing Behavior), S7 (## Phases + **Phase** tags); internal §4 coupling: Proposed ADRs field is None when body is 'None considered.'
### 3a. Orchestrator ReviewL160–163 · 89 tok · 53 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Gate-before-approval pointer using the ADR-0009-mandated body-resolved-path phrasing; the awkward wording is lint-driven (cortex-check-skill-path), not verbosity.
Pins: cortex/adr/0009-skill-path-resolution-for-plugin-distributed-skills.md; cortex-check-skill-path pre-commit lint
### 3b. Critical ReviewL164–178 · 452 tok · 271 wtok · v8MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 200 pin hits
rationale
Evidence-backed correctness gate (#317 Task 11, R8 hardening) and heavily test-pinned, but the fail-safe paragraph's rationale clauses ('the resume-to-spec signature that Clarify may have been bypassed...', 'its seed is re-sourced from backlog frontmatter...') plus line 171's why-clause near-duplicate critical-review-gate.md:11-13, which the same test pins as the rule's documentation home. Keep the operative trigger+action inline (backend != cortex-backlog AND tier=simple AND research.md exists -> run critical-review) and every pinned token/ordering; dedupe ~150 tokens of rationale to the gate ref. This preserves the site-specific inline arm, so it does not re-open killed idea R1.
Pins: tests/test_critical_review_gate_nonlocal_failsafe.py: '### 3b. Critical Review' heading verbatim; 'cortex-read-backlog-backend' token inside §3b textually BEFORE 'critical-review gate protocol'; '### 4. User Approval' used as section-slice anchor; skills/lifecycle/references/critical-review-gate.md:11-13 (duplicate rule text, test-pinned there too); skills/lifecycle/references/criticality-matrix.md:40 (reconcile-before-§3b ordering note); skills/critical-review/references/residue-write.md:53 (§3b auto-trigger session-bound resolution)

rationale
Inventoried kept user pause (approval surface) with project-specific field contract and the exactly-once spec_approved event invocation pinned by the roundtrip test; the no-emit-on-revision-loops rule is event-correctness, not narration.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:17 (spec approval pause); tests/test_lifecycle_event_roundtrip.py: specify.md must contain exactly 1 canonical spec_approved invocation

# Clarify Phase (intro)L1–4 · 73 tok · 44 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
What/Why framing (pre-research intent gate that aims research, not audits it) — genuinely shapes phase behavior in ~3 lines.
## ProtocolL5–6 · 4 tok · 2 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
Bare structural heading, 2 tokens.
### 1. Resolve InputL7–27 · 414 tok · 248 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 414 pin hits
rationale
Dead on the live path: refine SKILL.md Step 3 directs 'follow its full protocol (§2–§7)' — §1 is never executed from here. SKILL.md Step 1 (lines 27-35) runs the same cortex-resolve-backlog-item command with its own result-branching prose; the two copies even diverge slightly on the no-match arm (clarify.md offers /cortex-backlog:backlog new; SKILL.md says derive kebab slug and proceed), a latent contradiction dedup would resolve. The title-phrase predicate (line 26) narrates the verb's internals, which are verb-owned and test-pinned in the verb's own suite. Only the Context A/B definitions (lines 22-24) are live — SKILL.md:35 cites '§1' for Context B and later sections reference the contexts.
Pins: skills/refine/SKILL.md:35 cross-references 'clarify.md §1' for the Context B definition — a §1 stub with the Context A/B definitions must survive; cortex-resolve-backlog-item exit-code contract is pinned at the verb level (tests/test_resolve_backlog_item.py, tests/test_cortex_resolve_backlog_item_parity.py) — pins the verb, not this prose; provenance: 8268d084 'Lifecycle adopts cortex-resolve-backlog-item; drop refine clarify (#176)' introduced this block when the verb replaced hand-rolled resolution; the SKILL.md duplicate came later
### 2. Load Requirements ContextL28–31 · 105 tok · 63 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 67 pin hits
rationale
Load-bearing pointer to the shared protocol plus the downstream path-list-injection contract, but SKILL.md:66 already restates the protocol pointer at the delegation site; compressible to roughly half while keeping the test-required citation phrase and the inject/relay contract.

### 3. Confidence AssessmentL32–45 · 264 tok · 158 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The three dimension NAMES are the contract; the High/Low description columns of the table are derivable by a frontier model from the names alone (what 'intent clarity: high' means needs no gloss). The prescriptive-ticket-body note (line 42) has observed-failure provenance (f1d66ddb) and must survive verbatim in spirit. Table collapses to a one-line dimension list plus the note, roughly halving the section.
Pins: provenance: commit f1d66ddb deliberately added the prescriptive-ticket-body note (implementation suggestions are hypotheses, not scope) — keep it; no test pins this file's §3 (the 'Confidence Assessment' pin in tests/test_clarify_critic_alignment_integration.py targets clarify-critic.md; test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py targets discovery's clarify.md)
### 3a. Critic ReviewL46–49 · 85 tok · 51 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable contract: sibling-path propagation per ADR-0009, plus the hand-written clarify_critic event site that ADR-0020 explicitly exempts from the event verb. Killed idea R2 covers exactly this event site.
Pins: cortex/adr/0020-lifecycle-event-emission-contract.md exempts clarify_critic from cortex-lifecycle-event (stays hand-written); cortex/adr/0009 skill-path-resolution rationale for the propagated-absolute-path phrasing; tests/test_clarify_critic_alignment_integration.py pins the clarify_critic event shape produced downstream of this instruction
### 4. Question ThresholdL50–57 · 190 tok · 114 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Protects a kept user pause (the low-confidence question batch) — a 9-10 anchor. The ≤5 cap, critic-Ask-first prioritization, and skip-if-all-high gate are project policy, not derivable.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:14 cites skills/refine/references/clarify.md:57; tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py enforces two-way parity on the AskUserQuestion reference at line 54 (±35-line tolerance)
### 5. Produce Clarify OutputL58–84 · 612 tok · 367 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 6 pin hits
rationale
The five-output handoff shape and the canonical complexity/criticality definitions are high-value non-derivable contracts — this file is the only place defining 'high is the appropriate default for most skill and agentic layer changes', which feeds the critical-review gate via reconcile-clarify. But the four verbatim requirements-alignment-note template strings (lines 77-81) are over-prescription a frontier model composes from 'state alignment/partial/none/conflict; resolve conflicts with user', and outputs 1 and 5 carry derivable narration. ~120-150 tokens trimmable without touching the definitions.
Pins: skills/refine/SKILL.md:67-73 lists the same five outputs as 'key outputs from Clarify' (summary form, not verbatim dup); cortex/adr/0019 + skills/lifecycle/references/criticality-matrix.md consume Clarify's computed tier/criticality — the definitions here are the upstream source; 'do not ask the user to confirm' (line 75) is the deliberate anti-pause complement to the kept-pauses inventory
### 6. Research Sufficiency CriteriaL85–99 · 316 tok · 190 wtok · v8LAZY_REFPROVISIONAL · 10 pin hits
rationale
High-value decision contract (signals a-d), but self-describedly 'defined here but applied at Research phase entry, not during Clarify' — it is resident in every Clarify load yet fires at a different step, and on resume=spec the whole 2447-token file is read to recover these 316 tokens. Extracting to its own reference (with a '### 6.' pointer stub per the #340 discipline) makes the resume=spec read ~7x cheaper; fresh-run cost is unchanged.
Pins: skills/refine/SKILL.md:49 and :89 cite 'clarify.md §6' — both cross-refs must be repointed; skills/refine/references/specify.md:65 cites 'clarify.md §6 staleness signals' — repoint; no test pins the §6 heading or (a)-(d) text (grep tests/ hooks/ cortex/adr/ cortex_command/ = 0 hits)
### 7. Write-Backs (Context A only)L100–115 · 317 tok · 190 wtok · v2MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 309 pin hits
rationale
Near-verbatim duplicate of refine SKILL.md Step 3 lines 78-86, which carries the same cortex-update-item invocation, the same none-arm advisory sentence, the same external-backend best-effort arm, the same failure/exit-2 handling — and SKILL.md is by definition already resident whenever this file is loaded, so the duplicate buys nothing. SKILL.md's copy is also better (gates on 'the backend resolved in Step 2' rather than re-resolving cortex-read-backlog-backend). This is NOT killed idea R1: R1 killed cross-skill dedup into a canonical reference that forces an extra Read; here the surviving copy is in this skill's own always-loaded body and no new Read is introduced.
Pins: no structural test pins this file's §7 — the backend-gate suite (test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py, test_decompose_index_backend_gate.py, test_complete_index_sync_gate.py, test_morning_review_failed_feature_gate.py) targets other files; test_refine_reconcile_clarify.py:253 pins skills/refine/SKILL.md, whose copy survives; cortex-check-contract prose-invocation lint: the SKILL.md copy already carries the required {placeholder} flags; deleting this copy removes the token entirely (safe per E101/E103 semantics)
## ConstraintsL116–122 · 67 tok · 40 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Phase-boundary contract (deep interview belongs to Specify, feasibility to Research) — prevents scope bleed between gates; already terse.

### 1. Load ContextL7–12 · 213 tok · 128 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale

rationale

rationale

### 2. Per-ticket capture fields (Title/Priority/Type/Size/Dependencies)L40–47 · 142 tok · 85 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Compact field list; priority-derivation rule and dependencies-from-How-they-connect are project-specific decision criteria not recoverable from code. Already terse.
### 3. Consolidation ReviewL48–58 · 336 tok · 202 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 11 pin hits
rationale
The wrong-set-vs-over-split routing decision is genuine What/Why, but this is the third in-file statement of the piece-set-ownership invariant (after §1/§2, before §4/§5/§6/Constraints). Test pins are narrow keyword phrases ('wrong'+'return to the research phase', 'right but over-split', '§4 grouping'/'§4 owns this'); the elaboration around them is unpinned and compressible ~50%. Counter to R6 kill: pins verified line-by-line — only the named literals are anchored, not the paragraphs; and this audit's load model rates the file common@0.6, not rarest-path.
Pins: tests/test_decompose_rules.py::test_grouping_section_3_distinguishes_wrong_set_from_over_split; tests/test_decompose_rules.py::test_decompose_body_omits_removed_research_phase_r3_gate (negative pin: 'research-phase R3' must stay absent); cortex/adr/0007 (names §3 prose in its reversal-cost inventory)

rationale

Compress those two clauses to pointers, ~30% savings, keeping 'consolidate-pieces', 'merge convention', 'cortex-check-prescriptive-prose', 'pre-commit'.

### 5. LEX-1 regex specification (patterns + section-boundary detection)L99–108 · 292 tok · 175 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 43 pin hits
rationale
The exact regexes and boundary-detection algorithm duplicate the authoritative implementation in cortex_command/lint/prescriptive_prose.py (PATH_LINE_RE, SECTION_INDEX_RE, SECTION_HEADING_RE, FENCE_RE) — the prose itself admits 'baked into this prose'. The authoring agent needs only the semantic rule (path:line and §N citations live only in Touch points), already stated in §2 and Constraints; enforcement is the scanner, which fails loud at pre-commit. Keep the pinned Forbidden/Permitted-sections lines (105) verbatim; delete patterns 1-3 and the boundary paragraph. Counter to R6 kill: verified the regex block is NOT test-anchored — test_decompose_rules pins only 'Forbidden sections'/'Permitted section' + header literals + scanner name, none of the pattern lines.
Pins: tests/test_decompose_rules.py::test_prescriptive_prose_check_section_partitioning ('Forbidden sections', 'Permitted section', '## Role', '## Integration', '## Edges', '## Touch points' — all satisfiable by line 105 alone); cortex_command/lint/prescriptive_prose.py (authoritative source the block duplicates)
### 5. LEX-1 worked examples (6 PASS/FLAG cases) + scanner-timing noteL109–119 · 297 tok · 178 wtok · v2COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 137 pin hits
rationale
Six PASS/FLAG/anti-pattern examples teach a frontier model where a regex matches — weak-model guarding; the scanner fails loud at write time and pre-commit, so a mis-authored body cannot land silently. None of the example strings are test-pinned ('pre-commit' pin at line 118 is already satisfied by line 97). Compress to one FLAGS + one PASSES pair, ~70% savings. Counter to R6 kill: examples are unpinned (verified against test_decompose_rules.py) and the loud-failure enforcement makes prose redundancy safe to cut.
Pins: none on the example lines; 'pre-commit' (line 118) duplicates line 97's pinned occurrence

### 4. Write Research Artifact (template)L68–127 · 487 tok · 292 wtok · v8COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 4 pin hits
rationale
The template headings are the most-pinned contract in the file (parser, three test files, ADR-0007, decompose.md, and SKILL.md's R4 revise loop re-walks this template) and must stay verbatim; but the 3-example per-claim marker block (lines 84-87, commit 58f7c4bc) and the Architecture HTML comment restate the marker/merging rules already carried by Constraints and the template comments — ~110 tokens of the section are commentary, not contract.
Pins: cortex_command/discovery.py (Architecture extractor); tests/test_discovery_research_sizing.py _TASK10_RESEARCH_MD (deliberate restatement of the shipped template); tests/test_discovery_gate_presentation.py (requires '### Pieces' + '### How they connect' + 'references/research.md' tokens in SKILL.md; bans drifted vocabulary); tests/test_decompose_rules.py (§2 consumes '### Pieces'); cortex/adr/0007-decompose-groups-pieces-into-tickets.md; skills/discovery/SKILL.md R4 revise loop (re-walks 'the live template in references/research.md §6' — note existing §6-vs-§4 numbering drift)
### 4a. Orchestrator ReviewL128–131 · 52 tok · 31 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable gate ordering (orchestrator review passes before critical review, before commit/user presentation); the protocol itself is already lazily deferred to references/orchestrator-review.md.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md (Post-Research checklist; 'always runs' rule); skills/discovery/SKILL.md line 76 (body-resolved orchestrator-review path)
### 4b. Critical ReviewL132–135 · 41 tok · 25 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Minimal gate invocation, already terse; ordering after 4a is the contract.
### 5. TransitionL136–139 · 39 tok · 23 wtok · v3COMPRESS
rationale
Contains a stale phase name: 'proceed to Specify' — discovery's next phase is Decompose via SKILL.md's Research→Decompose approval gate (spec.md is backward-compat only per SKILL.md line 47). A clarity-harm contradiction of the #342 kind: the commit instruction is fine, the transition target is wrong.
Pins: skills/discovery/SKILL.md 'Research → Decompose approval gate (spec R4)' (the actual transition owner this line contradicts)
## ConstraintsL140–147 · 180 tok · 108 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Read-only/all-findings-in-artifact/scope are real behavioral gates; the Citations and Empty-corpus bullets are the canonical single statement of the marker contract (commit a7692f2a, lifecycle 138 codify-citation-norm) and should be the keep-side of the s14 dedup.
Pins: commit a7692f2a (lifecycle 138 Task 1 provenance); tests/fixtures/discovery-brief/*/research.md (fixtures exercise the markers as content)
### Signal formatsL148–155 · 114 tok · 68 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Third restatement of the same three markers already fully specified (with exact syntax) in the Constraints bullets two paragraphs above and exemplified in the §4 template; grep of skills/, tests/, cortex_command/, hooks/ shows NO downstream code or skill parses these markers (decompose.md never mentions them), so the 'stable contract for downstream consumers' framing has no consumer to break. Commit ce24f28e (same lifecycle 138).
Pins: commit ce24f28e (lifecycle 138 provenance — deliberate spec task, but duplicative within the same file)
skills/discovery/references/clarify.md common · ×1 · 2,189 tok · 1,314 wtok · median v7 · 11 sections
# Clarify Phase (intro)L1–6 · 100 tok · 60 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Purpose framing (aims research before it runs) plus the ad-hoc no-backlog-item property genuinely orient the phase; concise. The ad-hoc statement is the canonical copy that s2 and s11 duplicate.
## Protocol / ### 1. Resolve InputL7–12 · 40 tok · 24 wtok · v2MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
§1's only content restates line 5 verbatim in different words (no backlog item; discovery creates them) — the same fact also appears a third time in the Constraints table row 2. Collapse §1 to nothing or a single clause under the Protocol heading.
### 2. Load Requirements ContextL13–18 · 166 tok · 100 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 66 pin hits
rationale
The cortex-load-requirements citation and the discovery-specific omit-`--feature` arm are non-derivable contract; but the sentence paraphrases the protocol steps that load-requirements.md itself states when read, and line 17's glossary-rule sentence duplicates the canonical copy in load-requirements.md — the pinning test's own RULE_CARRIERS comment says the discovery copies 'remain pending their own trim tasks'.
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py CONSUMER_REFS citation regex (load-requirements.md|tag-based.*loading); tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py RULE_CARRIERS prose regex (absence as a signal|surface the term) — challengeable; test comment anticipates discovery-copy trim with RULE_CARRIERS update
### 3. Check Existing Backlog CoverageL19–27 · 256 tok · 154 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Evidence-backed correctness gate (#321 R7, commit 4f0d888c): two-arm backend gate with verb name, ordering constraint, skip-arm advisory, and ADR-0016 back-pointer, all structurally pinned by a discriminating test with a negative control. The killed idea R1 already ruled out deduping backend-routing blocks into a canonical reference.
Pins: tests/test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py: heading '### 3. Check Existing Backlog Coverage' verbatim, 'cortex-read-backlog-backend' before scan glob 'cortex/backlog/[0-9]*-*.md', 'disabled for this repo' skip arm, 'two arms' divergence note, 'ADR-0016' back-pointer
### 4. Confidence AssessmentL28–38 · 206 tok · 124 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The four dimension names (topic aim, domain, novelty, requirements alignment) are the load-bearing decision criteria; the High/Low confidence column prose is derivable elaboration a frontier model reproduces from the dimension names alone. Collapse the table to a one-line dimension list (~120 token save). No pins — the '## Confidence Assessment' test anchor targets refine's clarify-critic.md H2 heading, not this H3.
### 5. Question ThresholdL39–44 · 87 tok · 52 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Concise behavioral contract: ≤4 questions, don't re-ask the obvious, wait for answers, skip entirely on all-high confidence. Already near-minimal.
### 6. Produce Clarify Output (outputs 1-4)L45–60 · 262 tok · 157 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The named handoff outputs and the conflict-resolve-with-user veto (a user affordance) must stay; the worked example sentence in output 1 and the four enumerated alignment-note template strings are prescriptive filler a frontier model composes from 'state alignment/partial/none/conflict' (~60-80 token save).
### 6 outputs 5-6: research-sizing complexity + criticalityL61–68 · 508 tok · 305 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 227 pin hits
rationale
Keep the non-derivable policy (sizing-only-not-implementation distinction, skew-complex, criticality floors at medium — floor is code-pinned in cortex_command/discovery.py:1053-1058). Cut the upward-bias rationale stated twice nearly verbatim (once per output) and the fanout-plumbing narration: clarify only PRODUCES the values; research.md §2 (the actual fanout consumer) already carries the identical body-propagated-path sentence, so a bare 'feeds Research's fan-out sizing' clause suffices (~200+ token save).
Pins: cortex_command/discovery.py:1053-1058 (floor default {complexity:simple, criticality:medium}, 'floors at medium' comment); skills/discovery/references/research.md:41 carries the duplicate fanout propagated-path sentence (the consumer-side copy)
### 6 output 7: Scope envelopeL69–74 · 159 tok · 95 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Useful optional affordance, but the closing 'Fire when boundaries are tractable; skip when they are not' restates the two sentences immediately above it, and the in/out bullet structure is derivable. Halvable (~70 token save). Provenance d4357268 shows a deliberate addition, not an observed-failure fix.
### Persist the research-sizing assessmentL75–86 · 198 tok · 119 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Producer side of a real cross-phase contract: exact verb + flags (cortex-discovery emit-research-sizing --topic/--complexity/--criticality), event name discovery_research_sizing, and the phase-resume Why. Research reads it back via read-research-sizing; conversation memory does not survive independent phase entry.
Pins: cortex_command/discovery.py (emit-research-sizing parser + discovery_research_sizing event); tests/test_discovery_research_sizing.py (event survives clarify→research resume)
## ConstraintsL87–93 · 207 tok · 124 wtok · v2MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 40 pin hits
rationale
All three Thought/Reality rows restate content already in this same file: row 1 re-explains the sizing-vs-implementation distinction already stated in BOTH outputs 5 and 6 (making it a triplication); row 2 restates the ad-hoc no-backlog-item fact from lines 5 and 11 (also a triplication); row 3 is derivable from the intro's 'aims research before it runs'. No test, hook, ADR, or cortex_command pin on any row.
skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md common · ×1 · 857 tok · 514 wtok · median v9 · 4 sections
# Orchestrator Phase Review (Discovery) — intro + no-skip ruleL1–6 · 113 tok · 68 wtok · v6MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Line 5 (discovery has NO applicability skip) is a high-value override of the canonical's Applicability section and must stay. Line 3's purpose paragraph is a verbatim duplicate of skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md line 3, and the Protocol section mandates reading that canonical file in the same pass, so the paragraph is always co-resident twice. Provenance: commit c0439253 (2026-06-10) already deduped ~65 lines of shared protocol out of this file but left this paragraph.
Pins: tests/test_dual_source_reference_parity.py:52 (discovery dir mirror parity — process pin, requires mirror regen on edit); tests/fixtures/discovery-brief/complex-topic/research.md:40 (inert fixture citing stale line numbers in this file)
## ProtocolL7–13 · 208 tok · 125 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The deliberate discovery-specific delta shipped by drift-pair dedup commit c0439253: pointer to the lifecycle canonical plus two non-derivable substitutions (exact cortex/research/{topic}/{artifact} path swap in the Fix Agent Prompt Template; plain-prose return envelope vs lifecycle's YAML). Losing either substitution silently breaks fix dispatch paths or envelope parsing.
Pins: skills/discovery/SKILL.md:76 (body-resolved propagation line this section cites, ADR-0009 pattern); skills/discovery/references/research.md:130 (§4a directs read-and-follow of this file); tests/test_dual_source_reference_parity.py:52 (mirror parity process pin)
## Checklists / ### Post-Research ChecklistL14–27 · 314 tok · 188 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The actual review rubric (R1-R5) — project-specific decision criteria naming exact research.md section anchors (Codebase Analysis, Web Research, Open Questions, Web & Documentation Research) and encoding the research→decompose deferral contract (R4). Also the ~1KB block pasted into fix-agent prompts when an item flags. Not derivable from surrounding context; this is the substitution target the Protocol section points at.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/research.md:130 (§4a consumption); tests/test_dual_source_reference_parity.py:52 (mirror parity process pin)
## ConstraintsL28–35 · 223 tok · 134 wtok · v4MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 40 pin hits
rationale
Four anti-rationalization Thought/Reality rows guarding against motivated shortcuts a frontier model is trusted not to take. Row 1 duplicates the canonical's step-2 'Evaluate the artifact against every item' instruction and row 4 duplicates the canonical's Cycle Cap section ('Do not start cycle 3 — escalate', line 133) — both co-read on every run per the Protocol pointer. Rows 2-3 are phase-generic (apply equally to lifecycle) and belong once in the canonical's Constraints table, which currently holds only a skip-rule row that is itself inapplicable to discovery; row 2 ('orchestrator does not edit phase artifacts directly') also tensions with the canonical Fix Dispatch same-conversation mode ('revise the artifact in the current context'), a clarity harm consolidation would force resolving. No test/hook/ADR/cortex_command pins on any row string (greps empty).
Pins: tests/test_dual_source_reference_parity.py:52 (mirror parity process pin only — no content pin found for any Constraints row)

skills/critical-review/references/reviewer-prompt.md always · ×4 dispatch · 998 tok · 3,992 wtok · median v8 · 9 sections
# Per-Angle Reviewer Prompt Template (header/meta, lines 1-9 incl. --- separator)L1–9 · 92 tok · 368 wtok · v4COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 17 pin hits
rationale
Orchestrator-only header (load notes: only the body after '---' is embedded into reviewers, so its true multiplier is 1, not 4). Its five-placeholder enumeration duplicates skills/critical-review/SKILL.md:60 verbatim; 7 lines of explanation compress to ~2 while keeping the '---' body-start boundary the orchestrator splits on.
Pins: skills/critical-review/SKILL.md:60 (duplicate enumeration of the same 5 substitution slots — the dedup target, not a blocker)
(preamble) You are conducting an adversarial review of one specific angle.L10–12 · 17 tok · 68 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
One-line role framing that sets the adversarial posture for the whole reviewer; minimal cost.
## Artifact (path/SHA + READ_OK/READ_FAILED sentinel contract)L13–23 · 203 tok · 812 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Wire protocol: sentinel lines are parsed by column-0 regexes in cortex_command/critical_review/__init__.py:393-394 with OK-first precedence and a 50-line window; the relaxed 'on its own line ... before the first ## heading' wording was a deliberate parser-alignment change (commit 9e333616, sentinel-gate-relax lifecycle / #229). Only slack is the doubled 'Read the literal absolute path' sentence on line 18 (~23 tokens, covered by the file-compress aggregate).
Pins: cortex_command/critical_review/__init__.py:393-394 (_REVIEWER_OK_RE/_REVIEWER_FAILED_RE column-0 regexes); tests/test_critical_review_sentinel_window.py (window/precedence/anchor behavior the prompt wording must match); tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py:165-173 Req 10(c) (verbatim 'READ_OK: <path> <sha>' pinned in SKILL.md, which mirrors this contract); commit 9e333616 (provenance: 'Align reviewer prompts with relaxed sentinel parser contract')
## Project Context (substitution slot)L24–26 · 28 tok · 112 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Substitution slot with an omit-if-empty conditional the orchestrator executes at dispatch; non-derivable contract, 3 lines.

## Your Angle (substitution slot)L27–29 · 28 tok · 112 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Angle-name/description substitution slot; the per-angle isolation is the skill's core mechanism.

## Finding ClassesL30–39 · 232 tok · 928 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The A/B/C taxonomy plus the fix_invalidation_argument requirement drive the entire downstream classifier/rubric system; the B-class worked example text is echoed as the canonical adjacent case in a-to-b-downgrade-rubric.md Example 5, and Trigger 1 of the rubric fires on the line-38 requirement. 'Multi-class tags are prohibited' duplicates line 42 (~15 tokens, file-compress aggregate).
Pins: tests/test_critical_review_classifier.py (envelope class semantics, Trigger 1/2/3 fixtures); skills/critical-review/references/a-to-b-downgrade-rubric.md (Trigger 1 on fix_invalidation_argument; Example 5 reuses the B-class worked example); skills/critical-review/SKILL.md:60 (class names A fix-invalidating / B adjacent-gap / C framing quoted verbatim)
### Straddle ProtocolL40–43 · 77 tok · 308 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
Split-vs-bias-up semantics are directly load-bearing for the synthesizer's Trigger-3 Straddle exemption, isolated as the single load-bearing variable in a matched fixture pair in the classifier tests. Final sentence duplicates line 32's prohibition (file-compress aggregate).
Pins: tests/test_critical_review_classifier.py:506-511,704-706 (straddle_rationale as sole variable; exemption preempts Trigger 3); skills/critical-review/references/a-to-b-downgrade-rubric.md:13 (Straddle exemption clause)
## Instructions (+ literal prose findings format: What's wrong / Assumptions at risk / Convergence signal)L44–61 · 141 tok · 564 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The three prose-format headings have zero mechanical consumers (grep of tests/, cortex_command/, hooks/ finds no parser or pin on 'What's wrong'/'Assumptions at risk'/'Convergence signal'); the prose block only needs to exist as the malformed-envelope fallback payload (synthesizer-prompt.md:27,55). The scaffolding — numbered steps restating the Read+angle focus, bracketed placeholder descriptions — compresses ~40% for a frontier reviewer; keep the anti-hedging lines and the cite-exact-text bar, and keep at least one '## ' heading after the sentinel (the s3 contract references the first '## ' heading as the sentinel deadline).
Pins: skills/critical-review/references/synthesizer-prompt.md:27,55 (untagged prose blocks from malformed-envelope reviewers are a consumed fallback — prose emission itself must survive); line 20 (s3) sentinel-before-first-'## '-heading clause assumes a heading-bearing findings body follows
(JSON envelope spec) <!--findings-json--> delimiter + schema exemplarL62–77 · 183 tok · 732 wtok · v9COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Delimiter and field names are exact wire contract (verification-gates.md:65-66 last-occurrence regex + json.loads schema assert; classifier fixtures replicate the field set byte-for-byte). The only compressible tokens are the two long optional-field description strings, which restate line 38 and the Straddle Protocol nearly verbatim (~40 tokens); shorten to pointer-style descriptions while keeping keys and delimiter exact.
Pins: skills/critical-review/references/verification-gates.md:65-66 (delimiter regex + schema: class/finding/evidence_quote/straddle_rationale/fix_invalidation_argument); tests/test_critical_review_classifier.py (envelope field names byte-for-byte in fixtures); cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py:63, cortex_command/overnight/tests/test_orchestrator_round.py:97 (same last-occurrence delimiter convention family)

frontmatterL1–14 · 324 tok · 324 wtok · v8KEEP

# Critical Review (intro)L15–19 · 72 tok · 72 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Compact statement of mechanism plus the no-anchoring Why (fresh reviewers, no exposure to the reasoning that produced the artifact) — this Why shapes every downstream dispatch decision.
## Contents (TOC)L20–26 · 59 tok · 59 wtok · v1DELETEPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
A 4-entry table of contents for a 114-line file the orchestrator reads in full; pure navigation boilerplate with zero runtime function. critical-review is the ONLY skill in skills/ with a TOC; no test, hook, or lint requires a '## Contents' section or the anchor slugs.
## Step 1: Find the ArtifactL27–30 · 78 tok · 78 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable artifact-precedence contract (plan.md -> spec.md -> research.md) plus the ask-before-proceeding affordance. Already terse.
## Step 2 / ### Step 2a: Load Domain ContextL31–41 · 337 tok · 337 wtok · v4OFFLOAD_CLIPROVISIONAL · 58 pin hits
rationale
Four numbered steps of deterministic file parsing/assembly: read project.md Overview (~250 words), validate lifecycle.config.md 'type:' (present/non-empty/uncommented), extract glossary '## Language' verbatim, assemble-or-omit the '## Project Context' block. This is mechanical extraction that fits the existing cortex-critical-review verb family (e.g. a project-context subcommand, or fold into prepare-dispatch's stdout JSON); the skill body then keeps one sentence of What+Why. Not a #336 re-proposal: that harvest covered lifecycle mechanics, and critical-review already has its own CLI (cortex_command/critical_review/) so this extends an established pattern. No pinned strings live in these lines (the pinned exemption anchor is the adjacent blockquote, s6); provenance 3af9a2a1 wired the glossary step.
> Requirements loading: deliberately exempt (blockquote)L42–43 · 193 tok · 193 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 25 pin hits
rationale
Deliberate-decision record ('do not fix this exemption') with real anti-regression value and a test-pinned anchor phrase, but the rationale runs ~120 words where ~60 carry it; the vocabulary-is-definitional aside and the parenthetical enumeration are compressible while keeping the anchor substring verbatim. Provenance: 5f9697f1 (requirements-skill-v2 R5).
Pins: tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py:134 (anchor substring 'Requirements loading: deliberately exempt' must appear in SKILL.md)

rationale
Non-derivable verb contract (cortex-critical-review prepare-dispatch, placeholder binding, halt-on-nonzero) already trimmed to gate-first pointer form by #300; the pointer at line 48 is structurally pinned.

Decision criteria themselves are genuine What+Why and stay.

rationale
Dispatch contract is load-bearing and multiply pinned, but the trailing clause summarizing what reviewer-prompt.md instructs (class-tag scheme, envelope) duplicates a reference the orchestrator reads verbatim at dispatch time; can tighten ~40 tokens while preserving the pinned 'READ_OK: <path> <sha>' literal and both counted placeholders.

rationale
Largest section; carries several 9-grade contracts (resolve-model no-criticality/no-state rule from #334, halt-on-nonzero, placeholder set, SYNTH_READ_OK sentinel) but line 78 (~100 tokens) restates the rubric-sourcing already fully specified in line 76, and line 76's explanatory asides ('exactly as you substitute...', the skill-dir-path Why that is canonical in CLAUDE.md/ADR-0009) are compressible. ~120-150 tokens recoverable with every pinned literal preserved. ### Step 2e: Residue WriteL88–93 · 172 tok · 172 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 137 pin hits
rationale
The trigger condition (B-class findings -> sidecar for morning report, skip-silent-when-zero) is load-bearing, but the .session-file {feature} resolution mechanics and the 'tempfile + os.replace atomic rename' internals narrate what cortex-critical-review-write-residue and references/residue-write.md (line 92 pointer) already own; ~60 tokens recoverable by deferring mechanics to the existing pointer.
## Step 3: PresentL94–97 · 22 tok · 22 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Two-sentence anti-softening directive protecting the adversarial output from editorializing; negligible cost.
## Step 4: Apply FeedbackL98–114 · 339 tok · 339 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Apply/Dismiss/Ask criteria + anchor-checks + the empirical-measurement rule (deliberate provenance 2fed8ab6) are genuine decision criteria and stay; the presentation micro-prescription (mandated six-verb list for bullet openers, exact Dismiss-count-line format, consolidation formatting) is How a frontier model derives from 'compact summary: direction-of-change bullets, dismissal count, consolidated asks' — ~70-90 tokens recoverable; no test pins any Step 4 string.
skills/critical-review/references/verification-gates.md always · ×1 · 2,610 tok · 2,610 wtok · median v8 · 10 sections
# Verification Gates (Step 2c.5 and Step 2d.5) — Implementation Detail (preamble)L1–8 · 97 tok · 97 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
The route-through-canonical-subcommands / never-append-events.log-inline policy is the file's core anti-double-emission gate; grandfathered MUSTs explicitly left unchanged by 55dd8d42 ('Preamble MUST/MUST-NOT lines unchanged'). Small enough that compression yields <30 tokens.

## Step 2a.5: Pre-Dispatch (atomic path + SHA pin)L9–28 · 336 tok · 336 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 149 pin hits

The invocation, stdout schema, bind names, and exit-2 reaction are non-derivable contract, but SKILL.md:46 already carries invocation+bind+substitute+stop verbatim, and line 17 narrates the obvious ('is the candidate artifact path resolved in Step 1') while line 18 re-explains the $LIFECYCLE_SESSION_ID resolver that the Step 2e residue-write reference canonically owns. ~120 tokens compressible while keeping the pinned heading and pinned exit-2 sentence byte-exact.

## Step 2c.5: Sentinel-First Verification Gate (intro, lines 29-34)L29–34 · 118 tok · 118 wtok · v4MERGE_DEDUPPROVISIONAL · 6 pin hits
rationale

## Atomic WriteL21–28 · 98 tok · 59 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 120 pin hits
rationale

rationale
READ_OK/READ_FAILED sentinel emission rules are the wire protocol parsed by cortex_command.critical_review.check_artifact_stable (column-0 regexes, 50-line window, before-first-## placement) — non-derivable contract with observed-failure provenance (commit 9e333616 aligned prompts with the relaxed parser). Duplicates reviewer-prompt.md:13-22 but each template must be self-contained for standalone embedding on the fallback path.

## Commit Message FormatL19–40 · 120 tok · 1,080 wtok · v6COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
Four of the rules (capital, no trailing period, imperative mood, blank line before body) are structurally enforced by hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh which denies with the reason, so the prose only saves a retry round-trip; the template code block is derivable. The genuinely non-enforced judgment lines (summarize the why, skip body when self-evident, 72-char wrap) are the kernel worth keeping (~half the tokens).
Pins: hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh:57-88 enforces the same four rules structurally (duplicate, not a prose pin)
## Commit CommandL41–53 · 88 tok · 792 wtok · v5COMPRESSVERIFIED
rationale
The two `git commit -m` example blocks narrate the obvious; the HEREDOC/command-substitution prohibition (sandbox temp-file failure mode) and the dangerouslyDisableSandbox prohibition are the load-bearing, non-derivable half. Present since initial commit 428e54ea (grandfathered); the hook itself parses HEREDOC so the prohibition is sandbox-motivated, not validation-motivated — keep the two prohibition sentences, delete the examples (~45 tokens).
Pins: hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh:26 parses HEREDOC-format commits (tolerates the format the prose forbids)
## ValidationL54–57 · 41 tok · 369 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 14 pin hits
rationale
The do-not-bypass instruction protects the PreToolUse correctness gate, but two sentences can fold into the Workflow constraints line ('a PreToolUse hook validates the message; fix rejections, never bypass') saving ~20 tokens with no content loss.
Pins: hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh is the hook this section describes
## Release-type markersL58–78 · 406 tok · 3,654 wtok · v4LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
45% of the file, resident on every one of ~9 loads per feature run, but only release-cut commits (rare branch) need the regex, precedence, BREAKING: fallback semantics, and pre-merge --dry-run procedure — all of which duplicate contracts owned by cortex_command/auto_bump_version.py:33-48. Keep a 2-line pointer carrying the valuable kernel (marker token + must-be-own-line anchor + patch default), move the rest to skills/commit/references/release-type.md. Added in f79089b2 (PR #10) as helper documentation, not as a fix for an observed model failure; no test pins this prose.
Pins: cortex_command/auto_bump_version.py:33-48 owns MARKER/BREAKING regexes (code is source of truth); tests/test_cortex_auto_bump_version.py + parity test pin the helper, not this prose; docs/release-process.md:124 references the [release-type: minor] pattern independently
### ExamplesL79–99 · 77 tok · 693 wtok · v2LAZY_REFVERIFIED

Two worked examples that only illustrate the own-line marker rule already stated once; pure derivable filler on the hot path. Moves with s7 into references/release-type.md.

## Review CriteriaL20–26 · 82 tok · 328 wtok · v4MERGE_DEDUPREFUTED · KEEP
rationale
Three of the four bullets duplicate CLAUDE.md Conventions nearly verbatim (valid-JSON settings, chmod +x scripts, name+description frontmatter) and the fourth restates CLAUDE.md Distribution's no-host-symlinks rule; every consumer session in this repo already carries CLAUDE.md. The heading is the review phase's designated injection point, so keep the heading with only genuinely non-CLAUDE.md criteria (currently: none unique). No test pins the runtime instance's bullets — the parity gate (ADR-0017, tests/test_lifecycle_config_parity.py) binds only asset/template frontmatter.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:32 (generic mention that the config contains review criteria); skills/lifecycle/assets/lifecycle.config.md:35 + cortex_command/init/templates/cortex/lifecycle.config.md (heading exists in scaffold placeholder, content differs)
## Branch Mode (intro)L27–32 · 177 tok · 708 wtok · v2LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
Operator documentation for the branch-mode field, added with the feature itself (commit d012511e); the behavior it narrates is fully enforced by cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py + lifecycle_implement.py and the cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode verb. This repo sets branch-mode: prompt (explicitly 'equivalent to leaving the field unset'), so no runtime consumer ever needs this prose; it costs 4 reads per lifecycle. Relocate to docs (docs/overnight-operations.md:717 already hosts the consumed-but-unscaffolded branch-mode note) and leave a one-line comment on the frontmatter field.
Pins: no test/hook pins the runtime-instance body; behavior pins live in tests/test_lifecycle_config.py and tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py (against code, not this file); docs/overnight-operations.md:717 (consumed-but-unscaffolded exception note — natural relocation target)
### Values (closed set)L33–39 · 146 tok · 584 wtok · v3LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
The closed set is a real contract but it is enforced fail-safe in code (invalid value -> stderr warning naming the rejection + picker fires, tests/test_lifecycle_config.py cases) and each value's semantics are documented in ADR-0004/ADR-0008 and implement.md's routing block (test-pinned to name all four values). An operator editing the field can be served by a lazily-read doc; runtime never needs it.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py:292 (four values pinned in skills/lifecycle/references/implement.md routing block, not in this file); cortex/adr/0004 + cortex/adr/0008 (value semantics)
### Carve-outs (picker fires regardless of branch-mode)L40–46 · 196 tok · 784 wtok · v2LAZY_REFVERIFIED
rationale
Both carve-outs (dirty tree, live interactive PID) are structurally enforced by the branch-mode preflight (tests a2/a3 in tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py) and the suppression condition is already duplicated in the parity-tested skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:19. Prose here is a third copy of code-enforced behavior, resident on every config read.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md:19 (same suppression condition, parity-tested by tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py); tests/test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py (a2 dirty_tree, a3 live_interactive_worktree_session — pins code, not this file)
### Normalization rulesL47–54 · 230 tok · 920 wtok · v1DELETEVERIFIED
rationale
Pure narration of parser mechanics (case-sensitivity, whitespace strip, yaml.safe_load duplicate-key last-wins, commented->null, invalid->fall-through) that cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py enforces and tests/test_lifecycle_config.py exercises case-by-case (cases 5/6/7 cite these exact behaviors). Textbook deterministic-behavior-in-prose; the CLI verb already exists so no consumer — model or human — needs this to act correctly, and typos degrade safely to the picker with a named stderr warning.
Pins: tests/test_lifecycle_config.py:11-13,119-172 (each rule tested against lifecycle_config.py — code pin, not a pin on this file's prose)
### Edge casesL55–57 · 71 tok · 284 wtok · v1DELETEVERIFIED
rationale
Single edge note about sandbox-preflight sys.exit(2) on the worktree-interactive short-circuit — a path ADR-0008 records as 'never-fired power-user opt-in — no repo sets branch-mode: worktree-interactive in practice', and this repo sets prompt. Vestigial documentation of code-enforced halt behavior.
Pins: cortex/adr/0008-picker-selection-authorizes-enterworktree.md (records the path as never-fired in practice)

## Functional Requirements / ### Session OrchestrationL11–30 · 862 tok · 259 wtok · v7COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 166 pin hits
rationale
Core contracts (forward-only phases, atomic writes, branch persistence, artifact-commit placement, dual morning-report paths) are high-value and non-derivable. But lines 26-28 (~2.4KB of the 3.4KB) narrate PR-flip mechanics, --dry-run test-fixture internals (byte-identical snapshot vs tests/fixtures/dry_run_reference.txt, 'full-line equality, not substring' — recoverable from the named test itself), and cancel/runner.pid verification details that are restated in this same file's Dependencies bullet (L168) and in docs/overnight-operations.md:218. Contract-level statements would halve these three bullets.
Pins: docs/overnight-operations.md:212 line-anchored cite of pipeline.md:28 (runner.sh retirement) — must be updated by any line-shifting edit; tests/test_runner_pr_gating.py exists independently and enforces the dry-run behavior named in L27
### Feature Execution and Failure HandlingL31–46 · 727 tok · 218 wtok · v8COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 171 pin hits
rationale

But L42 (~1.17KB, single bullet) narrates compute_dependency_batches implementation grammar (task_id integer+suffix, '### Task Na' heading parseability, parse_error naming) — the requirement is 'declared intra-feature ordering is honored; unparseable ordering fails the feature loudly, naming the offending id', ~3x shorter; the grammar lives in and is tested by cortex_command code.
Pins: No doc-reader pins found; anchor terms recoverable_branch/blocker_failed appear in cortex_command/overnight/{state,outcome_router,report}.py and their tests as implementation, not as readers of this doc

## Touch points (optional)L32–35 · 113 tok · 68 wtok · v8COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Sole-permitted-location and omit-when-unknown are load-bearing contract, but the heading suffix '(optional)' is a copy-hazard: prescriptive_prose.py SECTION_HEADING_RE (^## ...\s*$) does not match '## Touch points (optional)', so a ticket copying the heading verbatim gets its citations misattributed to Edges and flagged. Test pin uses a non-$-anchored regex, so moving '(optional)' into prose is safe; the sole-permitted-location sentence also restates the preamble exemption.
Pins: tests/test_decompose_rules.py:211-213 (regex ^## Touch points, non-$-anchored); cortex_command/lint/prescriptive_prose.py:50,71 (PERMITTED_SECTIONS + heading regex that '(optional)' suffix fails); tests/fixtures/backlog_author/valid_five_section.md:25 (fixture uses bare heading)
skills/pr/SKILL.md conditional · ×1 · 1,132 tok · 338 wtok · median v7 · 9 sections
frontmatterL1–11 · 135 tok · 40 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
name+description are the L1 routing surface, budget-pinned at 237B by the ratchet test. inputs/outputs/preconditions have no tooling consumer found, and preconditions duplicate Workflow steps 4-5, but the block is small and schema-conventional.
Pins: tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py:65 ("pr": 237 description budget)
# Pull Request (title + intro)L12–16 · 18 tok · 5 wtok · v2DELETE
rationale
One-sentence restatement of the frontmatter description; pure filler. Keep the H1 line only.
## Workflow (steps 1-7: preflight + push + template detection)L17–30 · 199 tok · 60 wtok · v3COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 42 pin hits
rationale
Step-by-step How for mechanics a frontier model executes correctly from What+Why alone (get branch, detect base, check dirty/ahead, push, look for .github templates); steps 4-5 also duplicate the frontmatter preconditions. The genuinely load-bearing bits — stop-and-route-to-/cortex-core:commit on dirty tree, both template casings/dir forms — survive a 3-4 line What+Why rewrite. Not proposing OFFLOAD_CLI given the harvested-category kill; COMPRESS is the safe form.
## Workflow (step 8: template-filling guidance)L31–36 · 354 tok · 106 wtok · v7LAZY_REFPROVISIONAL · 7 pin hits
rationale
Highest-value prose in the file ({PR_NUMBER} deferred-resolution gotcha, strip-commented-optional-blocks rule, meaningful-testing-steps criterion) but it fires ONLY when a PR template exists — and cortex-command has none (.github/ contains only workflows/), so on the primary load path (lifecycle Complete Step 3 in this repo) all 354 tokens are dead payload. Move to skills/pr/references/template-filling.md, Read only when step-7 detection finds a template; resolve the path in the SKILL.md body per the CLAUDE.md ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} principle.
## Workflow (steps 9-11 + no-conversational-output)L37–49 · 127 tok · 38 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The two-Bash-call --body-file pattern (no $()) is evidence-adjacent — commit skill line 52 documents the sandbox temp-file failure mode — and the no-conversational-text contract shapes output; keep both. But step 10's printf/gh-create command block is repeated verbatim as the standalone '## PR Body Format' section (s6), and steps 9/11 ('use default format below', 'Output the PR URL') are pointer/obvious narration.
## Default PR Body FormatL50–63 · 107 tok · 32 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Project-specific output contract (Summary/Changes shape) plus the why-not-what framing that genuinely shapes PR descriptions; not derivable from code.
## PR TitleL64–70 · 38 tok · 11 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Repo title contract (70 chars, imperative, no period, why-not-what) mirroring the commit-message convention; compact already.
## PR Body FormatL71–84 · 91 tok · 27 wtok · v1MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Verbatim internal duplicate of Workflow step 10 (same printf temp-file + gh pr create --body-file commands, same no-$() rule) in the same file; delete this section or step 10's copy, keeping exactly one. No test/hook pins the heading or commands (the tests/test_runner_pr_gating.py body-file hit is the overnight runner's own gh invocation, unrelated).
## ConstraintsL85–92 · 63 tok · 19 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Behavioral contracts protecting user affordances (no --draft/--reviewer/--label without explicit request, no --force ever) plus the repo's no-AI-attribution policy; compact and non-derivable.
cortex/requirements/remote-access.md conditional · ×1 · 859 tok · 258 wtok · median v5 · 10 sections
Title + Last-gathered + Parent doc linkL1–6 · 29 tok · 9 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
Standard area-doc preamble: staleness stamp and parent link. Tiny; conforms to the v2 template shape.
Pins: cortex/requirements/project.md:86 Conditional Loading trigger pins the file path
## OverviewL7–10 · 105 tok · 32 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 20 pin hits
rationale
The capability-level framing ('tmux is the current tool, requirement is tool-agnostic') is a genuine project decision but is stated at 105 tokens and then restated almost verbatim in Open Questions (s10). Compressible to ~2 lines with the s10 duplicate folded in.
## Functional Requirements (bare heading)L11–12 · 7 tok · 2 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
Schema heading; matches the v2 REQUIRED_SECTIONS set that tests/test_requirements_skill_e2e.py enforces on requirements-write output, so keeping it avoids drift on re-gather.
Pins: tests/test_requirements_skill_e2e.py:62-67 REQUIRED_SECTIONS (convention pin on heading names, not on this file directly)
### Session PersistenceL13–25 · 227 tok · 68 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
The acceptance-criteria list is the real capability contract (enumerable, name/ID-addressable, identity preserved across detach/reattach) and shapes any tool-replacement feature — keep it. The Inputs/Outputs lines are template filler derivable from the Description, and two criteria restate the Description sentence; ~40% trim is safe.
### Remote Session ReattachmentL26–36 · 167 tok · 50 wtok · v6COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · no pins found
rationale
Tailscale+mosh stack choice, no-port-forwarding constraint, and should-have priority are project-specific and non-derivable — keep. Inputs/Outputs filler and the 'mosh survives IP changes / roaming' criterion (that is just what mosh is) are derivable by a frontier model; ~35% trim safe.
## Non-Functional RequirementsL37–42 · 102 tok · 31 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 3 pin hits
rationale
'Silent failures acceptable for personal use' is a real recorded decision — keep. 'Timeout: Session reattachment depends on network latency' is vacuous filler; the Platform/Ghostty line is duplicated by Architectural Constraints (s7) and Dependencies (s8), so one canonical statement suffices.
## Architectural ConstraintsL43–46 · 37 tok · 11 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Single bullet that restates the s6 Platform line and s8 Ghostty dependency (macOS terminal with persistent containers, currently Ghostty). Keep the schema heading, fold the content into one canonical statement.
Pins: tests/test_requirements_skill_e2e.py:62-67 REQUIRED_SECTIONS (heading name only)
## DependenciesL47–52 · 72 tok · 22 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
The tmux/Ghostty/Tailscale/mosh inventory is the doc's most load-bearing non-derivable content — keep. The 'Local notifications: terminal-notifier / Ghostty click-to-activate' line duplicates observability.md Dependencies ('Notifications (macOS): terminal-notifier ... Ghostty terminal') and is vestigial in this area after commit 373ca304 removed cortex-notify-remote; delete that one line.
## Edge CasesL53–57 · 64 tok · 19 wtok · v2DELETE
rationale
Both edge cases reduce to 'dependency not installed -> that leg fails, other leg unaffected, suggest install' — fully derivable by a frontier model from the Dependencies list. Keep the schema heading with '- None noteworthy' or a one-liner.
Pins: tests/test_requirements_skill_e2e.py:62-67 REQUIRED_SECTIONS (heading name only)
## Open QuestionsL58–61 · 52 tok · 16 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Restates the Overview's (s2) tool-agnostic framing near-verbatim ('tmux skill under review; requirements describe the capability regardless of tool'). One statement should own this; also stale — last gathered 2026-04-03 with no update to the 'under review' status since.
cortex_command/overnight/prompts/plan-synthesizer.md rare · ×1 · 2,176 tok · 219 wtok · median v7 · 16 sections
# Plan Synthesizer (plan_synthesizer_v1)L1–2 · 11 tok · 1 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Title carries the version id; negligible cost.
## IdentityL3–8 · 121 tok · 12 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
Fresh-by-construction judge framing genuinely shapes trust in the verdict, but the do-not-delegate/no-Task-tool lines are restated verbatim in the Constraints section (line 118); state once.
## Role and ContextL9–19 · 190 tok · 19 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
The blinded-labels contract (never see generator IDs; treat variants as anonymous) is a real anti-sway design element and high value; the numbered read-score-verdict-emit task list is procedural narration a frontier model derives from the rubric and output sections.
## Untrusted Variant DataL20–25 · 196 tok · 20 wtok · v8COMPRESS
rationale
Prompt-injection defense over adversarially-influencable variant files is a genuine security gate; keep it, but the closing sentence cross-referencing the /cortex-core:research convention is meta-commentary with zero runtime effect on the judge, and the four-example redirect enumeration can be one clause.
## Anti-Sway Protections (intro)L26–29 · 26 tok · 3 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
Two-line framing for the numbered protections; negligible cost.
### 1. Avoid any position biasesL30–33 · 99 tok · 10 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Core MT-Bench-derived bias instruction; the phrase 'Avoid any position biases' is a verbatim test pin.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py::test_prompt_fragment_contains_swap_and_bias_instructions (verbatim 'Avoid any position biases')
### 2. Run the comparison twice with variant order swappedL34–39 · 141 tok · 14 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The swap-and-require-agreement protocol is the calibration mechanism both dispatchers derive position_swap_check_result from (confidence high/medium implies swap agreed); verbatim test pin plus consumer contract.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py::test_prompt_fragment_contains_swap_and_bias_instructions (verbatim 'Run the comparison twice with variant order swapped'); skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:69,104 (swap instruction restated in user prompt; position_swap_check_result derived from confidence); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:300,347
### 3. Per-criterion scoring before prose rationaleL40–43 · 107 tok · 11 wtok · v6MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Score-before-narrative anchoring is a real judge-quality lever, but it is stated three times in this file: here, in the s15 closing positional-order paragraph (line 112), and as a Constraints bullet (line 120, near-verbatim). One statement suffices for a frontier model.
### 4. Ignore variant order and lengthL44–47 · 81 tok · 8 wtok · v2MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Self-declared '(re-state: ...)' duplicate of section 1's position/length bias rules plus 'Re-read this rule if you notice yourself...' defensive boilerplate; pure weak-model guarding under the stated trust bar. The test pin lives in s6, not here.
### 5. When uncertain, assign low confidenceL48–51 · 85 tok · 9 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Anti-overconfidence calibration with the project-specific consequence (low routes to defer-to-morning / user-pick fallback); genuinely shapes verdict quality. Its duplicate in the Constraints bullet (line 121) is the delete target, not this.
Pins: skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:81 (low-confidence fallback routing); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:355 (PLAN_SYNTHESIS_DEFERRED on low)
### 6. Tie verdict (`C`)L52–55 · 69 tok · 7 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
C-pairs-with-low is a non-derivable consumer contract: both dispatchers treat C at high/medium confidence as a malformed envelope, and the tie test asserts the (C, low) pairing citing anti-sway rule 6.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py:154-155 (asserts verdict/confidence == ('C','low') 'per anti-sway rule 6'); skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:79 (C at high/medium treated as malformed); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:331
### 7. If a variant did not produce any variant contentL56–59 · 70 tok · 7 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Project-specific edge contract (empty variant file: score 1 across criteria, exclude from verdict) that a model would otherwise resolve unpredictably; upstream plan-gen failures are a real occurrence the orchestrator survivor-count logic anticipates.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:296 (single-surviving-variant skip path anticipates variant failures)
## Scoring RubricL60–71 · 221 tok · 22 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
The five criteria definitions are the decision criteria that shape the judgment (What+Why, not How); the 'names are illustrative, emit whatever keys fit' note matches the consumers validating per_criterion only as a dict, so the flexibility clause is load-bearing.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:317 (per_criterion validated only as isinstance dict); cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py:294-295 (reads internal_consistency keys from canned envelopes only)
## Output: JSON Envelope (schema + worked example)L72–103 · 284 tok · 28 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The delimiter placement, LAST-occurrence semantics, and worked JSON example are the format contract two parsers depend on; a malformed envelope silently downgrades to the deferral path, so the example is cheap format insurance.
Pins: cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_plan_synthesizer.py::_extract_envelope (LAST-occurrence regex); skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:73-75 (extraction + schema validation); cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:306-327
## Output: JSON Envelope — Field-by-fieldL104–113 · 306 tok · 31 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 301 pin hits
rationale
Largest section, and mostly re-describes the five fields already fully visible in the worked example directly above; the only non-derivable content is the verdict-must-be-letter-token rule (fold into s14) — the C/low pairing duplicates s11, the positional-order closing paragraph duplicates s8, and 'anchors the v2 schema sweep' is inert meta.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:313-320 (consumers validate field names/enums, not this prose); skills/lifecycle/references/competing-plans.md:74; no verbatim test pin on the Field-by-field text itself
## ConstraintsL114–121 · 169 tok · 17 wtok · v3MERGE_DEDUP
rationale
Five of six bullets restate rules already given: one-envelope/LAST-occurrence (s14), no Task tool (s2), don't skip swap (s7), score-first (s8), low-confidence-safe (s10); only 'Do not modify any file. You are read-only.' is new here (mirrored by orchestrator-round.md:300 'The synthesizer is read-only') and should be kept, relocated into Identity.
Pins: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:300 (read-only expectation)

## InvocationL24–27 · 61 tok · 6 wtok · v1DELETE
rationale
Pure restatement of the frontmatter inputs block (add takes title, archive takes item) and of the per-subcommand sections below. Zero information a frontier model doesn't already have twice. No pins found.
## Frontmatter SchemaL28–31 · 43 tok · 4 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale

## Filename ConventionL32–37 · 95 tok · 10 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale

Pins: skills/discovery/references/decompose.md:144-146 (inlined copy of the ID-scan convention, cited in load profile)
## Subcommands (no-arg menu)L38–49 · 122 tok · 12 wtok · v4COMPRESS
rationale
The no-arg AskUserQuestion affordance is worth one sentence; the 7-bullet menu with one-line glosses duplicates the ### subcommand headings immediately below, from which a frontier model can compose the menu itself.
### addL50–56 · 94 tok · 9 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable verb contract (cortex-create-backlog-item flags, type/priority defaults, --parent) plus the open-for-review user affordance. Invocation line is contract-lint checked.
Pins: cortex_command/lint/contract.py:422 (skills/**/*.md scanned; E101/E103 pin required flags on cortex-* invocations)
### newL57–65 · 125 tok · 12 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
The /backlog-author delegation and --body handoff are load-bearing routing; the trailing paragraph (body already authored, not opened unlike add) restates what the two steps already imply and can fold into the intro line.
Pins: cortex_command/lint/contract.py:422 (invocation line contract-checked)
### listL66–73 · 70 tok · 7 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
The missing-index regeneration arm has observed-failure provenance (commit c3b9a293 'Make backlog SKILL prose work on a missing index') and 'local cache, not version-controlled' is a non-derivable fact that prevents a wrong commit.
Pins: git c3b9a293 (missing-index arm provenance)
### archiveL74–87 · 131 tok · 13 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale
High-value core: 'no file move' corrects the archive/-directory inference from the intro, cascade/auto-close side-effects are non-derivable, and the warn-before-closing line is a user affordance. Compressible edges: two near-identical command examples collapse to one with a {status} placeholder, and the stale 'using update_item.py' internal-name mention can drop.
Pins: cortex_command/lint/contract.py:422 (invocation lines contract-checked)
### pickL88–104 · 348 tok · 35 wtok · v5COMPRESSPROVISIONAL · 305 pin hits
rationale
Keeps: error-JSON handling + reindex suggestion, the first-non-empty-group selection policy (a real decision, not derivable), and the step-7 second AskUserQuestion with /cortex-core:lifecycle routing (the frontmatter-declared output). Cuts: steps 3-6 enumerate 0/1/2-4/5+ presentation cases with label/description formatting — pure How a frontier model derives from 'present the set via AskUserQuestion, top 4 by priority, note omissions' — and the step-2 ordering parenthetical re-narrates ordering cortex-backlog-ready already applies to its JSON (cortex_command/backlog/ready.py:62,141). ~40-50% compressible; presentation branching dates to the initial commit with no failure provenance.
Pins: cortex_command/lint/contract.py:422 (cortex-backlog-ready invocation contract-checked); tests/test_backlog_ready_render.py (pins the verb's JSON wire format, NOT this prose)
### readyL105–111 · 138 tok · 14 wtok · v5MERGE_DEDUP
rationale

Pins: cortex_command/lint/contract.py:422 (invocation contract-checked); no pins on 'Backlog is clear' or render format in tests/, hooks/, cortex_command/
### reindexL112–117 · 39 tok · 4 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Minimal already: verb name plus a report expectation naming both index artifacts. Nothing to cut worth the edit.
skills/interview/references/loop.md rare · ×1 · 1,224 tok · 122 wtok · median v7 · 8 sections
# Interview Loop (preamble)L1–6 · 138 tok · 14 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale
Carries two non-derivable contracts: caller-agnostic scope (answer disposition belongs to the invoker) and the canonical-source designation for the one-at-a-time cadence rule that specify.md and requirements-gather cite. But 'Read this and follow it' restates SKILL.md:14's own instruction, and the scope sentence runs long; halves cleanly.
Pins: skills/interview/SKILL.md:14 (reads file in full, calls it single source for cadence); skills/refine/references/specify.md:42 (cites file as canonical cadence rule); skills/requirements-gather/SKILL.md:33 (cites file as canonical cadence rule)
## Decision rulesL7–8 · 5 tok · 0 wtok · v5KEEP
rationale
Bare structural heading; zero cost.
### Ask one at a timeL9–14 · 154 tok · 15 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale
This is the canonical rule two other surfaces point at, so the What (one question, answer gates the next, no fixed list) must survive verbatim in meaning. The Why paragraph (adaptive interviews beat front-loaded batches) is fully derivable by a frontier model and can shrink to one clause.
Pins: skills/refine/references/specify.md:42 (names 'canonical rule' at this path); skills/requirements-gather/SKILL.md:33 (names 'canonical one-at-a-time cadence rule' at this path)
### Keep the grilling conversational — not batched AskUserQuestionL15–20 · 203 tok · 20 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale
Project-specific tool-routing constraint a frontier model could plausibly get wrong (harness nudges toward AskUserQuestion), and the parenthetical carve-out (callers may still use it for discrete decision points) is load-bearing. The Why paragraph largely re-derives s3's answer-gates-next argument — the dedup with s3's Why is where the compression lives.
### Recommend before askingL21–26 · 167 tok · 17 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
The taste-question suppression rule is genuine non-obvious What+Why (anchoring contaminates elicited preference); the base recommend-then-confirm pattern is closer to derivable. requirements-gather/SKILL.md:27 carries its own richer site-specific variant (Recommended answer: line mechanics) — deliberate variant, not a dedup target per the R1 kill reason (site-specific arms are load-bearing). Both Why sentences compress to clauses.
Pins: skills/requirements-gather/SKILL.md:27 (same heading, independent richer variant — overlap, not a pointer)
### Let the codebase trump the interviewL27–32 · 176 tok · 18 wtok · v6COMPRESS
rationale
Shapes real behavior (models do ask users disk-recoverable facts), and the confirm-a-finding pattern is a useful concrete form; but the Why paragraph (people's time is scarce, source beats recall) is fully derivable. requirements-gather has its own 'Codebase trumps interview' variant — same non-dedup reasoning as s5.
Pins: skills/requirements-gather/SKILL.md ('Codebase trumps interview' independent variant — overlap, not a pointer)
### Funnel from broad to narrowL33–38 · 123 tok · 12 wtok · v4COMPRESS
rationale
Textbook funnel-interviewing technique a frontier model applies unprompted in a grilling context; both the What and the Why are derivable. One sentence suffices if kept at all; no pins found.
### Stop at saturationL39–45 · 259 tok · 26 wtok · v7COMPRESS
rationale
The stop-signal choice (saturation, explicitly not template coverage) is a project decision, and the early-exit-honored-immediately plus soft-cap 'keep going or wrap up?' check protect a user affordance — those survive. The Why paragraph re-argues both halves at length and is the largest single compressible block in the file.
