Under the 30 weighted-cost candidate threshold, so recorded but not surfaced.
Pins: skills/refine/references/clarify-critic.md:153 (verbatim duplicate of the lead sentence); tests/test_clarify_critic_alignment_integration.py:690-705 (v3 count-row fixture exercising the count fields, not the arithmetic prose)
cortex/adr/0016-configurable-backlog-backend-and-llm-as-adapter.md rare · ×1 · 583 tok · 58 wtok · median v7 · 7 sections
frontmatter (status: proposed)L1–4 · 7 tok · 1 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Schema field for ADR tooling. Note (accuracy, not tokens): status is still 'proposed' although the capability shipped in v2.29.0 and ADR-0019 treats it as released; a status bump to accepted is a correctness follow-up, not a trim.
Pins: cortex/adr/README.md ADR conventions; bin/cortex-adr-citation-audit (ADR citation tooling from #304)
# Configurable backlog backend and LLM-as-adapterL5–6 · 13 tok · 1 wtok · v8KEEP
rationale
File identity; cited by name/number from tests, ADR-0019, docs/setup.md, and two discovery references.
Pins: tests/test_decompose_index_backend_gate.py:77; tests/test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py:78; cortex/adr/0019-skill-helper-verb-backend-structural-guard.md; docs/setup.md:120; skills/discovery/references/decompose.md:194; skills/discovery/references/clarify.md:26
## ContextL7–14 · 107 tok · 11 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
Non-derivable Why: the adoption motivation and the zero-per-tracker-adapter driver that explains every consumer's routing shape. Four sentences, no fat.
Pins: skills/discovery/references/clarify.md:26 (back-points here for rationale instead of restating)
## DecisionL15–24 · 156 tok · 16 wtok · v10KEEP
rationale
The core project-specific contract: exact config path/key/default (cortex/lifecycle.config.md, backlog.backend, cortex-backlog), the 'CLIs gain no backend awareness' invariant ADR-0019 litigates against verbatim, the overnight fail-closed gate location, and fail-toward-local. Canonical source that consumer prose deliberately does NOT restate (killed idea R1 relies on this ADR staying authoritative).
Pins: cortex/adr/0019-...md:9,13,23,31,33 (quotes and reasons against this exact invariant); tests/test_decompose_index_backend_gate.py:77; tests/test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py:78; docs/setup.md:120
## Trade-off / rejected alternativesL25–33 · 131 tok · 13 wtok · v9KEEP
rationale
The 'real trade-off' record is the constitutive purpose of an ADR; the plugin-introspection rejection (wheel cannot introspect Claude Code plugins) is a non-derivable environment fact that pre-empts a plausible-looking redesign.
Pins: cortex/adr/README.md three-criteria gate (real trade-off criterion); skills/refine/references/specify.md:30 (ADR posture requires this section shape)
## ConsequencesL34–42 · 100 tok · 10 wtok · v7KEEP
rationale
The honest-best-effort external-backend caveat shapes user expectations, and 'Consumers back-point to this ADR rather than restating' is the load-bearing anti-duplication rule two tests enforce.
Pins: tests/test_decompose_index_backend_gate.py:77 and tests/test_clarify_coverage_backend_gate.py:78 (enforce the back-pointer pattern this line declares)
(Three-criteria gate parenthetical)L43–46 · 71 tok · 7 wtok · v6KEEP
rationale
Conforms to the repo-wide ADR admission convention defined in cortex/adr/README.md and required by specify.md's ADR posture; 7 weighted tokens on a rare-load file is below any worthwhile trim.
Pins: cortex/adr/README.md (defines the gate); skills/refine/references/specify.md:30 (instructs drafting against it)

Cross-file duplication

Mechanical 5-gram shingle similarity (Jaccard ≥ 0.55) across all 56 files. Honest finding: cross-file duplication is modest — the two clarify.md files and the two orchestrator-review.md files are structural siblings, not near-copies. Single-sourcing is worth doing where flagged but is not where the big tokens are.

Progressive-disclosure architecture: hot-path budget by cluster

clusterhot wtok todayverified savingsincl. provisionalproposed hot wtok
Lifecycle48,372−14,836−14,83633,536
Refine10,970−278−3,2147,756
Discovery10,634−0−3,9696,665
Critical-review12,918−292−3,7479,171
Transitive54,142−17,678−21,83032,312

Savings model per category: DELETE / MERGE_DEDUP / LAZY_REF reclaim ~90% of a section's weighted cost (LAZY_REF moves it off the hot path), OFFLOAD_CLI ~70% (a one-line verb invocation remains), COMPRESS ~40%. The single biggest structural lever the data shows: content that rides inside dispatched sub-agent prompts (dispatch multiplier > 1) — implement.md and the prompt-template references — pays its cost once per agent, so LAZY_REF and COMPRESS there beat larger raw cuts elsewhere.

Prior art honored (not re-discovered)

Killed ideas from the 2026-06-30 skill-efficiency audit (epic #340) — re-proposals were auto-flagged; open/recent tickets (#341–#345, #298–#302, #322, #326, #331, #334…) were overlap-annotated rather than re-reported:

  • R1 — dedupe the ~12 repeated backend-routing prose blocks across skills into one canonical reference — Killed as net negative (research.md RQ2): the blocks route different actions (read/create/close/index-regen/gate) with load-bearing site-specific arms; the canonical backlog-writeback.md block is write-back-specific; and standalone skills (/dev, /discovery, /refine, /morning-review) don't load backlog-writeback.md, so a pointer forces a 6.7KB Read to recover ≤1.5KB of inline prose.
  • R4-§2a — offload/trim the morning-review demo-selection prose — Killed: #072 shipped 'agent-reasoned demo selection' as an intentional model-judgment affordance (cortex/backlog/072-...md); the prose IS the feature, not waste.
  • R6 — trim/dedupe decompose.md regex/grouping repetition — Killed: decompose.md is the 2nd-largest file but sits on the rarest runtime path and its repetition is test-anchored (a test-enforced guard); the hot-path resident-token ranking demotes it below usefulness (research.md Decision Records + epic #340 out-of-scope).
  • R2 — migrate the hand-written clarify_critic/plan_comparison JSONL event sites to the cortex-lifecycle-event verb — Contested, default-dropped (research.md Open Questions): plan_comparison has two deliberately parity-visible producers (plan.md + orchestrator-round.md); migrating only one severs the visible schema parity a shared metrics consumer relies on, and the net byte win is ~1 line. Reopen only if the parity argument is shown not to apply.
  • Per-phase context isolation for the interactive lifecycle (fresh context per phase, like the overnight runner's fresh-per-task executor) — Declined at high confidence, recorded as wontfix ticket #344: the ~51K worst-case accumulation ceiling is real but heavy work is already dispatched to fresh sub-agents (only instruction prose accumulates); mandatory pauses + the Complete process-split already shed most of it; the harness has no context-shed/eviction API (context only grows), killing selective ref-shedding; full isolation is L/XL and fights interactive's human-in-the-loop single-thread property. Reopen only on a harness eviction API or evidence auto-compaction fails to bound it.
  • Verb-offload of the dev-router Step-3c triage block to a CLI verb — Killed in favor of restructure-to-lazy-ref (research.md RQ5, now ticket #343): the 3c recommendation tree is presentation/routing judgment over the child map build_epic_map.py already emits deterministically; a verb would mingle routing policy into the map generator. Do not re-propose the verb form.
  • Reordering plan.md §1b within the file instead of physically extracting it — Saves nothing: the whole file enters context on Read regardless of internal section order; the win requires physical extraction to a separately-Read-gated file (research.md Codebase Analysis) — shipped as #341.
  • Ranking trim candidates by bytes-on-disk — Inverted by the neutral cost-model: the three runtime paths share almost no files and prompt caching makes resident tokens cheap in dollars; the real cost is attention-dilution on hot paths plus clarity-harm. This elevated the near-zero-byte morning-review contradiction (#342) and demoted the large decompose.md.
  • Deterministic-procedure→CLI-verb offloads for lifecycle mechanics (as a further hunting ground) — Category already harvested by completed epic #336 and children #326/#330/#331/#332/#333 (plus #322/#325/#329 siblings); research.md explicitly starts where #336 stopped — re-proposing offloads in this category duplicates completed work.

Pin infrastructure the audit greps against:

  • tests/test_l1_surface_ratchet.py — Per-skill L1 frontmatter (description+when_to_use) byte budgets with ratchet direction, non-cluster ≤400B assertion, and a completeness gate failing any new skill without a budget row.
  • tests/test_skill_size_budget.py — 500-line SKILL.md body cap; in-file size-budget-exception comments are the only escape.
  • tests/test_skill_routing_disambiguation.py — Trigger-phrase substrings across the concatenated description+when_to_use of the ROUTING_PRESSURE_CLUSTER (dev/lifecycle/refine/research/discovery/critical-review); also the single source of cluster membership imported by the ratchet.
  • tests/test_skill_descriptions.py + tests/fixtures/skill_trigger_phrases.yaml — Trigger-phrase substring presence in every canonical SKILL.md description — trimming a description can break routing corpus rows.
  • tests/test_skill_handoff.py + tests/fixtures/skill_handoff_schema.yaml — Cross-skill handoff field-name presence in SKILL.md and references/*.md prose (e.g. refine→research hand-off field names).
  • tests/test_skill_section_citations.py — Section headings cited normatively by overnight prompts and lifecycle_config — including plan.md's '### 1b. Competing Plans (Critical Only)' stub (line 57-65); a heading rename/delete fails even if content moved correctly.
  • tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py — Two-way parity between the kept-pauses inventory (skills/lifecycle/references/kept-pauses.md) and the actual AskUserQuestion/phase-exit pause sites in lifecycle prose.
  • tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py — Every lifecycle/<slug> citation in ALL git-tracked *.md resolves (5 citation-form regexes); deleting/moving any tracked .md crashes it with FileNotFoundError until the move is committed.
  • tests/test_critical_review_reference_pins.py — Load-bearing contract literals/designators in the critical-review reference family (incl. a user-facing total-failure string) that no other static gate covers — the anti-careless-trim guard from #300.
  • tests/test_critical_review_classifier.py — Live-model (slow, --run-slow) classifier validation of critical-review V2 prompts against a baseline-stability fixture.
  • tests/test_morning_review_status_close_ordering.py — walkthrough.md source order: backlog closure appears AFTER merge and is conditioned on successful merge (the #342 ordering guard; spelling-agnostic close/ordering guard added 74f38d92).
  • tests/test_decompose_rules.py — Section-aware placement of each decompose rule's text within its expected heading block in skills/discovery/references/decompose.md — the reason R6's decompose dedup was called test-anchored.
  • tests/test_discovery_gate_brief.py / test_discovery_gate_presentation.py / test_discovery_research_sizing.py — Discovery gate brief contracts (Req 5/6/7 corpus regression from #236), Research→Decompose gate drop semantics, and complexity-scaled fan-out sizing.
  • tests/test_research_fanout_matrix.py — Invariants of the tier×criticality fan-out matrix in skills/research/references/fanout.md (prose-applied by the model, so the matrix shape is test-pinned).
  • tests/test_dispatch_template_placeholders.py — Pure string placeholder properties ({spec_path} etc.) of the three canonical sub-agent dispatch templates — was a 3rd missed {spec_path} citer in #341.
  • tests/test_refine_skill.py — Prose shape of refine SKILL.md §4 complexity-value gate: (Recommended) suffix proximity, 'recommend' trigger, rationale clue placement.
  • tests/test_refine_lifecycle_start_wiring.py / test_refine_reconcile_wiring.py / test_post_refine_commit_wired.py — Refine SKILL.md call-site wiring: emit-lifecycle-start ordering, reconcile-clarify site, and the post-refine-commit reference content+wiring.
  • tests/test_clarify_critic_alignment_integration.py — The clarify-critic parent-epic alignment flow (fixture-based, per #161).
  • implement.md pin family: test_implement_worktree_interactive_contract.py, test_lifecycle_implement_branch_mode.py, test_lifecycle_implement_md_daytime_free.py, test_lifecycle_enterworktree_callsites.py, test_lifecycle_picker_label_pins_worktree.py, test_lifecycle_step_v_ordering.py — implement.md structure: worktree-interactive dispatch surface, R3/R4 branch-mode carve-outs, absence of daytime-pipeline tokens, EnterWorktree call-site co-location with precondition probes, the §1 picker's worktree option label, and §1a step-v operation order.
  • complete.md pin family: test_complete_md_finalization_commit.py, test_lifecycle_complete_state_routing.py — The <!-- finalization-commit-step --> region of complete.md and Complete-phase routing (post-#331 targets the complete-route verb, not prose).
  • tests/test_lifecycle_invocation_grammar_parity.py — Docs-derived drift guard: every advertised /cortex-core:lifecycle <form> scraped from live SKILL.md+references bytes must parse through cortex-lifecycle-parse-args — editing invocation examples changes the test corpus.
  • tests/test_lifecycle_config_parity.py — Frontmatter byte-slice parity between the two lifecycle.config.md sources (asset vs cortex-init template, ADR-0017).
  • tests/test_load_requirements_protocol.py / test_cortex_load_requirements_parity.py — The tag-based requirements-loading protocol across the non-exempt consumer references and its verb parity (#333).
  • tests/test_dual_source_reference_parity.py + tests/test_plugin_mirror_parity.py — Byte-identity between canonical skills/ files and plugins/cortex-core mirrors (CI backstop with sentinel-mutation gate) — every skill edit must ship its regenerated mirror in the same commit.
  • .githooks/pre-commit dual-source drift hook (just setup-githooks) — Regenerates plugin mirrors via rsync on canonical-touch commits and blocks canonical/mirror drift; forces the build-plugin + canonical edit into one commit on main.
  • bin/cortex-check-skill-path (SP001/SP002) — Pre-commit lint: no raw ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} or bare *.md consult-refs inside composed subagent prompts (D1); no bare-relative Read/execute paths (D2); suppress with <!-- skill-path-lint:ignore-next -->; rationale ADR-0009.
  • bin/cortex-check-contract — Skill/doc prose mentions of cortex-* subcommands must satisfy the argparse contract (required flags via {placeholder}) — E101/E103 fire on trimmed or relocated prose invocations.
  • bin/cortex-check-parity + bin/.parity-exceptions.md — Every bin/cortex-* script must be referenced from an in-scope SKILL.md/requirements/docs/hooks/justfile/tests file — deleting the last prose mention of a verb breaks parity; NOT fenced-code aware (#156 deferred).
  • bin/cortex-check-prescriptive-prose — Positive-routing diagnostic against prescriptive-prose patterns in the corpus (R7 of the post-4.7 adaptation); gotcha from #342: create-backlog-item §N prose triggers the lint.
  • bin/cortex-check-bare-python-import (L201) — No bare-Python cortex_command imports in skills/**/*.md; console-script invocations only; <!-- bare-python-lint:ignore-next --> for illustrative prose.
  • bin/cortex-check-events-registry + tests/test_backlog_grep_targets_resolve.py — Event names in prose and backlog grep -c acceptance checks must resolve to bin/.events-registry.md or literal strings under cortex_command/ — no hallucinated event tokens.
  • bin/cortex-check-path-hardcoding — Parity gate preventing cortex-root path drift in skill/hook prose (#203).
  • tests/test_skill_callgraph.py (scripts/validate-callgraph.py) + cortex-adr-citation-audit (#304, report-only) — Callgraph: no skill programmatically invokes another carrying disable-model-invocation:true; ADR auditor: skills back-point to ADRs, flagging stale/missing ADR citations when rationale prose is trimmed.

Method

Pipeline. 4 cluster mappers traced what loads when (incl. transitive loads and dispatch-prompt embeds) → 56 per-file scorers decomposed every file into sections with full line coverage and scored value 0–10 against an anchored rubric → adversarial verifiers attempted to refute each candidate (3 lenses for big ones: pins/enforcement, git provenance, behavioral necessity) → mechanical passes (repo-wide pin grep, shingle dup-scan, inventory parity: 56/56 files scored, zero gaps).

Cost model. tokens = bytes/4 · weighted by load frequency (always 1.0 / common 0.6 / conditional 0.3 / rare 0.1) · × dispatch multiplier where content is embedded into sub-agent prompts. Trust bar per user calibration: all consumer models assumed capable — prose that only guards against weak-model misreads scores low. Test/lint pins were challengeable: a pin never auto-killed a candidate; verifiers priced pin+prose changes together.

Epistemic honesty. 96 candidates verified-survived, 5 verified-refuted, 32 provisional-clean, 132 provisional-pinned. Session token limits killed two verification waves; rather than re-spend ~3M tokens, the remaining big candidates were batch-verified per file and the tail left provisional — your execution-time critical-review gates are the safety net for those. Estimated savings are model-based (see savings model above), not measured.
